Dr K. when you use the word 'consciousness' are you referring specifically to human consciousness?. I suppose it is all a 'continuum' from the cold blooded reptiles who don't have REM sleep to us who have REM sleep and 'consciousness'. But what about other mammals do they have REM sleep as far as we know? Sometimes it seems to me philosophers and psychologists over the ages have made too big a deal of 'consciousness' in that animals have something pretty close to what could be referred to by that word. But for sure humans are a can I say it a quantum jump again, specifically human consciousness is something more.

And I imagine if animals 'dream' it is not the same as when humans dream. Like I am amazed sometimes when I 'analyse' my dreams by myself when I wake up how subtle they are, the very minute and subtle meanings that can kind of 'escape' me during waking. Like there is an 'intelligence' operating there that seems more subtle than waking in some ways. That's why Freud announced that dreams were 'the royal road to the sub-conscious' I am rambling here but I think it might help to clear up something for me if you define exactly what you mean when you use the word 'consciousness'. Are you referring to specifically human 'consciousness'?

### Josh (Paleo Osteo) — 2015-01-04T03:56:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tensegrity-13-is-live-and-ready-for-you-comments.12507/post-151558>

the definition of "consciousness" definitely needs clarity in this context.
if a dream is perceived, the true consciousness or awareness is that which is perceiving it.
consciousness cannot be on the perceived side

### Josh (Paleo Osteo) — 2015-01-04T03:59:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tensegrity-13-is-live-and-ready-for-you-comments.12507/post-151560>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=151553)
>
> maybe the dream replaces or interrupts the conscious flow and this is why it is rarely recalled?

the dream replaces the waking/thinking mind, but not the consciousness itself. interestingly we can find no evidence that the dream state is any less a reality than the waking state, because the current state of reality at any time is that which is perceived.
this is why quantum physics is mature, it understand that this is its limitation, the fact that the minute a perception is made, this becomes the current reality.
the big question is what are the qualities of that which is truly perceiving.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-01-04T04:11:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tensegrity-13-is-live-and-ready-for-you-comments.12507/post-151562>

Josh consciousness can't be explained by any or all our mental tools in my opinion. This includes mathematics; all are measuring/observation devices which disturb the ability to see the subatomic world as they really are. Consciousness is a manifestation of this world. It is emergent. Observation and measurement are tools for the "mapping" of reality. And in this light, the map is not the thing mapped, and the map is not the territory. We are perceiving constant collapse of waves.......kinda like watching on old school movie of a sequence of photo's moving forward on a wheel at a certain speed. At sleep.........that speed is altered.

### Josh (Paleo Osteo) — 2015-01-04T04:22:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tensegrity-13-is-live-and-ready-for-you-comments.12507/post-151563>

its true, because the tools for mapping have come out of consciousness, they do not merely appear within it

### patgrif@hotmail.com — 2015-01-04T04:47:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tensegrity-13-is-live-and-ready-for-you-comments.12507/post-151564>

I was thinking a little more on JK's ideas on wakefulness, sleep etc. I wonder if in childhood we have a kind of 'in-between' stage where the child's brain even when 'awake' is partly in the quantum/dream state and partly in the 'awake' state. If we go back further to intra-uterine life in what sense can we say the 'infant/fetus' is awake or asleep?

But I am more interested in a bit later and thinking about it now a child can be in a world of 'imagination' so much more easily than an adult. It can 'play' at and with vast possibilities and so unlike most adults who are in a world of 'facts and figures'. And it seems to me that is where a lot of dysfunction starts where a child is moved too quickly away from it's true nature into the 'adult/awake' world. I am speaking from my own expedience here also as a child I was rushed into an adult 'awake' world too soon. I ended up an adult who was too 'serious' and unable to 'play' very much or well.

Which bring me to the next point..............if that has happened what is the best 'therapy' for that? Or IS there a therapy is it just a matter of damage done? Also I am sure there are myriads of 'biological' consequences to this, including increased cortisol, chronic stress etc leading to a lot of different 'disease' later. I was involved in 'primal therapy' where the notion was to 'go back' and 'relive' 'remember' those emotions the theory being it was too much at the time but can be 'integrated' now. I have found this to have some effect but limited I would say at the same time I find it hard to dismiss it out of hand. But I like to think now with the help of Dr K I glimpse 'another way' or a way that may truly be of benefit.

What I mean is by feeding the body and brain with all the things that increase 'coherence' (CT, DHA, good sleep if you can do it, grounding etc etc - you know the 'list') it puts the brain sort of back in that state or the possibility of it, the state before it was damaged. And Ironically enough that seems to make it much more possible to 'relive' or 'remember' the traumas so in a sense do real and effective therapy. I got this 'flash' as I was whipping along on my bike feeling intense cold and I could 'remember' my real nature as a playful child who was in ecstasy because he was 'alive' but also the 'damage done'. It seems to me and maybe this is mostly a lot of 'hang over' from my 'primal therapy' days but still at least for me it seems important to 'recognize' the damage and this is one problem I have always had with 'meditation only' type approaches. Of course 'meditation' means different things to different people but sometimes it seems there is an attempt to subsume the actual condition of a person into some 'infinite awareness' I am sure that can be helpful to sometimes a bit more is needed IMO.

These days as best as I can I led my brain 'flow' wherever it goes, sometimes it goes into ecstasy and sometimes into something like hell. But I believe in letting it go where it will and not 'force' it in any particular direction. I think Dr K somewhere said the essence of brain function was 'randomness' and mostly our 'belief systems' are an attempt to thwart that. It seems to me most 'disease' is a manifestation of earlier events and mostly now we are dealing with the 'effects' which can be of course very complicated. But does true health mean delving back into some of those 'earlier events'. It seems to me it may well do but combined with Dr K's protocols it may well have a chance to actually 'work' which was mostly not my experience in 'primal therapy'

When I spoke to Dr K on Skype last week I mentioned this................maybe another way to put it is what is the best 'cure' for PTSD? This applies I am sure to adult victims of war but I am also bringing up childhood pain and trauma. And as bad as war is and can be I think the infant is so vulnerable and helpless that even 'small' things can have a very big effect.

PS. I find it ironic that I have/had such a 'block' about Dr K's ideas about 'quantum entanglement' it's like that state was knocked out of me too soon but now I begin to glimpse it.....................

the thinking mind hasnt been conditioned so heavily yet in childhood. this is where conventional "education" (read: kid goes to school and is made to regurgitate concepts) is to blame.
i think you are right that the more electrons, DHA etc added to the system increases the ability to filter noise and keep all possibilities open until they have been properly analyzed/evaluated or some such...i have seen jack write that the more electrons you collect the more you are able to know about the universe, because all electrons are a product of the big bang, or the beginning of what we know. i kinda liked that notion.

Children imagine a lot. they also spend more time in REM than adults. What does this imply Patrick if you are following how we entangle light protons and electrons? They are far more capable of coherent transmissions because they have more entangled particle on a per unit basis of tissue/mitochondrial masses. Entangled particles allow instantaneous communication at any distance and biology has revealed that the spark of life and consciousness can emerge from the ability of entanglement to occur. it is among the weirdest of the quantum abilities but in my humble opinion is the most important for the existence of life. I believe in the eukaryotic tree that as we rise from low to high the main evolutionary finding is the ability to entangle particles rises as we go to man..........Therefore all consciousness is then considered a gradient effect of entanglement.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-01-04T05:53:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tensegrity-13-is-live-and-ready-for-you-comments.12507/post-151575>

So with my responses above reconsider Tensegrity 12 statement: what life is? Consider all things are made up of atoms.......and they are not alive. then consider all living things are made up of water and molecules. Neither are alive..... molecules are dead. So we are made up of dead things yet we are alive? How? Entanglement becomes that linkage. There's no sense in which individual molecules can be said to exhibit the "phenomenon of life". You need a process among many, many components -- molecular components, of course, included -- of an organism in order to have something which has some of the characteristics we would want to regard as living. virus's come closest to living but they too are considered non-living. It's pretty clear that DNA on its own, proteins on their own, metabolites on their own, lipids on their own, are not alive. It's the network of system interaction that can be said to have living characteristics, however one defines those. What connects them all? Entanglement of electrons, protons, and photons.

### patgrif@hotmail.com — 2015-01-04T06:00:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tensegrity-13-is-live-and-ready-for-you-comments.12507/post-151576>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=151571)
>
> Children imagine a lot. they also spend more time in REM than adults. What does this imply Patrick if you are following how we entangle light protons and electrons? They are far more capable of coherent transmissions because they have more entangled particle on a per unit basis of tissue/mitochondrial masses. Entangled particles allow instantaneous communication at any distance and biology has revealed that the spark of life and consciousness can emerge from the ability of entanglement to occur. it is among the weirdest of the quantum abilities but in my humble opinion is the most important for the existence of life. I believe in the eukaryotic tree that as we rise from low to high the main evolutionary finding is the ability to entangle particles rises as we go to man..........Therefore all consciousness is then considered a gradient effect of entanglement.

Dr K. I have made some 'progress' in quantum world I see now and accept how the wave theory and function explains a lot. To simplify vastly sleep is more wave, wakefulness is more like particles, same could be said for children (more wavey) than adults. BUT and I am just being honest here I am very confused by the 'entanglement' thing. I watched the BBC videos all the way through and even there 'entanglement' was said to be the most 'queer' quality in an already queer world. I honestly would hope you might have another crack at 'explaining' it all in good time whenever you have the time. I just am not even at first base with it...............like if an electron is 'entangled' with another electron can it become 'dis-entangled'. when and where did the 'entanglement' begin and can one 'entanglement' rip up what is already entangled with something else. I am deliberately using 'simple' words to show I just don't understand it. And my fear is it may always be hard to understand. Also are you using this idea in some sense 'meta-metaphorically' I mean I could accept that we become 'entangled' for example with other people so that is a meta-phorical use of the word. Or is it? Do we have entangled electrons with other people? Also to me there is a danger it just can become some kind of buzz word, it can be cute for a while but later can seem a bit absurd. But I am not being argumentative here I am aware I am missing something but would like more understanding

In the BBC documentary they quote Bell the famous Irish physicist who tried to resolve all this and he said:

"Bohr was inconsistent, unclear and willfully obscure and right

Einstein was consistent, clear down to earth and wrong"

This kind of statement leaves one (me) in a funny position. It's like wacky world and reminds me of something I saw last night this guy doing magic tricks that I could not explain. But I know they are just tricks. Obviously this is not the same and if you say "**I believe in the eukaryotic tree that as we rise from low to high the main evolutionary finding is the ability to entangle particles rises as we go to man."** this is something very 'real' indeed. I mean in one way I suppose it makes sense if 'consciousness' and especially human consciousness or the human brain is the most intricate piece of machinery in the (known) universe I suppose it makes sense it would utilize the most 'exotic' qualities of the physical world...................so as 'metaphor' I could accept but would like more 'understanding'..................no rush meanwhile I will keep 'thinking' about it.........................

### Jack Kruse — 2015-01-04T06:01:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tensegrity-13-is-live-and-ready-for-you-comments.12507/post-151577>

Now I said in OSF 3 Darwin was wrong.......why ? In principle, Darwin didn't refer to any mechanisms in his theory of evolution did he? It was simply what we now regard as a fairly obvious statement, which is if there is variation and no definition, not in Darwin's books, anyway as to what the cause of that variation might be, if there is variation, then there can be selection. Variation comes from quantum processes........and that is why he could not have known and why neo-Darwinists are dead wrong. They believe genes are the key to phenotype and they are not. Genes are nano quantum computers like mitochondria are, and the environment they exist in is their measuring devices. Measurement and observation effects their expression. We use HDAC and methylation patterns to measure the environment.

Just to review as these have bounced around in my head for a day or so....

1.  Most enzymes have transition metals in them that enhance their magnetic field and serve as electron sinks to buffer and tune sensitivity to the amount of energy in the environment.
2.  Enzymes work through proton/H+ tunneling which requires particular spacing and mass.
3.  Transition metals absorb nnEMF.
4.  When nnEMF can not be dissipated to preserve entropy and mass equivalence, the enzymes change shape and or size/mass equivalence=loss of proton tunneling and enzymatic action.
5.  Just replacing enzymes in the system from a supplement/raw food/etc., might not improve function as the nnEMF issue may affect their function similarly.
6.  Higher redox potentials buffer the environment to maintain enzyme function. This is the best way to buffer and modulate environmental signals from foods, nnEMF, etc..

7.  The integrity/mass equivalence of the transition metal containing proteins such as enzymes is vital to the generation of the plasma currents that act at a distance and create/maintain "entanglement".
8.  The integrity/strength of these currents and the plasma's they flow through such as ECM is vital to their coherence and effect, including whether we are "conscious" of these from memories to physiological effects to sensing the presence of "others" near and far.

### yewwei.tan — 2015-01-04T22:07:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tensegrity-13-is-live-and-ready-for-you-comments.12507/post-151619>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=151575)
>
> So with my responses above reconsider Tensegrity 12 statement: what life is? Consider all things are made up of atoms.......and they are not alive. then consider all living things are made up of water and molecules. Neither are alive..... molecules are dead. So we are made up of dead things yet we are alive? How? Entanglement becomes that linkage. There's no sense in which individual molecules can be said to exhibit the "phenomenon of life". You need a process among many, many components -- molecular components, of course, included -- of an organism in order to have something which has some of the characteristics we would want to regard as living. virus's come closest to living but they too are considered non-living. It's pretty clear that DNA on its own, proteins on their own, metabolites on their own, lipids on their own, are not alive. It's the network of system interaction that can be said to have living characteristics, however one defines those. What connects them all? Entanglement of electrons, protons, and photons.

Article dated <13-Oct-2013> summarising the work of Don Page and William Wootters, and talking about a recent paper by Ekaterina Moreva et al -- [https://medium.com/the-physics-arxi...w-time-emerges-from-entanglement-d5d3dc850933](https://medium.com/the-physics-arxiv-blog/quantum-experiment-shows-how-time-emerges-from-entanglement-d5d3dc850933)

Link to paper: [http://arxiv.org/abs/1310.4691](http://arxiv.org/abs/1310.4691)

Entanglement is a deep and powerful link and Page and Wootters showed how it can be used to measure time. Their idea was that the way a pair of entangled particles evolve is a kind of clock that can be used to measure change.

But the results depend on how the observation is made. One way to do this is to compare the change in the entangled particles with an external clock that is entirely independent of the universe. This is equivalent to god-like observer outside the universe measuring the evolution of the particles using an external clock.

In this case, Page and Wootters showed that the particles would appear entirely unchanging—that time would not exist in this scenario.

It suggests that time is an emergent phenomenon that comes about because of the nature of entanglement. And it exists only for observers inside the universe. Any god-like observer outside sees a static, unchanging universe, just as the Wheeler-DeWitt equations predict.

Today, Ekaterina Moreva at the Istituto Nazionale di Ricerca Metrologica (INRIM) in Turin, Italy, and a few pals have performed the first experimental test of Page and Wootters’ ideas. And they confirm that time is indeed an emergent phenomenon for ‘internal’ observers but absent for external ones.

The experimental details are straightforward. The entangled photons each have a polarisation which can be changed by passing it through a birefringent plate

(_Wiki on Birefringence -- [http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Birefringence](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Birefringence)_)

In the first set up, the observer measures the polarisation of one photon, thereby becoming entangled with it. He or she then compares this with the polarisation of the second photon. The difference is a measure of time.

In the second set up, the photons again both pass through the birefringent plates which change their polarisations. However, in this case, the observer only measures the global properties of both photons by comparing them against an independent clock.

In this case, the observer cannot detect any difference between the photons without becoming entangled with one or the other. And if there is no difference, the system appears static. In other words, time does not emerge.

That’s an impressive experiment. Emergence is a popular idea in science. In particular, physicists have recently become excited about the idea that gravity is an emergent phenomenon. So it’s a relatively small step to think that time may emerge in a similar way.​

### Jack Kruse — 2015-01-04T22:16:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tensegrity-13-is-live-and-ready-for-you-comments.12507/post-151624>

**LIGHT IN THE BRAIN**

There was another critical difference in Prusiner’s experiments using colored stains that highlight the optical changes and quanta effects of light in the brain. Initially, Congo red dye studies and histo-pathologic staining were indistinguishable from the CJD and Alzheimer’s diseases Prusiner studied. **Immuno-staining with antibodies, however, told a different story.** The plaques had a very allosterically different optical specificity basis. This told the reader of Prusiner’s data that the difference between CJD and AD amyloid would not be found in its molecular formula, but in its quantum energy profiles of the bent proteins in the brain. This is what caused Prusiner himself to look more closely at the optics of animal models he developed. He noticed early on that his transgenic mice _were emitting light_ from their brains **before** prion disease manifested.

He found that the Congo red dye bound to purified prion rods and exhibited a green gold birefringence. This tells us there has been a dramatic change in the optics of light within the brain tissue. Today this science is called [optogenetics](http://www.theguardian.com/science/neurophilosophy/2014/jun/03/optogenetic-memory-switch?CMP=twt_gu). Congo red is used as a pH indicator in a lab. This means excessive Congo red staining of the brain tissue in prion diseases tells us this tissue has a massively altered water exclusion zone. Pollack’s work has shown us that water loses its exclusion zone (EZ) at high salinity and/or low pH. Heavy Congo red staining in prion disease is a marker for a poor water EZ in the brain. **_It turns out all mis-folded prion proteins show this very basic physical effect with lowered voltages on EEG’s_**.

**BOOM**

What does a green/gold double refraction mean?

First, it should mean that we can no longer break light into the component that is used to perform useful work. Ideally, I think the appropriate birefringence should be to leave a beam of light in the IR range, and a beam of light the "UV healing frequency range".

### Jack Kruse — 2015-01-04T23:36:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tensegrity-13-is-live-and-ready-for-you-comments.12507/post-151627>

**WHY RED LIGHT IN THE BRAIN IS OPTIMAL:**

I want you to remember what I told you in OSF 3 about the ‘red giant star” and how a normal brain works in parallel fashion. In OSF 3, I showed you how red light optimizes mass equivalence for energy production in a star. Red stars that are colder emit less energy in the form of light because they are more thermodynamically organized. Since they have a better organized core, they emit red photons to their surroundings. Red light does not carry as much power as blue or white light. These stars are more energy efficient thermodynamically, and their lifespans are much longer. Red giants can remain energy efficient for trillions of years because they are very stable in how they balance their mass energy equivalence.

Now consider your brain’s use of red light. Melatonin is destroyed by blue light and restored by red light. Blue eyes are more common further from the equator because they have more melanin to absorb more light. A blue iris in an eye is due to Tyndall scattering in a turbid layer in the iris. Brown and black irises have the same layer except with more melanin in it. The melanin absorbs light better to improve vision. So blue eyes evolved where light photons do not have as much power. Color has a big impact on the physics of optics.

Did you know that the water in you brain’s microtubules absorbs photons best in the lower powered red photon band?

Carefully consider the “red giant star” analogy above with respect to the prion laden brain emission of green gold light. Optically, they are polar opposites. A normal brain should be optically optimized to release red photons that are polarized down our microtubules precisely the same way a red giant does it in space to live long. This is how synapses are strengthened in the brain by long term potentiation. Blue light weakens synaptic connections.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-01-04T23:51:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tensegrity-13-is-live-and-ready-for-you-comments.12507/post-151628>

And to hammer the green blue light issue and oscillation down..............Quantum electrodynamics is able to fully explain the effective weakening of the electromagnetic charge with distance. This become very important in the human brain. The electric charge carried by an object (think DHA) is a fixed and definite quantity. When a charge is surrounded by other freely moving charges (think water), however, its effects may be modified. If an electron enters a medium composed of molecules that have positively and negatively charged ends (water is the perfect dipole), it will polarize those molecules. Polarization is a property of waves that can oscillate with more than one orientation. When electrons are captured on DHAs, they will repel the water molecules’ negative ends (oxygen) and attract their positive ends (hydrogen end), effectively screening itself in the positive charge. The result of the polarization is to reduce the electron’s charge in the brain to allow light to take control of the neural network. _In an electromagnetic wave, such as light, both the electric field and magnetic field are oscillating, but in different directions_. Both the electric and magnetic fields are perpendicular to the wave’s direction of travel. **This allows the white matter tracts, filled with microtubules and water, to act like magnetic flux tubes in the brain (Casmir effect)**. The oscillation of these fields may be in a single direction (linear polarization), or the field may rotate at the optical frequency (circular or elliptical polarization). In that case, the direction of the fields’ rotation, and thus the specified polarization, may be either clockwise or counter clockwise. This is referred to as the wave’s chirality, or handedness. **This change in prion disease is manifested in the green gold birefrigence change mentioned above.** The polarization process always involves the use of a charged object to induce electron movement or electron rearrangement.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-01-05T01:13:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tensegrity-13-is-live-and-ready-for-you-comments.12507/post-151631>

Now this is for Patrick to understand entanglement better:
Coral has no eyes, no ears, and no brain, Patrick. Coral's do something rather amazing to survive.
Their life cycles use some quantum communication. You Pat, might describe it like a mystical marine riddles. So you have to ask what is the secret behind coral’s “telepathic” communication. Every year on the same day, at the same hour, usually within the same minute, corals of the same species, although separated by massive distances will suddenly spawn in perfect synchronicity (think circum-annual cycles). The dates and times vary from year to year for reasons that only the coral knows when this will happen. Stranger still, while one species of coral spawns during one hour, another species right next to it waits for a different hour, or a different day, or a different week before spawning in synchronicity with its own species. So how do they know how to do this Pat? In the studies of coral, distance seems to have no effect; In fact, if you broke off a chunk of coral and placed it in a bucket beneath a sink in LA or Ireland, that chunk would, in most cases, spawn at the same time as other coral of the same species around the world in the oceans!!!

The answer? Entanglement. They are all connected. None of them need fancy sensory systems to pull it off. They just need to be connected in a deep subatomic way.

The synchronous spawn is essential for corals’ survival, Pat. Coral colonies must continuously expand outward to thrive. To remain healthy and strong, they must breed outside of their gene pool with neighboring colonies. This means they must communicate among and within their environment in a deeply complex fashion. Once released to the surface, the coral sperm and eggs have only about thirty minutes to fuse. Any longer, and the coral eggs and sperm will either dissipate or die off in the ocean. Researchers have found that if the spawning is just fifteen minutes out of sync, coral colonies’ chances of survival are massively reduced. It seems corals pull it off. Today, the Great Barrier Reef is dying at an astounding pace. 50% dead in 50 years since we began using non native EMF in the photic zone of the ocean. Why do I pay attention to the Great Barrier reef Pat? It is the largest living creature on planet Earth. So when it dies it is mankind's canary in the coal mine. Coral is the largest biological structure on the planet and covers some 175,000 square miles of the seafloor. Its reproduction ability is intimately tied to its ability to communicate via entanglement. If it can't entangle its electrons and protons or photons it dies...........It can communicate in a way far more sophisticated than anyone ever thought. And when you understand it.......there is a great lesson for man here in its story. I will now remind you of something more shocking Pat, coral is one of the most primitive animals on Earth, yet it still has the ability to harness the spark of life to communicate. I will remind you again, coral has no eyes, no ears, and no brain. Entanglement allows for amazing natural abilities.............so it is critical you get how primordial its ability is and how it can lend all living things a huge boost.

Now your example above my first reaction is to refuse to believe it, I only give it credence because you said it so I know there is a lot there. It seems to me Dr K is sort of saying 'quantum entanglement' is the driving force of evolution the 'mechanism' you might say that Darwin did not know about. Now if this is true (and I suspect it is!) it seems to me this needs more explanation. In my mind it has gone from almost some debatable and 'queer' property of quantum physics (already a bit of an 'iffy' discipline!) to being the central driving force or 'mechanism' of evolution. That a long span in a few days. But this is very exciting and I suspect Dr K has just dropped a 'bread crumb' on me. Trouble is I don't eat bread so maybe he might drop something else that I can eat or digest more easily. Looking forward to it! Or should I just eat the bread lol................

### SeaHorse — 2015-01-05T18:17:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tensegrity-13-is-live-and-ready-for-you-comments.12507/post-151692>

> [patgrif@hotmail.com said:](/goto/post?id=151635)
>
> Dr Kruse, I very much appreciate you give me so much time............I wonder if I am 'worth it' anyway yes that's very interesting. I 'meditated' on this issue of entanglement a bit today out walking and wading at the beach and I conclude and I am aware this is only to do with me it says nothing about entanglement in general that I have a 'block' about it. It is hard for me to believe the world is that connected and I feel very much it has to do with the poverty of my early life. Entanglement for an infant would very much involve entanglement with a loving mother and for a variety of reasons that was not happening so much. Anyway that's my own issues but it is interesting even 'ideas' like that can appeal or not appeal to people for reasons not so much to do with the ideas in themselves but for some underlying 'feeling'.............
>
> Now your example above my first reaction is to refuse to believe it, I only give it credence because you said it so I know there is a lot there. I need time on this it's like I know I am being dragged to 'truth' but I have resistance. But again thank you so much for giving me the time and I believe in the end this could end up being quite 'therapeutic' for me something that helps me out of my isolation. Yes what is happening to the coral reefs is very concerning they are vital for all kinds of other fish as a spawning ground etc. I read somewhere when they go so goes the fish.................

Patrick…what you write about your resistance is so important….this is really the process of changing belief structures and we tend to think it is a process of agreeing or disagreeing about "truth." If we are trying to understand things that in many ways are beyond human comprehension, we will never be able to retain our pragmatic positions, which require things to be obviously "working" in a mechanical, cause and effect kind of way.

The "feelings" you mention that draw somebody to certain "ideas" may well come from the state of the nervous system's health….a lack of safety, unprocessed trauma, doubt, depressions, etc. etc. will all shut down the capacity to experience connection. This connection is passed over for behaviours and beliefs that we think will take away the pain. But underneath all these "covering up" actions there is still the deep desire and need for connection….and now I understand that this isn't actually a "need"….it is just the way things are…this is where the quantum entanglement and the spiritual tradition of "oneness" come together.

In our society we are not educated to believe that this sense of oneness is possible for just anybody…it is for "enlightened" people only. Well I call this a bunch of BS now….because enlightenment comes in so many forms and patterns…Our challenge is to figure out what particular beliefs and experiences we need to discard to recover and reconnect. Whatever the trauma, our sensing self is still working at this quantum level and it is more a process of getting out of our own way that lets us experience connection. And, I think there are plenty of people who fake this….people who want the connection and know how to say the words….but haven't really experienced it.

Corals don't have this problem….not having brains, they can simply be connected. We have to work with how our brains process the world before we can arrive at that splendid state.

All quantum process are tied to evolutionary trajectory Pat

Thanks Seahorse that's a lovely message. I think this sense is something I have always been striving towards but due to my 'education' or better said 'mis-education' could not allow myself to just experience it. It's like without some under-pinning of 'understanding' I cannot just accept things. This is a 'weakness' of mine as you say the coral do not need 'understanding' but we can all only work with the tools we have got. For me 'understanding' or 'truth' helps me enormously without it I feel like I am floundering. Again a weakness of mine. But sometimes when I feel Dr K really illuminates some point it gives me such strength just BECAUSE it feels so 'true'. Truth is important after all so much of the human race spend their lives looking for it.......................

Could it be that space is a second dimension qualified into three aspects of height, width, and length? **Moreover, might it be that consciousness is a third fundamental and universal dimension of all subatomic particles where there are three aspects of it called on, off, and non-existent?** Might the amount and degree of entanglement and coherence determine the degree of consciousness that manifests in that system of cells?"
_
Can you clarify what you mean by non existent? Dead? As yet uncreated? Potential?

### patgrif@hotmail.com — 2015-01-06T17:28:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tensegrity-13-is-live-and-ready-for-you-comments.12507/post-151827>

> [Dali Dula said:](/goto/post?id=151813)
>
> Why is meditation necessary? We spend a third of our life in sleep where many critical and beneficial processes occur. Why would we need an additional process in our waking state?

I find that such an interesting question, was actually thinking about the same thing myself. I find Dr K's explanations of the benefits of sleep very helpful, after all it's 'free' and we don't have to 'work' at it it's just so beautiful and just happens all by itself no extra 'processing' or effort needed. And something we take for granted but when you think about it it is such a miracle, like reborn every day and for 'free'.

But it seems there is something more or maybe better said something more is sometimes/often? needed. Like if we are troubled sleep of course will help but will it make our troubles go away it doesn't seem so............in my humble opinion I think it goes to what I call now for simplicity sake the PTSD problem. I don't mean only soldiers and what happens to them in war (though that too for sure) but the kind of PTSD a fair number of people including myself I would say suffer from. I have spent a good part of my life thinking and even trying to so something about this problem without much results it is fair to say.

It is one of the main reasons I am interested in Dr K's work strange as that may seem. And recently I feel I get a glimpse at 'solutions' and funnily enough for me it involves CT plus a lot of the other 'protocols'. When I spoke to Dr K about this issue and was asking him about 'entanglement' he immediately said that's exactly what PTSD sufferers have (I think he mostly meant war victims of it) and a beginning of a 'fix' to the problem is to do all the things that promote entanglement in the brain and body which of course includes sleep itself, DHA, grounding, CT, you all know the 'list'. But what I want to mention is CT and what I feel it does for me. Here before I have spoken about it's 'mental effects' I find it works (not always) like a 'shrink' for my brain, I can get 'below' a lot of my resentments, blocks, etc. What I do I mean 'get below'.................I can FEEL the origins of some of these issues, I can get below them it puts me into a deeper state, a state with more understanding and insight, very often afterwards I feel a more trusting, forgiving whole person. It often involves 'going back in time' to where the origin of a lot of these things are

Some people might call this 'meditation' and I suppose it is though I am not that crazy about using that word. Meditation means different things to different people for me this is what I would call 'processing' processing the past, the 'issues' I have about many different things. Is this a 'cure' for PTSD...............I dunno it seems to me it is a good beginning towards one but these days I like to say the cold Pacific Ocean is my 'shrink' not someone with a degree in 'mental health' who charges by the hour.............

### SeaHorse — 2015-01-06T17:56:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tensegrity-13-is-live-and-ready-for-you-comments.12507/post-151830>

The capacity to entangle is the capacity to respond in movement….the way I see PTSD, trauma, etc. is a an inability to respond with appropriate movement….the signalling and messaging gets diminished or frozen or stuck on bust…..what happens in the ocean is unlocking your potential to signal properly again….the subject or specifics of the trauma are not significant…it's your ability to sense and "know" in a different way entirely which can give you relief…this is not directed by the parts of our brain which we think control the show…..my pond is your ocean and I'm speaking for myself

### Josh — 2015-01-06T18:52:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tensegrity-13-is-live-and-ready-for-you-comments.12507/post-151839>

>
> Why is meditation necessary? We spend a third of our life in sleep where many critical and beneficial processes occur. Why would we need an additional process in our waking state?

My experience is that sleep restores my metabolism and reserves/redox, and morning meditatioin makes everything coherent. Evening mediation makes the the body more coherent so that sleep is more effective. I have had periods where meditation has been "deep" enough that I lost muscle control, etc. for a while.

### Da-mo — 2015-01-06T18:53:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tensegrity-13-is-live-and-ready-for-you-comments.12507/post-151840>

So does meditation help us to consciously sense some of the quantum aspects of the primordial state of sleep by changing the state of the observer?

Here I am thinking of the meditative state in terms of non-attachment to outcomes, non-striving, detachment etc. and how it might affect our perception - precisely because we are in the act of experiencing versus determining - such that in some way we remove the effect of measurement on quantum phenomena and thus leave the possibilities (superposition) more intact.

If a thought can change your DNA, what is the effect of non-thought?

It seems that the collapsing of a wave function condenses many possibilities into probabilities and the into a perceived reality. Is non-thought then a way to take a perceived reality back into the field of possibilities . . . and then with a thought to collapse the wave function, condense it into a different reality using a different perception?

Does meditation allow us to entangle the awake consciousness more fully with the subconscious/unconscious?

"Be still, and know that I am God" Psalm 46:10

If you're wondering why I put that bible quote in there^^^ - the idea of God is that of being omnipresent (everywhere at once - superposition), omniscient (all-knowing - entanglement), omnipotent (all powerful - that which governs/controls/creates/manifests/materialises everything - quantum scale effects) and superluminal (faster than light - entanglement again). It could just as well say, "be still so you can experience wave functions, entangle and become enlightened/aware".

### Jack Kruse — 2015-01-08T00:27:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tensegrity-13-is-live-and-ready-for-you-comments.12507/post-152027>

Pat here is another example for you to mull over:
I was in the Caribbean sea not too long ago and saw this bioluminescent jelly fish when I was in a submersible.
I saw a 3 ft orb of flashing color approaching me; it hovered a few inches from my viewing area. Along the top of this orb was a cornucopia of lights, all flickering, one after the other, in perfect synchronicity. First, only blue lights flash in the deep purple water that acted as a screen, then only red; then purple; then yellow, until every color in the spectrum of color appeared. Then all the colors flash at the same time and the spectacle repeats. Hundreds of rows of little lights were evenly spaced around the orb. It appeared like a Los Angeles cityscape at night. When the lights were red, they looked like the taillights of cars on a freeway; when they were white, they looked like a grid of streetlights on Park Ave in NYC as viewed from an airplane thousands of feet above. Between these lights, there is nothing present in this orb. There was no visible flesh, no nerves, no bones or body.

I was looking at a comb jellyfish. Comb jellyfish live at depths below 3000 ft of seawater. They are members of the Ctenophora phylum, and are very common in deep waters where the sunlight can not penetrate yet they create massive amounts of fireworks. They propel themselves with an outer layer of fine hairs called cilia and can grow up to five feet in length. Like all jellyfish, the comb jellyfish has no eyes, no ears, no digestive system, no muscles. Their body is an orb composed of 98 percent water and a scant network of invisible nerves and collagen, all held together by two layers of transparent cells that are bound together by an electric charge using calcium as the cationic cement. It has no brain, and yet this animal hunts prey its own size, mates, and can move nimbly through the water. So ask yourself how the hell is this physically possible without quantum biology?

### Jack Kruse — 2015-01-08T02:54:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tensegrity-13-is-live-and-ready-for-you-comments.12507/post-152038>

In 2007, I learned about researchers who tried to find out how coral did it and when I did I realize right away humans were aquatic and they also had CRY2 for a deep circadian reason. It was also what clued me in that life began at the seafloor and that sleep was primordial and wakefulness came as DHA showed up. They discovered that coral had a gene called CRY2 that allowed it to distinguish between subtle changes in light. Many plants and animals, including humans, share the CRY2 gene, which is associated with monitoring levels of light as well as sensing **_subtle shifts in magnetic fields_**. Yes, this clued me into to magnetism too, Pat. In humans, CRY2 proteins help set circadian rhythms of sleep and is also related to the development of depression, eating disorders, and mood disorders. Corals were using this gene as the tiniest and most primitive of eyes one could imagine using quantum processes for its function. Water, light and magnetism are at play in CRY2. Surprise. So what does CRY2 do? This particular gene allows the coral to _sense blue light_ and **to actually work out what phase the moon is in.** This story was built in a study, in the October 22, 2007, issue of Science magazine. And using these CRY2 genes, corals were able to sense the passing of seasons and so perfectly timed their mass sync'd spawning to a particular light on a particular day. The corals weren’t telepathic at all; they were just taking cues from the sky and counting the days using blue wave light oscillations. They inherited the CRY2 gene about 240 million years ago. To this day the researchers have not been linked to the observations of humans with aquariums. Chunks of coral species totally hidden from natural light will still spawn at the same time as other corals a hundred feet deep and hundreds of miles away. Aquarium owners around the world often witness this phenomenon. This is why humans have retained their amphibious reflexes even to today. This reflex is the basis of my CT protocol..........I just have never connected the dots for you until now. I do that a lot.........I like dropping hints and seeing if you can solve the puzzle.

Tensegrity #2 talks about CLOCK, BMAL1, SIRT1 -- [http://jackkruse.com/tensegrity-2-cortisol/](http://jackkruse.com/tensegrity-2-cortisol/)

This is relevant in the sense that Crytochrome activation by blue light or magnetic fields can inhibit CLOCK and BMAL1 -- [http://genesdev.cshlp.org/content/28/18/1989](http://genesdev.cshlp.org/content/28/18/1989)

You generally want high CLOCK and BMAL1 activity during sleep to up-regulate NAMPT and thus have enough NAD+ to go around as a signalling molecule (see Jack's FB post below, as well as my forum post about NAD+ entanglement -- [http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.ph...omments-welcome-here.12294/page-3#post-149552](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/tensegrity-11-comments-welcome-here.12294/page-3#post-149552))

Bill Lagakos talked about the weird relationship between CLOCK, BMAL1, and SIRT1 -- [http://caloriesproper.com/fasting-circadian-biology-and-epigenetics/](http://caloriesproper.com/fasting-circadian-biology-and-epigenetics/)

The resveratrol link to SIRT1 is where the gut comes in. Resveratrol seems to up-regulate SIRT1 pretty well, especially at night (when CLOCK and BMAL1 should be high). I'm still waiting on Jack for the mechanics behind some of this

The implication of all of this is simply that blue light at the wrong times will cause damage, and disruptions to native magnetic fields will cause damage.

We should emphasise the importance of being able to sense native magnetic fields. I've seen some papers describing clearly that cryptochromes have the ability to sense these magnetic fields, and that these cryptochromes typically have a 24-hr periodicity. So we know that they do work, and are yoked to the magnetic field, though we don't know exactly how those senses work.

\-----

This post from Jack on Facebook should be required reading -- [https://www.facebook.com/jack.kruse/posts/10204540292686081](https://www.facebook.com/jack.kruse/posts/10204540292686081) . It is reproduced below:

Just some random thoughts tonight.

IL-6 is linked to just about every cancer on this planet. IL-6 destroys the redox potential everywhere it is found, because it raises cortisol in the stroma of organs.

When cortisol is high, collagen is destroyed, and cells get larger in volume and mass. Water flows in cell are altered, and swelling ensues worsening the volumes and mass relationship. Any time these are altered apoptosis and autophagy programs are altered. This is why water has its own specific nuclear footprint when we see cancer on an MRI. There is massive alterations to the Rayleigh Benard cycling of water inside cells. Everyone with sleep apnea has high levels of IL-6 in the brain which alters the EZ of water in the brain.

It correlates 98% of the time with DHEA levels. This is why DHEA and sleep are linked for the clinician.

We can tolerate more inflammation within our brain from a ketogenic diet because our immune system is getting more electrons. Ketogenic diets produced NAD+.

What most don't know is **NAD+ has two roles in biology– the common one as a cofactor vs the uncommon role as a signaling molecule**. NAD+ does two things: it serves as a cofactor in redox and important metabolic reactions in which case it is shuttled back and forth between the NAD+ and NADH forms but is not consumed, and number 2, **it serves as a signaling molecule in which case it is consumed as a substrate by sirtuins.**

Of the two roles, biology only talks about the redox action, when health and longevity are controlled with NAD+ is consumed by sirtuins. I am rambling on here........as I write this new blog.........Night.​

> ... She had the name of Sarvakhipi, given to her by the village people, the meaning of which is "the woman who is mad about all things"....
> Sarvakhipi: who is mad about all things. Not only in one thing is she mad but in all things she is mad -- absolutely mad.
> ... She fixed her starlike eyes upon my face and startled me with the question: "When are you coming to meet me underneath the trees?" Evidently she pitied me, who lived, according to her, imprisoned behind walls, banished away from the great meeting place of the all, where she had her dwelling.
> Just at that moment, my gardener came with his basket, and when the woman understood that the flowers in the vase on my table were going to be thrown away to make place for the fresh ones, she looked pained and said to me, "You are always engaged in reading and writing. You do not see." Then she took the discarded flowers in her palms, kissed them, and touched them with her forehead, and reverently murmured to herself, "Beloved of my heart."
> I felt that this woman, in her direct vision of the infinite personality in the heart of all things, truly represented the spirit of the East.

Some ramblings from a hundred years ago or so....no colliders, no nothing except the 3 lb carbon based system on the shoulders....

> SUTRAS 7-10: "Chitta, the spiritualized Atom, in which Ahamkara (the idea of separate existence of Self) appears, has five manifestations (aura electricities). They (the five aura electricities) constitute the causal body of Purusha. The five electricities, Pancha Tattwa, from their three attributes, Gunas — Sattwa (positive ), Rajas (neutralizing), and Tamas (negative)- — produce Jnanandriyas (organs of sense), Karmendriyas (organs of action), and Tanmatras (objects of sense). These fifteen attributes plus Mind and Intelligence constitute the seventeen "fine limbs'\* of the subtle body, the Lingasarira.” Pancha Tattwa, the Root-Causes of creation , are the causal body. This spiritualized Atom, Chitta (the Heart), being the Repulsion manifested, produces five sorts of aura electricities from its five different parts: one from the middle, two from the two extremities, and the other two from the spaces intervening between the middle and each of the extremities. **_These five sorts of electricities, being attracted under the influence of Universal Love (the Holy Ghost) toward the Real Substance, Sat, produce a magnetic field which is called the body of Sattw a Biiddhi, the Intelligence._** These five electricities being the causes of all other creations are called Pancha Tattwa, the five Root-Causes, and are considered the causal body of Purusha, the Son of God, Three Gunas, the electric attributes. The electricities, being evolved from the polarized Chitta, are also in a polarized state and are endowed with its three attributes or Gunas: Sattiva the positive, Tamos the negative, and Rajas the neutralizing.
>
> Swami Sri Yukteswar (2013-05-29). The Holy Science (Kindle Locations 277-289). . Kindle Edition.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tensegrity-13-is-live-and-ready-for-you-comments.12507/post-152598>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=152577)
>
> [http://io9.com/heres-a-photo-of-som...utm\_source=io9\_facebook&utm\_medium=socialflow](http://io9.com/heres-a-photo-of-something-that-cant-be-photographed-1678918200?utm_campaign=socialflow_io9_facebook&utm_source=io9_facebook&utm_medium=socialflow)

> First they created an experimental set-up in which a photon from a laser could take one of two paths; as long as they didn't try to detect which path the photon took, the photon went simultaneously down both paths, making two virtual photons.

Thoughts (prior warning, playing with meditation and the woo-woo here):

So if observation/measurement collapses superposition/entanglement - non-observation allows it to remain.

The coral lives in a a more primordial state than we do within a medium that is conducive to entanglement and can spawn with astounding synchronicity.

Sleep, stillness and meditation (natural and induced primordial states) allow for the suspension of things that collapse wave functions.

It would seem that the Universe breathes in via QED to create coherent domains far from equilibrium . . . . then breathes out via thermodynamics to allow those concentrations of energy to decay back towards equilibrium and drive action/reaction processes. Thus it makes sense that life should perch upon the transition between thermodynamics and QED.

In this way, following the circadian rhythm, we too energize through QED during primordial states and then determine reality during our waking time mainly via thermodynamics. But what if we can use QED to determine our reality?

> So quantum entanglement gives us a picture made of photons that could never have interacted with the object the picture represents.

Maybe dreams/visions are created in a similar way - images created within us which are entangled with remote phenomena.

But we can imagine and create these images by will/intent also. If these imaginations are entangled with remote phenomena, then maybe the reverse can happen - maybe we can affect things remotely through pure visualization in a way that classical physics is unable to explain.

My experience with meditative martial arts leads me to believe that this is indeed the case - that we can affect matter and energy at a distance both within and beyond our own bodies using a combination of meditation (tapping into the primordial state), imagination (creating the image of a reality) and will/intent (the determining force - possibly the flip side of collapsing a wave function via observation - the creation of a wave function where by the measurement/image is first created and then manifested via entanglement). Classical physics places formidable restrictions upon this ability, QED less so.

This might also be a way of looking at the placebo effect. After all, the images/thoughts/interpretations we carry have very real physical manifestations (stress, love etc.). Furthermore - our state of being affects those around us. They can be attracted, repelled, energized and de-energized purely by our presence - or even lack of presence.

Energy seems to follow attention. The novel, "The Celestine Prophecy" [https://www.dmt-nexus.me/doc/The Celestine Phrophecy .pdf](https://www.dmt-nexus.me/doc/The%20Celestine%20Phrophecy%20.pdf) has an interesting chapter on the behaviors people develop in order to steal energy from one another - methods of gaining another's attention and causing them to flow energy from one to another. These power dramas only become necessary when someone has become disconnected (disentangled) from the background environmental energy. The gaining of attention using a power drama creates a wave function where energy is transferred. It can kind of become a rock paper scissors game they are unaware they are even playing - with damaging consequences.

Through meditation we can develop skill with our attention. From that skill we can build strong focus. From that focus we can build strong intent/will. The ability to voluntarily manifest a reality is then a function of energy, focus and will. Chi follows Yi - energy follows intent (as distinct from attention). But here is the catch - and it is a really tricky one - the wave functions we create are susceptible to the same observation and measurement destruction as the photo of the non-cat in the linked experiment. Hence a more primordial state of mind, non-attachment to outcome, is necessary or the wave function collapses. It a paradox - its a juggling act - but every juggler will say that that trick is more in the throw than the catch.

A mentor taught me . . . "Be . . . do . . . . have". Most people get that sequence backwards.

Most of what we discuss here comes at our quantum health from the perspective of becoming aware of external factors and then mitigating or controlling them. The above is about how we might also approach our quantum health from the perspective of internal factors of mental/conscious/mind effects - not just as isolated people, but also as entangled ones. This is part of our environment too and our potential in this area compliments and correlates redox potential etc. It is part of our structure - and this structure determines function too.

Neurons that fire together wire together - and these neural networks can easily become our default behaviors, perceptions, reactions and realities with minimal energy required to process incoming stimuli. Every time we let these networks fire, they become stronger - ultimately to the point that all stimuli become perceived in the same way - something we should always keep in mind (pun intended )

### yewwei.tan — 2015-01-13T10:15:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tensegrity-13-is-live-and-ready-for-you-comments.12507/post-152618>

> [Josh (Paleo Osteo) said:](/goto/post?id=152616)
>
> this is starting to get towards the cosmic consciousness i have been hinting at, that was given some airtime in the webinar with erwan (albeing drug induced glimpses)

I'll spill a little of a conversation I had with Jack and Rubin in NOLA

My question was about DMT, because a certain acquaintance of Rubin was experienced in it's use, and mostly because I have once felt drawn to it when my redox is low, but all of a sudden I don't "hear the call of Mother Aya" anymore once my redox recovered.

I thought DMT to be kinda like ECT in a way -- a quick jolt to the system, but not a way to reliably add electrons over the long term.

I also though DMT to basically force the user into the natural harmonics of the earth, which seems to be congruent with some of the EEG data -- [http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/december-2014-q-a.12385/page-6#post-150594](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/december-2014-q-a.12385/page-6#post-150594)

Jack basically agreed that DMT and other psychedelics aren't a long-term solution, and hinted that their increasing popularity is a direct response to the human race becoming out of sync with nature, and needed to be forcibly re-tuned. I tend to agree with this train of thought.

He re-recommended 'The Sixth Extinction' during that conversation, which I have yet to read -- [http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The\_Sixth\_Extinction\_(book)](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Sixth_Extinction_%28book%29)

\---

Would I recommend the use of DMT and other psychedelics to people with low redox?

To be honest, if it is legal where you are, I'd say yes, but be sure to get the correct setting (eg: the right Shaman), and be sure to couple it with a good Epi-Paleo diet and minding of circadian rhythms.

...

### Jack Kruse — 2015-01-13T15:31:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tensegrity-13-is-live-and-ready-for-you-comments.12507/post-152638>

The turn in this thread concerns me..........not because of the ideas presented but because it un-focuses the point of Tensegrity 13. Focus on understanding how communication pathways are broken in cells instead of making guesses of what might patch that system of breaks. To me this is a distraction and not an illuminating point of wisdom.

Change distracts people because we overestimate what we have now, and we underestimate what we may gain by giving up what we have. Transformation deposits information and wisdom into one's life for the better. It requires focus, to avoid distraction, more truths, to suppress deceptions. It allows inspiration, to de-clutter our innermind. Today embrace new knowledge and reflect upon it, it might erase some ignorance we currently believe.

### Josh — 2015-01-13T17:57:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tensegrity-13-is-live-and-ready-for-you-comments.12507/post-152647>

Just some thoughts based to some practical observations of light affecting the oscillations and vibrations in my body. I am quite interested in the potential for of "resonant/light/water/magnetism" treatments. A neighbor has one of these:

[http://www.ilwsystems.com/](http://www.ilwsystems.com/)

The theory "sounds" good, but I suspect that they have not taken into account the subtler N=1 issues regarding circadian rhthym, circannual rhythm and the N=1 of the individual. I had one of the broader treatments that tries to do a bit of everything and noticed that by the end of the treatment I had begun to crave carbs which continued through the day suggesting a circadian mismatch from my recent post-holiday keto-adapted winter state. There was also a moderate amount of heat from the LED's which would have additional effects that I wondered about.

The manner in which devices are used with people without careful regard for the underlying physiology and its connection with the environment is a dilemma throughout the current "health" and "training" industries. There is little or no incentive to understand these issues and how they will interact with a given treatment. Even Jack's qualification of how to use red light in the proper season for the right N=1 and now possibly nm380 light required a great deal of consideration and lengthy experimentation. It is like being a pilot that has to check the wind, route and instruments for each flight....

### Josh — 2015-01-13T18:39:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tensegrity-13-is-live-and-ready-for-you-comments.12507/post-152649>

Drugs are always a short cut to things. Driving one's car in 2nd gear at 6000rpms going 40km/h feels thrilling, but destroys the car and one never really goes fat. When one goes out of sequence, it gets weird. Psychotropics probably give one an out of sequence preview of the state just after death in some distorted manner. A little like Dickens in "A Christmas Carol" with some "Star Wars" thrown in....It can be life changing if the person can digest it, and there is a slower steadier path which seems to have less down sides over time. They were always used for introductory experience to demonstrate why someone might actually want to do the work and travel through all of that and beyond.

> In 1967 when I first came to India, I brought with me a supply of LSD, hoping to find someone who might understand more about these substances than we did in the West. When I had met Maharajji, after some days the thought had crossed my mind that he would be a perfect person to ask. The next day after having that thought, I was called to him and he asked me immediately, “Do you have a question?” Of course, being before him was such a powerful experience that I had completely forgotten the question I had had in my mind the night before. So I looked stupid and said, “No, Maharajji, I have no question.” He appeared irritated and said, “Where is the medicine?” I was confused but Bhagavan Das suggested, “Maybe he means the LSD.” I asked and Maharajji nodded. The bottle of LSD was in the car and I was sent to fetch it. When I returned I emptied the vial of pills into my hand. In addition to the LSD there were a number of other pills for this and that— diarrhea, fever, a sleeping pill, and so forth. He asked about each of these. He asked if they gave powers. I didn’t understand
> at the time and thought that by “powers” perhaps he meant physical strength. I said, “No.” Later, of course, I came to understand that the word he had used, “siddhis,” means psychic powers. Then he held out his hand for the LSD. I put one pill on his palm. Each of these pills was about three hundred micrograms of very pure LSD— a solid dose for an adult. He beckoned for more, so I put a second pill in his hand— six hundred micrograms. Again he beckoned and I added yet another, making the total dosage nine hundred micrograms— certainly not a dose for beginners. Then he threw all the pills into his mouth. My reaction was one of shock mixed with the fascination of a social scientist eager to see what would happen. He allowed me to stay for an hour— and nothing happened. Nothing whatsoever. He just laughed at me. The whole thing had happened very fast and unexpectedly. When I returned to the United States in 1968 I told many people about this acid feat. But there had remained in me a gnawing doubt that perhaps he had been putting me on and had thrown the pills over his shoulder or palmed them, because I hadn’t actually seen them go into his mouth. Three years later, when I was back in India, he asked me one day, “Did you give me medicine when you were in India last time?” “Yes.”“Did I take it?” he asked. (Ah, there was my doubt made manifest!) “I think you did.” “What happened?” “Nothing.”“Oh! Jao!” and he sent me off for the evening. The next morning I was called over to the porch in front of his room, where he sat in the mornings on a tucket. He asked, “Have you got any more of that medicine?” It just so happened that I was still carrying a small supply of LSD for “just-in-case,” and this was obviously it. “Yes.”“Get it,” he said. So I did. In the bottle were five pills of three hundred micrograms each. One of the pills was broken. I placed them on my palm and held them out to him. He took the four unbroken pills. Then, one by one, very obviously and very deliberately, he placed each one in his mouth and swallowed it— another unspoken thought of mine now answered. As soon as he had swallowed the last one, he asked, “Can I take water?” “Yes.”“Hot or cold?” “It doesn’t matter.” He started yelling for water and drank a cup when it was brought. Then he asked, “How long will it take to act?” “Anywhere from twenty minutes to an hour.” He called for an older man, a longtime devotee who had a watch, and Maharajji held the man’s wrist, often pulling it up to him to peer at the watch. Then he asked, “Will it make me crazy?” That seemed so bizarre to me that I could only go along with what seemed to be a gag. So I said, “Probably.” And then we waited. After some time he pulled the blanket over his face, and when he came out after a moment his eyes were rolling and his mouth was ajar and he looked totally mad. I got upset. What was happening? Had I misjudged his powers? After all, he was an old man (though how old I had no idea), and I had let him take twelve hundred micrograms. Maybe last time he had thrown them away and then he read my mind and was trying to prove to me that he could do it, not realizing how strong the “medicine” really was. Guilt and anxiety poured through me. But when I looked at him again he was perfectly normal and looking at the watch. At the end of an hour it was obvious that nothing had happened. His reactions had been a total put-on. And then he asked, “Have you got anything stronger?” I didn’t. Then he said, “These medicines were used in Kulu Valley long ago. But yogis have lost that knowledge. They were used with fasting. Nobody knows now. To take them with no effect, your mind must be firmly fixed on God. Others would be afraid to take. Many saints would not take this.” And he left it at that. (R.D.)
>
>
> Dass, Ram (2014-09-15). Miracle of Love: Stories about Neem Karoli Baba (Kindle Locations 3769-3777). Love Serve Remember Foundation. Kindle Edition.

Neem Karoli lived in a blanket in the foothills of the Himalaya for most of his life. One of his homes was on the way to the ashram I lived in and when I sat in his 6x8 foot bedroom 30 years after his death, there was still a great deal of tangible "vibration".

I know someone who volunteered for and LSD experiment during medical school in the late 50's. They were kept tripping for 7 days continuously in a room. They remember the experience as if it were a "trip". It did not seriously alter their life trajectory.

These kinds of observations and experiences inspire me to continue to grapple with this material. I take Einstein's saying regarding the more information that we have the more we can imagine. For me, this leads in both linear and non-linear ways to expanded experience and "perspectives" shall we say....

### Da-mo — 2015-01-13T19:01:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tensegrity-13-is-live-and-ready-for-you-comments.12507/post-152651>

So where were we . . .

> **Beta waves** are those associated with day to day wakefulness.
>
> These waves are the highest in frequency and lowest in amplitude, and also more **desynchronous** than other waves. That is, the waves are not very consistent in their pattern.
>
> This desynchrony makes sense given that day to day mental activity consists of many cognitive,
> sensory, and motor activities and experiences, and, thus, when awake, we are mentally
> desynchronous as well. During periods of relaxation, while still awake, our brain waves become
> slower, increase in amplitude and become more **synchronous**. These types of waves are called **alpha waves**. For example, such brain waves are often associated with states of relaxation and peacefulness during meditation and biofeedback. As we will see in future lessons, recent evidence indicates that activities that promote alpha wave activity, appear to have positive health benefits.
> [http://web.mst.edu/~psyworld/general/sleepstages/sleepstages.pdf](http://web.mst.edu/~psyworld/general/sleepstages/sleepstages.pdf)

So far so good

> As the sleeper moves to stage 2 sleep theta wave activity continues, interspersed with two unusual wave phenomena. These phenomena, which occur periodically
> very minute or so, and are defining characteristics of stage 2 sleep, are termed **sleep spindles**
> and **K complexes** (figure 2).

So are these the jolts that kickstart and then add energy to the desired vibration?

But then . .

> During a normal nights sleep a sleeper passes from the theta waves of stage 1 and 2, to the **delta waves** of stage 3 and 4. Delta waves are the slowest and highest amplitude brain waves.

The deepest sleep stages where you would think that the maximum vibrational effects would occur . . . except that oscillation cam come into play by . . .

> Interestingly, delta sleep is when sleep walking and sleep talking is most likely to occur.

So it seems that in our deepest sleep - we are not paralysed and oscillation can possibly be produced. Yet this is not considered the dreaming stage of sleep.

Also, what is considered the dreaming stage (REM) is the least deepest stage of sleep apart from intial onset. Yet we are paralysed at that time except for our eyeballs.

And since we cycle through the stages several times during the night - does this imply that each stage has a different role to play regarding entanglement or are the different stages doing other things like protein folding or replacement? - I.e does the body needs to be quiet for one purpose and the brain quiet for another?

How would being overtired or undertired affect the stages and cycles?

>
> A friend tried one of these that served in place of an alarm clock. He said he wouldn't do it again because he felt as if he'd been forcibly ripped from the dream he was having.

I have tried with some success for part of the night. When my father was a very dedicated Tibetan practitioner, he said he could go through about half of the night conscious. After a few years, he said he stopped as it did not seem to lead anywhere for him. Many advanced practitioners say that they are always "awake/conscious" even when asleep. I wonder if we can actually figure out the mechanics of all of this and induce it with alterations in magnetic fields? I wonder if the meditative "technologies" can ever be replaced with contemporary electro-magnetic devices? Rumor has it that this has been tried before.

### Jonathin — 2015-01-13T21:53:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tensegrity-13-is-live-and-ready-for-you-comments.12507/post-152676>

About 20 yrs ago for some unknown reason I was able to be consciously asleep for part of the night. After laying down in bed on my back, a wave of energy would slowly disembody my extremities and then my whole body. It seemed I could 'will' it simply by my concentrating on the 'feeling'. Sometimes I would go unconscious briefly during this transition but then become conscious when my body disappeared. I could not think but simply experienced the feeling. It was an energy state that I felt very intensely - kind of like a pulling on all the skin. Sometimes it felt 'tiring' after awhile and like Josh mentioned I too was a bit unsure what the point was. The next morning I would wake up suddenly and it was morning with no sense of time transition. I don't remember being tired or unrested.

At that period in my life, this experience was a curiosity. I am not sure what changed but after a few weeks or months, this sleep mode was gone.
