# Thread: Doc uses ZRT labs, calling for my list soon, don't know what to request
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/doc-uses-zrt-labs-calling-for-my-list-soon-dont-know-what-to-request.12527/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 17
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Hope2Learn — 2015-01-05T19:52:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/doc-uses-zrt-labs-calling-for-my-list-soon-dont-know-what-to-request.12527/post-151705>

Hi all.. I have a very detailed journal over in the journals forum which gives specifics. [http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/nowhere-to-go-but-up.12501/](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/nowhere-to-go-but-up.12501/) But here's the bottom line. My doc referred me to a compounding pharmacy whose pharmacist does patient consults regarding BHRT. Then the pharmacist faxes the recommendations back to my doc, who will write prescriptions for me based on the consult.

The pharmacist is calling me back soon to get a list of labs I want run. He uses ZRT labs. [https://store.zrtlab.com/](https://store.zrtlab.com/)

I'm 42, female, reason for going is for BHRT purposes. No sex drive, poor sleep (already reduced EMF greatly, blue blockers /blue light issues being worked on as we speak, only one cell tower within four miles of my home, etc). Thyroid tests always at the bottom-end of normal.

(TSH Was last done in August.. they always seem to pick and choose one or another of them to do. Haven't had any of the others done in years probably. I have an appointment to see my GP on Thursday regarding sleep issues and long-standing fatigue that he is already treating me for, and I hope he will be able to get my thyroid and vitamin D and maybe even adrenals tested/covered by insurance so that I only need ZRT to do my sex hormones and 'oddball' stuff you all may recommend).

_\[August TSH labs:
TSH- normal range: 0.47-4.68 uIU/mL - Mine:2.03 \]
\[they did a lipid profile the same day - here are those results in case they're helpful - normal range is in parentheses and my result is afterwards:

Cholesterol(<200 mg/dL)150
HDL Cholesterol(35-60 mg/dL)41
Triglycerides(<150 mg/dL)110
LDL Cholesterol(<100 md/dl)87
Cholesterol / Hdl Ratio3.7
LDL / HDL Ratio2.1 \]_

I really don't know what I need them to order for me, and normally they don't even ORDER any labs, she said the pharmacist usually knows by what a woman's symptoms are, what she will need. I didn't feel comfortable with that though, I want info to start with.. so time is of the essence. No matter how fast I try to read (and I have been reading almost non-stop here for over a week now), no matter if I have podcasts running all day while everyone is at work and school (as I have been for several days), I still need just a bit of help here because he's calling back faster than I can find the information I need.

And the tests are expensive so I can only order what I truly need. I will not skimp, but it must be justified thoroughly.

Can ANYONE help me based on what ZRT labs offers? They have a lot of different test to choose from and I just don't know where to start.

[https://store.zrtlab.com/](https://store.zrtlab.com/)

Thank you so much.. I will gladly pay it forward in any way I can, for now I already started by ordering a second copy of Jack's book for my oldest son, who is away from home right now.

Thanks again, and for more info please check out my journal:
[http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/nowhere-to-go-but-up.12501/](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/nowhere-to-go-but-up.12501/)

### caroline — 2015-01-06T00:39:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/doc-uses-zrt-labs-calling-for-my-list-soon-dont-know-what-to-request.12527/post-151759>

an ASI - saliva test ...cortisol x 4 \[diurnal\] ......great start for now and not too expensive - my opinion btw!
this one tests hormones and cortisol.....
cortisol is important to tell us how the Rolex in our brain is working

you may want to test melatonin ........

### Josh (Paleo Osteo) — 2015-01-06T07:32:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/doc-uses-zrt-labs-calling-for-my-list-soon-dont-know-what-to-request.12527/post-151796>

hey
i use zrt labs for hormones/thyroid and ASI
these are the only real ones i know from them, they are saliva and blood spot tests.

i would do the blood spot for the hormones including thyroid $350 here in Aus
also the salivary ASI (maybe $140 here in Aus)

you can also tick various additions on the requisition form

### Josh (Paleo Osteo) — 2015-01-06T07:33:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/doc-uses-zrt-labs-calling-for-my-list-soon-dont-know-what-to-request.12527/post-151797>

multi test kit might be the go

### Hope2Learn — 2015-01-06T16:48:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/doc-uses-zrt-labs-calling-for-my-list-soon-dont-know-what-to-request.12527/post-151821>

ASI is adrenal stress index? They don't call it that there but pretty sure thats' what it is.

I'm having real trouble figuring this out because I am going to see my GP on Thursday (day after tomorrow) to see if he can order any tests for me and get my insurance to cover them. I don't know which ones he will order and which ones he won't. So I don't know what to order myself!

I find it likely that he can and will order all the thyroid stuff for me because he has before. I can't imagine him saying no to vitamin d testing either. But I don't know what adrenal testing consists of. Can he just send me for fasting bloodwork the next morning and have them do that or is it different? All my doctors tests will be bloodwork.

I don't have any cash on hand so anything I order will have to go on credit and I really hate to do that. I feel like I Should forgo testing and just trust the pharmacist to throw stuff at me based on a telephone interview of my symptoms, because I can maybe afford that. But maybe not.. I guess whatever they prescribe I will have to pay cash for...? What does BHRT cost anyway?

so many questions and nobody to ask... no sisters, mom passed away ten years ago, no friends to speak of... \*sigh\*

I know I want my hormones tested and DHEA and DHA. And cortisol maybe? But I don't understand cortisol... everyone says it's important to have enough in the morning but then say it's bad because it's related to stress. So am I not supposed to stress in the morning? Because i thought you WANTED cortisol in the morning so I do my bills and stuff at that point and spend all morning in a crappy stressed out mood.. but see, you all say you want cortisol in the morning right?

Likewise I read articles about people who are like me, over 40 and with glaucoma, and it says they have BAD eyes because they DON"T let in blue light. So.. I thought I was supposed to never have blue light, ever. No lightbulbs, use blueblockers, etc. But now I read that my eyes a re bad because they DON"T LET blue light in!

I am so confused. I only ever found this website because I googled BHRT and landed in a monster thread all about it, so I signed up. Now I find that Jack has a whole thing about eating special, special EMF limits, all kinds of things I never head of before and never had a concern about.

Whic his GREAT and I'm incorporating much of it into my own life because it reads sensibly and seems legit... but it has led to overwhelming information overload and has made it so that now I don't know where to start. It had seemed so simple at first.. I'm constantly a bitch, so I need more hormones. Well okay that's counter intuitive, I've always lamented that I need LESS hormones because I'm so impossible. But now I think I need more because... no sex drive.

Oh I am just so confused and sad this morning at the fact that when the pharmacist calls all I can really tell him is please don't order any test for me because I don't know what I need yet. Some days I feel like I'm making progress and other days it hits me that I have changed nothing ,except I now spend MORE time on the couch on the computer, because I'm reading here and trying to learn. But I haven't. I still don't know what to do about BHRT or testing. On one hand my days have been less boring and more fulfilling because I found some things that sound interesting to try, which should keep me busy buut on the other hand they're more depressing and frustrating because I'm not getting anywhere, and now I'm more confused than ever. I thought I'd just ask to be put on the pill, because that should be completely balanced hormones right?

But now I don't know anymore after reading here.

Most people sign up here, I bet, because they wanted to lose weight and had tried everything and then decided to try Jack's book/diet/Rxes. So I can understand why everyone is so quick to advise me to do all the stuff they do. But my goals aren't the same.... so I feel like I don't know how to pick and choose what to do now. My n=1 is different, which I initially thought would be a great fit here but now I just.. well.. I'm used to being the smart one. Not so much here. I don't like that feeling.

hopefully the day will improve from here. This isn't like me. bleh.

### Hope2Learn — 2015-01-06T18:44:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/doc-uses-zrt-labs-calling-for-my-list-soon-dont-know-what-to-request.12527/post-151837>

Oh boy. I need to apologize... I'm so sorry for being in such a mood this morning. I surely shouldn't have had such an outburst. I'm sorry.

I have since turned off the laptop (wifi connection + blue light from wake up to bed time has to be the worst thing for me yet I just seem to have completely not even thought about that until now.. OMG can't see the forest for the trees... now that some trees are systematically being knocked down and hauled out of the way, the forest suddenly is so much more obvious. \*sigh\* live and learn.. know better, then do better... I have many ethernet cables and will dig one out once I'm done posting this).. (and holy run-on sentence, Batman!).. and showered, which always makes me feel a million times better, and realized the apology was due, then asked myself - why was I so positive yesterday and not today?

Yesterday morning I had steak for breakfast with raw greens and cucumber.

This morning I threw two slices of cinnamon raisin bread from the (discount) grocery store into the toaster and then used "light margarine spread" on them, then added on a banana.

...

Ya think this is it?

(could also be that my almost-blind, diabetic, 13 year old rat terrier, Daisy, had a warm and fragrant "accident" on the floor right in front of me this morning quite deliberately because I told her "not right now" when she wanted to go out. Touche!)

### Hope2Learn — 2015-01-06T19:40:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/doc-uses-zrt-labs-calling-for-my-list-soon-dont-know-what-to-request.12527/post-151848>

>
> i would do the blood spot for the hormones including thyroid $350 here in Aus
> also the salivary ASI (maybe $140 here in Aus)
>
> you can also tick various additions on the requisition form

Thank you, Josh! Your reply is much appreciated!

I have a question for you, or anyone who might have the answer and be lurking ... if my C-Reactive Protein was normal when it was tested in October, would it be necessary to get the high-sensitivity version done at this time? Are they at all related?

I had my HS--CRP done once before by my cardiologist, but it was a couple of years ago and I don't know that the results would still be relevant today.

Thanks!

### Hope2Learn — 2015-01-06T19:49:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/doc-uses-zrt-labs-calling-for-my-list-soon-dont-know-what-to-request.12527/post-151849>

> [Josh (Paleo Osteo) said:](/goto/post?id=151797)
>
> multi test kit might be the go

Hm yeah I'm looking and it says the Female profile II includes the hormone blood spot and the thyroid tests for $349.
(hormone blood spot includes Estradiol, Progesterone, Testosterone, SHBG, DHEA-S, and Cortisol)

Then it says the ASI includes DHEA-S and diurnal cortisol for $185. But since DHEA-S is already included in the Female Profile II test, couldn't I just get the diurnal cortisol test that is $135 all by itself?

Also.. the hormones above are good but do I need to test the other two estrogens? What about Pregnenalone? hm...)

Then again the Comprehensive Female Profile II includes:
E2, Pg, T, DHEA-S, SHBG, TSH, fT3, fT4, TPO; and saliva: Diurnal 4x Cortisol for $380 which, if I am not mistaken, is the exact same combo as listed above..?

Am I missing something, or is this precisely why you said perhaps a combo test is best?

EDIT: For $20 more I can get the combo kit that has all of the above PLUS FSH and LH. My childbearing years are behind me, technically (I"ve had a tubal), but is it worth the extra $20 to get them done, do you (anyone) think?

### Hope2Learn — 2015-01-06T20:03:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/doc-uses-zrt-labs-calling-for-my-list-soon-dont-know-what-to-request.12527/post-151855>

Ah, question for anyone reading - 23andme.com.... they gave me an alert that if they mail me the test to NY state, I must have the samples collected and sent back from elsewhere. This is no problem because my ladybits doctor is in Pennsylvania as is the compounding pharmacy and pharmacist who is doing my BHRT counseling/recommendations/testing. It seems like they could do that stuff there and I could mail it back from a post office down there easily enough. But is that good enough (plus the signed waiver they said they'd include)?

Anyone have any experience with this? Thanks as always!

### Josh (Paleo Osteo) — 2015-01-06T22:38:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/doc-uses-zrt-labs-calling-for-my-list-soon-dont-know-what-to-request.12527/post-151875>

CRP is not sensitive at all
hsCRP is much better

do the full ($380) if it covers everything

the tests are a pain becaus ethey cost money and dont necessarily cross over
for me the better the information = the better the intervention

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

here is a picture of a tanked ASI (adrenal fatigue)
you want it high in the AM as it is what wakes you up
it should follow like a bell curve skewed to the left (high am, tapering away to hardly anything at night)
if it is high late day or night time it indicates cortisol is raised due to a stressor etc
cortisol is magical when it works properly, we NEED it
it saves our lives in times of stress, but it gives your doc a good idea of where the stressors are when it is spiked everywhere or flat like the one above which is one of my patients

>
> [View attachment 1255](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/1255/)
> here is a picture of a tanked ASI (adrenal fatigue)
> you want it high in the AM as it is what wakes you up
> it should follow like a bell curve skewed to the left (high am, tapering away to hardly anything at night)
> if it is high late day or night time it indicates cortisol is raised due to a stressor etc
> cortisol is magical when it works properly, we NEED it
> it saves our lives in times of stress, but it gives your doc a good idea of where the stressors are when it is spiked everywhere or flat like the one above which is one of my patients

This is very helpful, thank you so much for your time and input!

I spend all day long stressing out dreadfully. Most of the time I'm in fight or flight mode. I can barely stand still when I see my husband approaching me to give me a kiss.. he knows to be quick and remove himself from my space immediately. I just can't handle... anything. I believe my cortisol will be off the charts. I am hopeful to get things under control fairly soon.

# Thread: Affect of a ketogenic diet on performance
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/affect-of-a-ketogenic-diet-on-performance.12528/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### justin potier — 2015-01-05T21:57:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/affect-of-a-ketogenic-diet-on-performance.12528/post-151728>

When talking about the performance gains associated with a ketogenic style diet such as the vo2 max gain after 2 to 3 years of utilizing the diet how much of a gain is this? considering the average vo2 max for an athletic person is 50-60 would the increase be minor such as a two point increase or would it be a large increase such as 20-30 points bringing someone to the level of cyclings greats like armstrong and indurain who both had vo2 max's of above 80? I heard your vo2 max is largely genetic which is what made me think about this. and if this has not been studied, scientifically speaking what type of gain should be seen?

### Shijin13 — 2015-01-05T22:49:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/affect-of-a-ketogenic-diet-on-performance.12528/post-151736>

Welcome @[justin potier](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/12037/) Start with this thread here: Barry nicely sums up the answer across this epic thread [http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/fasted-state-training-adaptations.297/](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/fasted-state-training-adaptations.297/)

### sjoshua — 2015-01-05T22:53:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/affect-of-a-ketogenic-diet-on-performance.12528/post-151738>

Hey Justin, welcome to the site!

As you explore, you'll probably notice that the mindset around here is that genetics are more of a potential blueprint, per se, where true expression of said blueprint is determined by the entire lifestyle and environment of the individual - i.e. epi-genetics. This makes it possible for situations like two identical twins to each be unique - because it is impossible for them to experience the same exact environmental conditions, even if they were always within inches of each other for their entire lifetimes. But, this also makes predicting others' results quite difficult, since the environmental conditions are quite improbably or likely impossible to replicate...

What VO2Max 'YOU' are able to achieve is so unique that the only real way to determine is to try it for yourself. I realize you are likely looking for some ballpark gauge, but I'd suggest that this would be almost useless considering it may be worlds below your individual potential, or worlds above - and setting the bar in the wrong place or making choices about health based on a specific metric instead of looking at the entire picture may be detrimental in the long term... Sure, elite athletes may choose to focus on bettering times, lifts, or other max outputs, but it is worth noting that elite athletes perform at elite levels for short periods of time, NOT their entire lives. They may choose to sacrifice none, little, some, or all of their long-term optimal potential for the short term elite athletic endeavor, but unless you are pushing into elite athletics I'd suggest a better pursuit for us regular folk would be to focus on overall optimal health.

Also, ketogenic diets are not all equal. Drinking canola oil would be ketogenic by definition, as would drinking pastured pork fat.... but which would you want to do? The difference in how our body reacts to these two situations is the important part. Here is a blog that talks directly to this: [http://jackkruse.com/organization-structural-failure-8-ketosis-appears-fail/](http://jackkruse.com/organization-structural-failure-8-ketosis-appears-fail/) - but that may not be a good starting point for you to dive in. This may be a better stepping stone to find information you desire: [http://jackkruse.com/getting-started-guide/](http://jackkruse.com/getting-started-guide/)

Hope this helps

### Josh — 2015-01-06T02:52:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wim-hof-new-cold-record.12529/post-151776>

What would limit the maintenance of heart and neural function in the mitochondria during hypothermia? Would all of the ubiquinol be used up as an antioxidant leaving a deficit for starting up the electron transport chain? Would an ubiquinol deficit result in decreased uncoupling and IR production? Or would the generation of reactive Nitrogen and Oxygen species with the failure to recycle Glutathione result in a sudden increase in apoptosis as the ubiquinol antioxodant stores become exhausted?

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

Figure 2: Modulation of apoptosis by hypothermia. After a serious insult the cell can trigger apoptosis, a highly regulated cell death mechanism.Intrinsic Pathway. Hypothermia increases ATP stores and slows ion channels then maintaining the integrity of the membranes. Hypothermia applied together or immediately after injury decreases the production of ROS. These events limit the rupture of the outer mitochondrial membrane and the release of proapoptotic molecules like cytochrome c into the cytosol. The hypothermia-induced increase in nitric oxide also avoids cytochrome c release and it is even reported that early NO production can exert a negative feedback regulation of iNOS \[[34](http://www.hindawi.com/journals/omcl/2013/957054/#B91)\]. Moreover, iNOS transcription activated by NFB was diminished after hypothermia \[[35](http://www.hindawi.com/journals/omcl/2013/957054/#B88)\]. Since catalase is absent in mitochondria, maintaining GSH redox cycle is critical to avoid H2O2 accumulation. There is abundant evidence that hypothermia keeps GSH pool. Extrinsic Pathway. It was found that hypothermia decreases the affinity of the death ligands-death receptors, with the consequent inhibition of the initiator caspases like caspase-8 or the NFB-family molecules.

[http://www.hindawi.com/journals/omcl/2013/957054/](http://www.hindawi.com/journals/omcl/2013/957054/)

# Thread: Suddenly averse to wearing gloves and a scarf!

### Hansen Kenimer — 2015-01-06T00:42:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/suddenly-averse-to-wearing-gloves-and-a-scarf.12530/post-151761>

My hands can be freezing cold to the touch (as they were this morning in the 10 degree F weather) but I cannot bring myself to wear gloves. I have also become resistant to wearing my winter scarf. My body has clearly learned that the cold receptors need to feel the cold in order to start the whole uncoupling process to produce the heat necessary for the conditions. It's some weird psychological compulsion, that's for sure. I find myself increasingly underdressing for the weather, and find it so stimulating and energizing!

Cool... ha

You are merely adjusting your relativity; under vs. over dressing is just individual perspective

What if... we'd never been versed in the concepts of cold vs. hot, but instead accepted either of them as merely 'sensation' on a wider scale of compatibility with our fleshpods...

>
> What if... we'd never been versed in the concepts of cold vs. hot, but instead accepted either of them as merely 'sensation' on a wider scale of compatibility with our fleshpods...

Well, I suddenly love the sensation! It was 23 degrees F this morning and I drove to work with my vents open. Breathing the cold air, freezing my hands off....it was GREAT. I'm high as a kite right now.

The interesting thing is I didn't flip this switch until I started sprinting again two days ago. Even with CT. It's like I burned off some metabolic sludge (I mean this literally, I feel it in my brain) and now I'm craving cold. And fat. My appetite for butter is insatiable. Along with ice cold water. And iced coffee.

I'm very curious to see what happens with HIIT aaaaaand CT aaaaand keto epi-paleo. I'm feeling absolutely superhuman right now, and I think I've just now opened the can of worms....or, the barrel of monkeys. ;-)

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=151763)
>
> Oyster artichoke soup........
>

### Jack Kruse — 2015-01-06T01:16:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-i-had-extra-oysters-tonight-and-made.12531/post-151767>

Melt butter ( one stick) in a large saucepan over medium heat. Saute onion and celery (one pint diced) until tender I add one two two diced carrots too, about 10 minutes. I add lots of garlic but it is your choice how much you like. It is diced. I'd say I put in 1/4 cup in this one. Pour in the seafood or chicken stock (cup to pint), and reduce heat to low. Cook for about 15 minutes. Add the artichokes (2 cups diced) and oysters (two dozen), and simmer for 10 more minutes. Take half out and add one cup of heavy cream and put in vitamix and blend. I add Lemon juice of one lemon and its zest, tumeric, black pepper, garlic, oregano, rosemary, sage, more diced onion and celery with some reserved artichoke chunks with some shrooms. Finally, stir in the heavy cream (another cup if you need to make a few more servings) Salt to taste. To thicken you can use egg yolk or parmeasean. I used the cheese tonight cause I had some left over from NYE party. Add back the Vitamix stuff to the blend you just made so the soup has some chunks in it. Cook until heated through, but do not boil, about 15 minutes o cook down. Serve immediately. If you want to make it a sauce add more cheese or yolks to thicken and pout over anything cause it rocks.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-01-06T02:19:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-i-had-extra-oysters-tonight-and-made.12531/post-151771>

Yes - it's the key to if you are able to hold water, use water and dispense water correctly. If it's tanked - you'll never be able to be hydrated  Check Blood & Urine osmolarity. Dr Shoemaker, MD

"Reduced MSH can decrease pituitary production of antidiuretic hormone (ADH) which can lead to thirst, frequent urination, neurally-mediated hypotension (NMH), low blood volume, and electric shocks from static electricity. While sex hormone production is often down-regulated the pituitary may upregulate the production of cortisol and ACTH in the early stages of illness, then drop to abnormally low, or low-normal ranges later."

"The blocked leptin receptor will no longer create the initiation of steps that lead to production of alpha melanocyte stimulating hormone (MSH)."

# Thread: Hello, everyone!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-everyone.12544/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Haley — 2015-01-07T00:47:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-everyone.12544/post-151897>

Posted on the forum about a year ago but thought i'd go ahead and start fresh!

I'm 22 and been an investigator of my own health for about 6 years now. I'm not sure I consider myself a die hard paleo-er as I often question everything I read in every sphere-since I've (probably) tried it all. My journey truly started at 15 when an eating disorder came into the picture. My strive for optimal (especially on the outside) has never seemed as easy..constantly feeling as if I'm in a battle with my body despite the "cleanest", strictest diet and attempts to live a healthy "primal" life. I'm at the point where I have tried to adopt every "guru's" philosophy leaving me restricted and my body resistant. So many conflicting ideas leads me to believe there is much deeper science not yet understood in the paleo community which is why I have always been drawn to Jack's work. My biggest problem right now is just not having the educational background or time to truly understand the principals he is teaching. I am curious if there is any diet templates or a list of understandable recommendations so that I can get started. I'm afraid it may take a lifetime of reading these articles to understand one recommendation to start applying it!

So far I have found a few bits of info that I have started applying like:adding seafood (and not supplementing with omega 3), cut out all starch in winter (eliminated the 15g of squash i was eating) and letting my body get COLD!-I live in Colorado and have been restraining from heaters but haven't adopted ice baths yet

thank you all for any help!

### sjoshua — 2015-01-07T01:41:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-everyone.12544/post-151905>

Hello to you as well! Glad you made it back

You're right, while you were gone there were probably a few dozen new blog posts issued, and then webinars and Q&A's to boot. There is a ton of information available, but you don't have to understand it all to skim the ideas from it and take action where you can. Consider, it may take a theoretical 'lifetime' to get through it all, sure, but is it worth trying if it were to allow you to add an optimal lifetime onto your lifetime, or at minimum allow your lifetime to be filling with wellness instead of pain?

I think the sound bite from Jack that goes something like: 'Learn to be cold, dark and boring, and learn how to sleep well' is the easiest way to sum it all up simply.

My interpretation of it:
cold = take literally, which it sounds like you are already beginning to embrace
dark = follow the sun's pattern for determining if/when to be exposed to light - be it a bulb, a TV, or your phone's screen, etc.
boring = limit stressors, be it emotional/mental (i.e., work out your problems amicably) or physical (like, non-native EMF)
sleep well = take literally, but implementing the first three effectively and this will likely happen on its own

> [Haley said:](/goto/post?id=151897)
>
> eliminated the 15g of squash i was eating

Geez, 15 grams huh? Those are some tiny squashes... Don't overdo it

### Haley — 2015-01-07T02:04:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-everyone.12544/post-151918>

sjoshua-thanks for your reply!

15g of squash....my bad...meant 15g of carbs from winter squash.....that is "winter" squash-still not acceptable? lol

Do you think living in elevation and a cold environment might be enough without the use of ice baths?
I think I'm doing well with my sleep patterns-usually going to sleep between 9-10pm and waking 6-8am. However, since I have cut out that last bit of starchy carbs I am awake a lot during the night-or sort of in half asleep mode. Which ALWAYS happens when I cut carbs. As well as increased cravings and hunger. Never can seem to find a solution for this. Already very weary about ketosis as I have done it in the past with poor long term results and found much information on it not being a good idea. I saw a post with Jack's comments on the issue and again, could not understand what he was saying (not his fault, but my inability).

I do hope this is a happy Q&A environment as I do have many questions already!

thanks!

### Inger — 2015-01-07T12:40:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-everyone.12544/post-151946>

Welcome  from another one with a eating disordered past....... You will heal for sure! This is the right place  Just never give up, and step by step it will get better. Remember healing is not linear.... it will be ups and downs.... but the ups will be bigger  I am not fully healed yet either... maybe we never are... we just improve all the way
The good thing is... Jacks protocols are very easy  and one can start right away.. and they does not cost a cent

I think it is a great move to do the ketosis with lost of seafood! Preferably raw

### nicld — 2015-01-07T16:37:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-everyone.12544/post-151958>

Welcome Haley,

If you are willing to share, what part of Colorado are you from? I have been there a couple of times and LOVED it out there, just not in Denver (too much EMF).

### Hansen Kenimer — 2015-01-07T17:20:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-everyone.12544/post-151968>

Welcome, Haley! I grew up in Montrose over on the western slope of CO.

### Haley — 2015-01-07T17:30:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-everyone.12544/post-151971>

Hi all!

I live in Durango, CO (also in the western slope!)..have the blessing of living in a A-frame out in the mountains..

### Martin — 2015-01-07T17:37:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-everyone.12544/post-151972>

Yes, there's a ton to read on the blogs, but you can get a flying start with Doc's book: The Epi-Paleo Rx. Gobs of data and easily understood in many ways. I'm constantly referencing it for many things, not to mention inspiring one to go cold, eat oysters, and get the hell away from technology whenever you can.

Listen to as many webinars as you can. So many complicated concepts are brought together when Doc goes all "freestyle" and links how things are connected. With those general concepts in mind, you can dive into the blogs for more.

This is the place, the Rosetta Stone, the Cutting Edge, for what you're looking for.

NSH

### Jack Kruse — 2015-01-07T18:39:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-everyone.12544/post-151987>

welcome..............you got good advice already........soon on the blog you'll be learning how light magnetism and water all link together in biology because of nitrogen. And this will certainly help your journey. Most people here have been with me from the beginning and they have gone from macro to micro with me.........soon we will be leaving the quantum level and going back to macro things on our world..........I have a very specific blog about your neck of the woods that ties to eating disorders and mental illnesses that you'll likely be intrigued with

### Haley — 2015-01-07T22:01:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-everyone.12544/post-152007>

Thank you Martin, I would love to get my hands on the book soon.

Jack-Thank you for your input...i like the sound of that..looking forward to it! I am especially intrigued with the mechanisms of an eating disorder as I feel it puts us on a slightly different playing field and i would love to connect the dots for folks so I can be a true teacher of it one day.

# Thread: Help with my new Trifield meter? :)
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-with-my-new-trifield-meter.12546/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 29
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Hope2Learn — 2015-01-07T01:31:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-with-my-new-trifield-meter.12546/post-151903>

Hi everyone! I just received my blue blockers, Jack's book, and my Trifield meter from Amazon today and I'm trying to figure out what exactly I ought to be checking with this meter. I'm already walking around figuring out how it works, but what should I be paying attention to? Is there a particular scale of reference for what I would want my router to be at, or my microwave, or the cordless phone? How high lamps ought to be, etc? Because I get readings of some kind or another off just about everything but don't know what it means.

I saw some people referring to testing their home, in a general way. Does that mean just standing in the middle of the room with the meter on the most sensitive setting and seeing if anything registers?

Thanks!

### Lahelada — 2015-01-07T01:40:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-with-my-new-trifield-meter.12546/post-151904>

I dont have one but if I did I would.
1\. check out my sleeping place, the mattress especially if it is a spring mattress.
2\. if the readjngs are not ideal, find out if there is an optimal spot in the house.
3\. Check out the walls if you have adjoining neigbours. Do they have a router near you,a smart meter?
4\. Your electrical appliances and sockets.
5\. even your floor readings may be interesting.
6\. your Pc/lap top.

Note down all the values ,post here and someone will tell you what is high.

No adjoining neighbors - we own a home on just over 5 acres of mostly conifer woods. Only one cell tower within 4 miles (it's actually under 2 miles away).

But.. one of the walls in my daughter's bedroom... the electrical outlet makes the magnetic (0-100 range) meter jump all the way up to 100 and stay pegged there when I point it at the outlet. I'm really really upset and sick about this right now. Does this mean the electrical back there in the addition was done bad?? What do I do? I'm so worried now!

Anyone? Thanks!

### JMO — 2015-01-07T03:26:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-with-my-new-trifield-meter.12546/post-151925>

> [Hope2Learn said:](/goto/post?id=151916)
>

>
> Upstairs bathroom - 1 (10 at the light switch).
>

>

>
> My tracfone/dumbphone - 2.5 or so when not in use**, jumps up to 50+ when turned on then bounces between 50 and 100.**

I found a lot of the same things in my house. Its great to have a meter cuz now you know! My husband had fluorescent lights in the garage and I had him swap them back to regular bulb outlets. Dimmer switches are also bad. Replaced cordless phones with wired. The jacuzzi in master bath was really high even when not running. Since I don't use it, I turned off the breaker. My daughters room is above the garage so I went crazy trying to get that lowered...it still bugs me. We found a plug incorrectly wired. Played with circuit breakers to try to figure it out. The grounding plug on laptop makes a big diff too so well worth it. I have virtually everything unplugged. I used a body voltage meter too. I also found the basement was the best. I just got the TES-593 meter not too long ago and have done some readings but not finding too much with it...prob need to do a consult with Michael.

### Inger — 2015-01-07T12:11:20+0200

Hope.... that is so cool you have measured your home... look now it is really easy to make improvements     yay!!!!

### av8r — 2015-01-07T18:18:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-with-my-new-trifield-meter.12546/post-151977>

I love the Back to the Future trilogy!

I unplug my microwave too; I would get rid of the damn thing if my wife didn’t use it on occasion. I also have a pellet stove that puts out very high EMF… no standing next to it when it is on.

Dirty electricity caused by switching power supplies can back feed through house wiring too and give you readings that are hard to trace down. Small plug -in power supplies for laptop computers (or other electronic devices) can create EMF nightmares. I have an outlet in my living room that feeds from wiring under the living room floor, if a laptop is plugged into that outlet, which happens on occasion, I can read very high EMF right down the middle of my living room floor… I also found a florescence drop light plugged in, but turned off, and hung from the ceiling of my basement right under my bedroom floor, I could read medium EMF levels at the head of my bed one floor above… the drop light was not even turned on!!! needless to say, that f@@@er is gone!

The saddest for me was measuring the seat of my Harley Sportster… in the 0-100 scale it pegged the EMF meter :-( ... even though that bike pegged my fun meter, I am not taking that risk of putting my prostate in that high of an EMF field. The bikes computer is mounted right under the seat, that is were the EMF is coming from. I sold the bike last fall. I will look for another bike but this one will be a much older model before computerized fuel injection.

### Martin — 2015-01-07T20:07:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-with-my-new-trifield-meter.12546/post-151994>

Welcome to testing emf's! You are finding out good things with your meter, but I've read and heard reviews by professional EMF researchers that Tri-Field's tend to read a little high. That might ease your worry a bit (remember, it causes stress! But you knew that).

Using a "Body Voltage" meter that runs a wire out the door and plugs into the ground is preferred over the "Electrical" setting on your meter. There are many meter options in getting those more accurate readings. Goggle it to get some more info.

The Tri is not good a reading microwaves very well. The microwave range starts out really high so you will miss the weaker readings that are very important. Still, the fact that it is telling you "something" is a plus and you got a great deal on this meter at the price you paid.

Magnetic fields: You've got some issues going on in your home. First, pay close attention to your sleeping area and turn off breakers as you read the field around your bed. Ditch the clock, horrible. Can openers, dehumidifiers, filters, anything with a motor (motors have a spinning metal thingy inside it that acts like a miniature iron core in the Earth) creates high mag fields. Move them away from sleeping areas and wherever you spend a lot of time. Get those fields down as close to zero as you can, especially at night during sleep (you do sleep at night I hope - shift work will kill ya quicker!).

Flourescent lights have a transformer in them that create large mag fields. Ditch them whenever/wherever. Watch out if you're over a crawlspace or second story as fixtures, h20 heaters, furnaces, can all create fields that can come from below.

Your 100 mil/gauss on an outlet is troubling. That's REALLY high. Is something plugged into it? Miss-wiring? Get an outlet checker at hardware ($8-10?) and plug it in to see any problems. Call electrician, and turn off the breaker when your kids are around it. I have a good meter and I get "0" from most of my outlets, and it takes placing the meter's probe very, very close to a motor or fluorescent fixture to get readings that high.

Totally, unmercifully, unquestionably, ditch the microwave oven. Forever.

Congrats on testing! I know it's a bit freaky now that you've checked, but you're ahead of the game now. Environment is where it's at!

NSH

### Hope2Learn — 2015-01-07T23:25:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-with-my-new-trifield-meter.12546/post-152019>

> [Martin said:](/goto/post?id=151994)
>
> Welcome to testing emf's! You are finding out good things with your meter, but I've read and heard reviews by professional EMF researchers that Tri-Field's tend to read a little high. That might ease your worry a bit (remember, it causes stress! But you knew that).
>
> Using a "Body Voltage" meter that runs a wire out the door and plugs into the ground is preferred over the "Electrical" setting on your meter. There are many meter options in getting those more accurate readings. Goggle it to get some more info.

Hi Martin, thanks for the info! I'm replying in chunks because there's so much here. I never heard of a "Body Voltage" meter until now, but it sounds interesting. My daughter was just asking me "if the human body has a magnetic field, can you measure it with that EMF meter?" .. I told her no, I believe you must need one much more sensitive than this. Is a "body voltage meter" something that would measure this? Voltage doesn't sound like EMF to me, but I'm admittedly just new here and learning a lot of things all at once while reading Jack's book for the first time. So this help is much appreciated.

> The Tri is not good a reading microwaves very well. The microwave range starts out really high so you will miss the weaker readings that are very important. Still, the fact that it is telling you "something" is a plus and you got a great deal on this meter at the price you paid.

I'm glad that I got a good price on this - I didn't shop around because I was reasonably sure I recalled them costing more last time I looked into getting one. I got it on amazon.com and it's probably still the same price if people search for it there.

> Magnetic fields: You've got some issues going on in your home. First, pay close attention to your sleeping area and turn off breakers as you read the field around your bed. Ditch the clock, horrible. Can openers, dehumidifiers, filters, anything with a motor (motors have a spinning metal thingy inside it that acts like a miniature iron core in the Earth) creates high mag fields. Move them away from sleeping areas and wherever you spend a lot of time. Get those fields down as close to zero as you can, especially at night during sleep (you do sleep at night I hope - shift work will kill ya quicker!).

I go to bed around 9:30 each night and get up at 5:30 in the morning with rare weekend or holiday exceptions. My quality of sleep is fairly poor but nowhere nearing insomnia thank goodness.

I didn't turn off any breakers when measuring, I didnt' even think of it. We will do that next time we get a chance (I want my husband here to assist with that.)

> Flourescent lights have a transformer in them that create large mag fields. Ditch them whenever/wherever. Watch out if you're over a crawlspace or second story as fixtures, h20 heaters, furnaces, can all create fields that can come from below.
>
> Your 100 mil/gauss on an outlet is troubling. That's REALLY high. Is something plugged into it? Miss-wiring? Get an outlet checker at hardware ($8-10?) and plug it in to see any problems. Call electrician, and turn off the breaker when your kids are around it. I have a good meter and I get "0" from most of my outlets, and it takes placing the meter's probe very, very close to a motor or fluorescent fixture to get readings that high.

Actually, my daughter's electrical outlet had her tv running from it when I measured it last night. I measured it with the tv off today and it has fairly benign readings like all of the outlets and light switches (around 4-6). I then turned the tv back on and it shot right up. The head of her bed is literally as far from that outlet as it can possibly be, at hte far other side of the room, so I told he to just minimize her TV time and sit back from it. Solid advice for anyone at any time I think!.

I told her about her kindle and cellphone readings today (she's almost 12 and in middle school). Then we went and read the desktop computer, the monitor, the mouse, everything back there including her sitting in the computer chair holding the meter - it's only a 2 except directly in front of the monitor so without being told, she has situated herself back there tonight instead of in her room on one of her other devices with her tv running for no reason. This is a win/win so far for several reasons.

> Totally, unmercifully, unquestionably, ditch the microwave oven. Forever.

I agree. I was going to get a new one, then I was going to leave this one unplugged except when we use it... then today I heard myself telling my husband "I think the microwave kills the water" and he gave me the funniest look until I said WTF radiation water? (not in so many words) and he agreed, hehe.

> Congrats on testing! I know it's a bit freaky now that you've checked, but you're ahead of the game now. Environment is where it's at!
>
> NSH

Thank you! I agree, and am so glad to know what I'm up against now! Out baseline mid-house reading in each room is around 1.5 - 2 but I suspect that's due to wifi. I will test with the router turned off later.

Oh I noticed our breaker box is really high like 3 feet out from it still (15ish) and maxed out over 100 when right in front of it.

The breaker box is in the kitchen right next to the cabinets with food in them.

What does this do to food, if anything?

Thanks again for all the help!

### enyaw — 2016-04-01T02:23:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-with-my-new-trifield-meter.12546/post-191804>

> [Hope2Learn said:](/goto/post?id=151912)
>
> Downstairs bathroom (mentioned above) - 0 (which is weird because we do get wireless internet in there).
>
> **Wireless combination modem/router/includes digital phone (wall-mounted next to the kitchen table, cannot be moved) - 30 to 100+ fluctuating constantly**

You won't get an accurate reading from a WiFi router with the Trifield. The Trifield only reads freqs up to 100KHz while a WiFi router operates at either 2.4 or 5 GHz.

# Thread: Long arctic winter nights and circadian rythym
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/long-arctic-winter-nights-and-circadian-rythym.12549/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Rouanne — 2015-01-07T05:38:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/long-arctic-winter-nights-and-circadian-rythym.12549/post-151932>

Hello!

I am new here and I absolutely need some help because I am going crazy :-o I am in my 30s, I have fibromyalgia, and I have about 50 lbs to lose. I am following the leptin reset diet to a T, plus I watch my caloric intake and since I am so gorged on this diet, I only eat 2 meals a day. I am taking supplements too..but I am not progressing at all. If I had only 10 lbs to lose I would understand but I am still nearly obese. I go to bed at 10-10.30 pm at night however I live in the Arctic region and in the winter the sun sets by 3 pm. I obviously cannot go to bed that early  and cannot stay in bed till 10 am when sun comes up. I wonder if it affects my ability to lose weight? Of course I cannot lose in the summer either - when we have 24/7 daylight..
Is there a hope for me or is this perhaps a coincidence?
I am at the end of my ropes because I am not losing inches either. Cant afford to get labs done..so I am in the dark here, so to speak!
Thanks for looking and any insight!

### caroline — 2015-01-07T06:19:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/long-arctic-winter-nights-and-circadian-rythym.12549/post-151934>

Hi ...and welcome Rouanne. first thing - please put up a picture - Dr. K's forum rule.

Please start a journal in the journal section and give us some health history - Dr. kruse needs context to be able to point you in the right direction.

What about your non native EMF situation? home? work? where do you live? Do you have blue blockers?

do you drink bottled water? grounding? sunshine? C/T? How is your sleep?

you mentioned calories - the reset is not about calories. I am assuming you are female .... we \[women\] don't lose easily on the reset. It is all about resetting our circadian cycle - that is hugely important.

Also - Dr. K. believes we should get all our nutrients in their natural package ...food.
What supps are you taking?

Do you have Dr. K's book? It covers the first two years of the blog ... so easier to grasp.

Do you listen to his podcasts? they are a quick and easier way to get info.

Please tell us more....

### Inger — 2015-01-07T12:29:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/long-arctic-winter-nights-and-circadian-rythym.12549/post-151944>

Welcome!
Use candle lights... or red lamps.. I use salt lamps for light... and i use blueblockers + f.lux and darken the screen on the computer in the night......
\+ take care to be outside when it does is light... i think this is really important too...... especially take care to get sunlight/daylight directly in your eyes (no glass in between) in the mornings!

### Jack Kruse — 2015-01-07T18:33:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/long-arctic-winter-nights-and-circadian-rythym.12549/post-151986>

# Thread: Greetings from a Long-Term Lurker
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-from-a-long-term-lurker.12553/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 18
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Graeme — 2015-01-07T18:03:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-from-a-long-term-lurker.12553/post-151974>

Hello to everyone!

After several years lurking in the shadows of this blog, all the time basking in the warm glow created by Jack's astounding knowledge and the genuine warmth and excitement created by his forum and its members, and now that I have read every line of text on this site, I felt that it was about time I introduced myself.

Firstly, I would like to express my deep respect and gratitude to Jack for the incredible work he is doing here. Rarely have I felt so humbled, yet simultaneously challenged and inspired by the ideas presented and the complex connections made by one individual. Jack, you are a very special person indeed. I salute you.

Congratulations are also due to the forum and its members. This is the only forum to which I have ever felt the urge to contribute - polite and welcoming, combining practical, knowledgeable help with opportunity for sometimes mind-bending, profound discussion. Even the inevitable, occasional squabble is deftly handled by the moderators and other members - I wonder, is this the world's first meta-stable forum? It seems that the quantum entanglement of Jack and his forum members are more than a match for the frailty of the lower dimensional disorder that is the IT infrastructure of the modern world.

Regarding myself - I may post a journal at a later date, but for now - I am a 47 year old, normal(ish) English male who has lived in Spain for the last 20 years. I was fortunate enough to have had access to a good, old fashioned education. I enjoyed being a good at multiple disciplines, a generalist, but I was a physics major having rejected medicine at the time mainly due to a comment from a respected aunt that success in medicine was more about "bedside manner" rather than medical knowledge. I didn't fully understand the comment at the time, but it was enough of an offence to my scientific mind to make me think twice. I never thanked my aunt, but I would have made a lousy doctor. I eventually ended up with a "career" in finance (I know it's all a joke, but it is, what it is!), but I maintained a keen interest in all things health related especially as I approached middle age and became a father.

So, by way of William Davis and Wheat Belly, then via MDA, I arrived here in late 2011 and I have been here ever since. In my element.

I feel I should also point out that I am a Rush fan. This trivia seems to be of real statistical significance I have never come across another place with so many other Rush fans. We were always a slightly odd minority. I find it curious in the extreme that so many should be drawn to Jack's work.
Thanks to NeilBB and Shijin13 for musically reminding me of this connection recently.

I'll finish, if I may, with a minor criticism of sorts, not with Jack, but with some of the members on the forum. I often feel that the wide range of discussions on the forum, which (simplifying greatly) sometimes get bogged-down either in the practical application of the basic principles or in the highly entertaining discussions of finding the source of consciousness etc. I often think that we need to make sure we don't forget the task that Jack has set himself here. He is trying to revolutionize the world's understanding of health by teaching, using new concepts and connections which he has weaved into his quilt from his years of experience and research across many disparate fields of knowledge. This is a very rare and special thing.

I suppose what I am trying to convey is that we should always remember the fact that we are all privileged to be here. I believe we are witnessing history in the making and I look forward to contributing in some small way to this great effort in the future.

Many thanks for everything,

Graeme

### Jack Kruse — 2015-01-27T14:52:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-from-a-long-term-lurker.12553/post-153987>

You said,
I'll finish, if I may, with a minor criticism of sorts, not with Jack, but with some of the members on the forum. I often feel that the wide range of discussions on the forum, which (simplifying greatly) sometimes get bogged-down either in the practical application of the basic principles or in the highly entertaining discussions of finding the source of consciousness etc. I often think that we need to make sure we don't forget the task that Jack has set himself here. He is trying to revolutionize the world's understanding of health by teaching, using new concepts and connections which he has weaved into his quilt from his years of experience and research across many disparate fields of knowledge. This is a very rare and special thing.

I suppose what I am trying to convey is that we should always remember the fact that we are all privileged to be here. I believe we are witnessing history in the making and I look forward to contributing in some small way to this great effort in the future.

I'll say:
Leaders never cross the finish line first. They wait for others because they are patient. To follow the leader means the leader is bringing others to a destination. That destination is not a place where followers congratulates the leader, it's the place where the leader applauds those who followed and were able to cross the finish line first before themselves. It is not looks, not height, not fitness, not intelligence, not wealth, not clothes, not humor. It is patience that matters for an innovator intent on transformative change.

If you want to change things, then finish early start early. Innovators know that speed matters. If you teach to lead people, then they must always be your top priority. Visionary leadership is packaged with some degree of delusion that pushes an individual forward, but...contains the potential for his or her destruction. An innovator accepts this risk because mistakes often point to areas in which we lack self-awareness of a weakness or threat.

Personally I have found the "discussions of finding the source of consciousness" very interesting even as it can be frustrating at times also. One can have too many exotic drawings of bar magnets etc............but I think it is important not to decide to rule out such discussions. The human mind naturally goes "deep" and 'movements' that rule out thinking or talking about such things usually constrain themselves in some way that shows up later as an important omission. Nothing should be 'taboo' and the way I see it at least so much 'thinking' by ruling out 'deep' questions (even if we easily don't find answers) short change themselves. Medicine itself is I think an example of that..............Dr K last winter talked a lot about how medicine just could not consider a lot of discoveries in physics and kind of put it all to the side and started filling out the crossword in a way that seemed "good enough" at the time but is now shown not to be good enough.....................

### caroline — 2015-01-28T00:40:31+0200

I often think I can see him saying to himself ...."you finally get it!"

In my opinion - this keeps us all engaged. I have no science background what so ever ...but Jack has never made me feel excluded and that I couldn't contribute in other ways.

Jack said something awhile ago \[well - so much important stuff\] that I will never forget. It is not some big "sciency thing" He told us to pay attention to people ...everyone has a remarkable story to tell if we will only stop and really listen ...and really see them....

I live this simple lesson every single day now ..... I can smile at a complete stranger and touch them lightly on the arm and ask how they are ...and really listen to their answer - and I didn't need a degree in Chemistry!

and .... I am the one that is completely and utterly enriched....

### Jack Kruse — 2015-01-08T05:43:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/and-here-i-thought-all-this-sun-cycle-stuff-was-a-little-far-fetched-8.12554/post-152057>

it is the simple math of erg's. We are beings of light and no one appreciates it yet. The sun is several thousand times less efficient than the human body at converting mass to energy, but it lights the spark of light in us and then a cell becomes a dissipative structure for sunlight the rest of its existence. One kilogram of the sun generates only 1/5000th of a watt of power on average, whereas your mitochondria typically generates usually more than 1 watt per kilogram. Now let us consider how light and electric current relate the sun and your mitochondria. When a nerve fires an impulse, a significant amount of electricity is produced. According to Oxford University geneticist and author Frances Ashcroft, the electric field through the ion channel is the equivalent of about 100,000 volts per centimeter. If all the electricity in a person’s body could be harnessed and converted to light by DHA, the human body would be sixty thousand times brighter than a comparable mass of the sun. Sounds incredible huh? Pound for pound, you could be brighter than the brightest star in the solar system. Most people will find this hard to swallow so let us do the math to prove to you this is not hyperbole. The sun’s volume is 1.4 x 1033 cubic centimeters. Each second, each centimeter of the sun emits 2.8 ergs (an erg is a unit of energy). So, the total luminosity of a cubic centimeter of sun is 2.8 ergs per second. The human body has a volume of about 75,000 cubic centimeters. Dividing human luminosity (1.3 x 1010 ergs/ sec) by volume gives you 170,000 ergs per second per cubic centimeter. There you have it. 2.8 erg's per second for he sun and 170,000 ergs per second for your cells. Life requires massive infusions of energy to attain its spark of life, but it needs the sun to ignite it.

# Thread: So vitamin D inhibits uncoupling proteins....this makes perfect sense!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-vitamin-d-inhibits-uncoupling-proteins-this-makes-perfect-sense.12555/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Hansen Kenimer — 2015-01-07T20:52:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-vitamin-d-inhibits-uncoupling-proteins-this-makes-perfect-sense.12555/post-152002>

[http://ajpendo.physiology.org/content/296/4/E820](http://ajpendo.physiology.org/content/296/4/E820)

So it seems natural and NECESSARY for your vit D levels to drop in the winter, to enable uncoupling proteins to work their magic in the cold. BOOM!

As a side note, forgive me if this has been discussed. My search didn't bring up anything on this topic.

### AndrÃ©s — 2015-01-30T20:29:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-vitamin-d-inhibits-uncoupling-proteins-this-makes-perfect-sense.12555/post-154284>

_Non sequitur_. It doesn't matter that VDR-null mice have their furniture always on. The question is if they are better adapted to cold exposure than normal mice. Sincerely I doubt it since I suppose that I am not the only one keeping high levels of 25-OH-vitD year round (greater than 50ng/ml) at the same time that keeping CTing (well, I still do mostly [DIY-ICE-vest-2.0](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/diy-ice-vest-2-0.5263/), drive with AC full blast toward me and keep heat-off at my office) year round too. Brown fat turns on without problems as far as I can see.

I think prudent to follow [Vieth's hypothesis](http://ar.iiarjournals.org/content/29/9/3675.full) while not falsified.

# Thread: The Jigsaw
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-jigsaw.12556/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 120
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### dantothep — 2015-01-08T03:35:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-jigsaw.12556/post-152041>

My Journal

First of all I want to say thanks to Jack for having the insight to join together all these disparate silos of health & nutritional information and assemble a theory that ties it all together. Between you and the rest of the community here, and the shared experiences you have documented has allowed me to try and put together the missing pieces of the jigsaw.

I will try and keep this succinct as many on the forums have a lot of reading to do (myself included). I have read the book, all the RX’s and now starting with Brain-Gut and trying to work through chronologically.

I’m mid- thirties currently living in London UK; I am married with lovely wife and three lovely children (ages are 11, 7 &3). I work for a Technology service provider as a technical Director (IT plus Video/Audio ENG). I have a passion for many disciplines of movement and have studied various Martial Arts, but currently love Parkour, Climbing, Olympic lifting & Gymnastics (in rotation). I also love food and cooking which helps.

Hair: Brown

Eyes: Blue

Height: 5’9”

Weight: 62 kgs @ approx. 9-14 % body fat

The optimisation of my health has been a lifetime goal for me as I have always known bad health growing up; with many people believing I was a moaner / hypochondriac. I have found things in my life that have massively improved my health but it is very much up and down and a recent downturn led me to find this site.

**BREIF MEDICAL HISTORY**

Childhood conditions:

Fractured skull @ 18 months (opened a ‘childproof’ window lock)

Severe Eczema / Asthma / Heyfever

Various Aches Pains / Bodily inflexibility / Pain and tightness near Diaphragm that got worse with stress.

Backache from 12 yrs old

Mental Disturbances from 09 yrs. old – inability to handle stress, depression, low self-worth, suicidal tendencies, & bi- polar type symptoms. Sporadically reoccurs\*

Always had stomach / digestive complaints. Had Symptomatic H.Pylori for years finally diagnosed @ 21 was given a single antibiotic pill and the stomach ulcer hasn’t plagued me since.\*

\*GUT-Brain connection.

Parents:

Mum died 10 years ago of Metastatic Lung cancer that spread to her bones. I’m very much my Mother’s son in terms of bodily health; as she had anxiety issues and generally had a poor state of health.

Dad is prone to back pain and has high blood pressure, and anger outbursts.

### dantothep — 2015-01-08T03:35:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-jigsaw.12556/post-152042>

When my Mum died I developed a hernia and struggled with the grief, and my digestion got even worse. This led me to find an amazing functional medicine practitioner here in London that blew away all the other complementary doctors I had ever seen before. She helped my modify my N=1 dietary experiments that I had been doing, gave me some supplements.

Then I had two massive breakthroughs

**1\.** About a year after seeing her she diagnosed me with Pyroluria and I tested high on the urine sample. I was given high dose Zinc (Zinc Picolinate, Zinc Citrate, Zinc sulphate) 26.5 mg x 3. P-5-P and a Methyl based B complex high in methylB12 and methyl folate. As well as decent multi mineral and Mag Malate/Glycinate combo. And of course an Omega 3 oil with Q10. This plus meditation massively helped my mood, stress handling, etc. **I was like a new person.**

**2\.** However some digestive issues still persisted and the Doctor recommended the work of Weston A Price (Traditional food diet, like paleo with fermented grains and dairy). I added more eggs and more animal fat + broths, plus more green veg to my diet and it got a lot better. (I now know I had improved my cholesterol levels and glutathione levels to fix up my leaky gut and start to remove inflammation from my gut). **I was now in better health than I could ever remember.** All veg fresh from farmers market and all meat from grass fed/high welfare sources.

Still a couple of things persisted though:

1\. On and off Back/Muscle pain.

2\. High levels of muscle tension all the time which led me to always stretch etc. I now believe this to be systemic inflammation as I have been able to remove these restrictions (just not consistently).

3\. I also have extreme lethargy in the summer esp. after spending a lot of time in the sun (inability to store the energy given by the sun?) I cannot wake up before 0800 in summer, but always wake around 0600 in winter. Vit D levels have been suboptimal but not out of clinical lab range (poor indicator) all three times I have been tested.

4\. Night time frequent urination, ache in lower back gets better upon drinking a glass of water.

5\. Dopamine seeking behaviours.

6\. Always eat loads and not put on weight.

7\. Extreme moods on Full Moon.

For 6 years or so I felt great, and when I stopped feeling good it was down to an external factor such as stress @ work / home, or because I had stopped taking my supplements and the fix was easy.

I was able to increase my activity levels and recover faster and actually enjoy life without worrying too much about my health; until Nov 2014 (prior to finding the Blogs)

### dantothep — 2015-01-08T03:36:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-jigsaw.12556/post-152043>

**Symptoms Nov / Dec**

· Loss of vitality / energy swings.

· Constant Muscle twitching

· Headaches (front, back and top – just not at the same time). Grade 1 most of the time up to Grade 7.

· Strong aversion to Light.

· Personality change

· Loud sudden noises would anger me or get me on edge.

· Mood swings (hourly)

· Energy massive slump between 1430 and 1700.

· Tight Diaphragm.

· Pins and Needles in arms at night in bed.

· Loss of libido

· Vertigo & dizziness (Inc. a couple of severe episodes when I collapsed)s

· Bi Polar Esqe mania and depression at the same time.

· Confusion

· Memory loss (didn’t know where I was on a couple of occasions)

· Paranoia

· Waking middle of the night 0300-0500.

· Cold hands / feet

· Severe Reactive Hypoglycaemia when hungry.

· Wind

· Gut Pain.

· Nausea

· Weight Loss (went from 65kg to 60kg in 3 months)

· Crazy sugar Cravings.

· Raised glands in groin

· Dark and Puffy eyes.

In this time the supplements didn’t help, have seen a really good Homeopath who has helped with some of the acute symptoms.

I have also been in the care of the National Health Service (GP and Hospital) here in the UK and have some blood tests (although not the right ones IMHO).

I have also had recently:

1\. ECG

2\. Chest X-ray x 2

3\. MRI Brain and Upper spine.

4\. CSF – Lumbar Puncture

5\. Full neurology exam.

6\. Dr Shoemaker VCS test (self admin)

7\. I have a sleep deprived EEG tomorrow morning (which has allowed me to write this journal).

All came back clear, but tested positive for cytomegalovirus (which states in the book that is correlated with low glutathione).

Also had multi coloured mould issue in the bedroom at the time which is now resolved.

**EMF Exposure**

I purchased a Trifield 100xe based upon info on the site and found the following readings:

1\. Office desk = 1.5 - 3 milligauss

2\. Office Background = 1.5 - 4.5 milligrams

3\. Speaker system under my feet at Desk (100 milligrams) – now turned on only when used).

4\. Horrid Fluoro strip lights in office = 25-30 milligrams – not used

5\. Bedroom environment = 0.5 – 1.5 milligrams

6\. Bedroom clock near wife’s head = 20-30 milligrams

Wi-Fi

1\. 2 Wi-Fi signals at work, 1 I can switch off 1 I can’t.

2\. Bedroom 3 Wi-Fi SSIDs out of my control.

3\. Home living area 7 Wi-Fi SSIDs out of my control.

4\. Have always turned router off at Night at home.

Cell

1\. iPhone 6 – always tried to keep phones away from body/head.

2\. 2 x Cell base stations 150m from office (they don’t read very high on the Trifeild though).

**Changes Implemented since (from info in the book / functional medicine)**

· Fish / Seafood daily

· Once a day seaweed (mixture of Arame, Wakame, Kombu, Nori, Dulse, Hijiki)

· Beef bone broth every other day in soup/stew.

· Flaxseed tea x 1 day

· Flaxseed oil rubbed onto inner thigh.

· Pudding of Almonds/Pecans/Macadamias, with Flax, Sunflower seeds and oils, phosphatidyl choline and cultured coconut cream.

· Strictly low TDS mineral water (in plastic thoughL) 1.5 – 2.0 litres per day.

· Strict bedtimes / rising (when well rested will always awake between 0600-0630 – still dark outside)

· Blue blocking sunglasses after sundown.

· Light timing (trying to be outside at sunrise and sunset). This is difficult in the grey temperate maritime climate of the UK.

· Eating 4-5 hours before bed, bed latest 2300 so eating latest @ 1900.

· Adherence to lying meditation (sensation based like Vipassana) every day pref before lunch.

· BAB – have always eaten a big breakfasts.

· Increased Fat intake.

· CT – face dunking in Morning in 55 deg F water. 6-9 full breath holds. Face and top of head are numb for a while after. Just started Chest icing although takes 20mins to get skin to 55 deg F. haven’t been drinking ICE water though.

· Earthing – on iron scaffold structure in my garden and on grass in cotton socks. Only for about 5 mins in morning.

· I collected water from a deep spring aquifer and have stored in the fridge and started drinking it cold before bed.

· One thing that is hard to get is Vit D from the sun in this country.

· Got a Near IR lamp but haven’t used it yet.

· Turmeric mixed in Coconut Oil

**Current Sups**

Although would love to cut these once optimal and spend more money on food.

· Triple Zinc

· P-5-P

· Methyl B complex

· Liposomal Methyl folate + B12

· [Total Mineral](http://catalog.designsforhealth.com/Complete-Mineral-Complex-90)

· Choline + Inositol

· Mag Malate 1 G at night time.

**Current Symptoms That Persist (i.e. the above changes have helped massively)**

· Phased Headache no visible pattern on timing.

· Reactive Hypoglycaemia

· Varying energy levels (daily)

· Night time urination.

· Some days very low energy afternoon affects mood (induces anger).

· Back Pain

· Sleep disturbed badly after salty meal in evening.

· Sore toes (gout/chilblains)

· Piles

· The BAB (even with loads of meat/fish and Fat) @ approx. 0700 is not enough to keep me going I am starving by 1230.

· Dark amrks

**Labs (should I post these on labs forum)?**

I have attempted to compile a spread sheet for the blood test that I have had done through the Doctors before paying privately.

A few things stand out to me:

· Low blood Volume, RBC and MCV = dehydration due to EMF exposure/lack of Sunlight exposure.

· Low Neutrophils = ??

· Slightly raised HB1AC + mid fasted gluc levels = something to do with Reactive Hypoglycaemia.

· Low Magnesium / Vit D = Low cellular Redox Potential casing Leaky Gut

· Low LDL Cholesterol = Poor liver function?

My doctor has stated to me that all my labs are perfect and they have taken every test possible, so he believes that it is solely a Psychological matter and he will refer me to a Psychiatrist / Psychologist.

In a nutshell things are starting to get better, but I need to get them to beyond Optimal (aim High!), I’m thinking missing Sunlight is a massive thing for me should I get a Lumie or equivalent?

### Jack Kruse — 2015-01-08T16:04:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-jigsaw.12556/post-152112>

In the new video's I created for the 2015 optimal reset you will learn a lot about how things go on underneath your perception. You should sign up because it is free. This is what I posted after the video went live.......because it is complex science.

About 12 years ago, researchers at the University of Western Ontario began a battery of tests on the effects of very low magnetic fields in the brain. The data from the majority of these tests showed that very low magnetic fields had a consistent and sometimes profound effect on the areas of the brain that processed unconscious thoughts and senses. In one test, conducted in 2009, researchers looked at the effects of very low magnetic fields on thirty-one volunteers. This experiment was designed to find the exact areas and mechanisms in the brain that were affected by the low magnetic fields. To do that, researchers put each volunteer in an fMRI scanner and prodded him or her with a heated stick. Next, the researchers broke the volunteers into two groups. In the control group, they repeated the exact same experiment with no changes; in the other group, they exposed each volunteer to a magnetic field of no more than two hundred microteslas (a measurement of magnetic flux). Neither the control group nor the exposed group reported feeling a difference between the two tests in the level of pain inflicted by the heated rod. However, the scans of the exposed group showed significant changes in the areas of the brain associated with processing pain (anterior cingulate, insula, hippocampus). The brains of the group exposed to the low magnetic field processed fewer pain signals, although the members of this group hadn’t consciously known it. The results of the study suggest that the effects of low magnetic fields aren’t patent— that is, consciously sensed— but latent. In other words, they could affect and influence brain function without us realizing it. This is why you can't perceive the benefits of the magnetico pad, but if you bio hacked yourself with function MRI you'd see it works. I would remind you all that the Earth’s magnetic field ranges between twenty-five and sixty-five microteslas, about four times less than the field used in the experiment mentioned above. Whether the subtle magnetic field of the Earth would be strong enough to make the human brain sense direction, though, nobody knows. But my bet, is this is how we get our sense of direction and it is how we tell seasons in the hemisphere we live. This information is shared with the Fe/S cores in your cytochromes and has to match. If it does not, metabolic syndrome is the most common disease one would expect to manifest. This is why T2D is the fastest growing neolithic disease in the world today.
