Site opens ok for me.

I think this whole frequency/biology area is really exciting. This sort of biotech was weaponised long ago but the prospect of using it to our benefit in terms of treatment and prevention instead is great.

I'm imagining using interference patterns to create localised standing waves at specific frequencies within a body to treat with light/vibration etc. - without needing, or lessening the need for, open surgery or pharmaceuticals.

Maybe such equipment as used in MRI for diagnosis can also be used for treatment (thinking of the case Dr. K. mentioned using magnetism for encephalisation) in new and innovative ways.

The mentioned effects of carcinogens changing light frequencies could possibly open up a field of technology for converting harmful radiation into beneficial forms.

For wireless communications technology - perhaps a change to Schumann harmonic frequencies or other beneficial frequencies and amplitude or other forms of modulation rather than frequency modulation could turn the tide.

In terms of food and water . . . . maybe it would be possible with technologies to match the energies of food electrons to enhance coherence per person and location rather than causing de-coherence.

I'm not suggesting we replace nature's ways with technology - rather that we enhance and amplify it where we can make adjustment to current technology and keep biocompatibility in mind with future tech.

How about a mono-polar magnetico? Or an electromagnetic mattress with flux created in pulsatile manner at biologically beneficial frequencies? Or a home that uses electricity at beneficial frequencies - a converter that takes the incoming power and changes the frequency to a beneficial one followed by gadgets that will run at that Hz and hence turn all your house electrics into a Schumann resonator?. All just following on from @[Josh](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/8791/) standing on a magnet.

Or a home that uses electricity at beneficial frequencies - a converter that takes the incoming power and changes the frequency to a beneficial one followed by gadgets that will run at that Hz and hence turn all your house electrics into a Schumann resonator?. All just following on from @[Josh](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/8791/) standing on a magnet.

I'm going to just post some speculative bullshit now

\----

I personally don't think that wireless communication can use the Schumann ranges that well without disrupting biology.

Firstly, I wonder how it would affect biology if organisms had to compete with interference from transmitting devices.

It also works the other way round I'd imagine -- that emittance from biological and geological sources with cause horrible signal-to-noise ratios for our technology.

\----

I think that we can make our receiving devices better at receiving weak EMF signals, thereby allowing us to use less power and have less nn-EMF pollution.

This is a limited area of optimisation in my mind, since signal amplification doesn't work at the receiving end to improve signal-to-noise ratio. (you amplify the noise as much as the signal).

Antenna directionality works to pick up signal from "quiet zones" .... if such zones exist .... and I believe we're going to see more wide-area-blast-and-conquer methodologies when it comes to feasibly getting internet connectivity to the world (works best for getting connectivity to a remote area).

In cities though, there is the possibility of having many low-powered "base stations". One of my personal software aspirations is to work on routing technologies that enable content-centric distributed designs that allow for what Pieter Hintjens calls the "EdgeNet", whereby a "base station" capable of routing internet traffic could be your very computer itself. Every internet router could become a base station.

In that model, it is not exactly known if many small transmitting devices is more or less harmful than fewer large cell towers. I think the level of harm will depend on the mitigation strategies that one uses.

Personally, I want the "many small base stations" model, because then I can put my own base station outside somewhere in the garden, and then shield the crap out of it. That is as opposed to some crazy big tower 1km away from me inundating the entire area with lots of EMF.

\----

I think the main issue is always the intensity of transmitting sources, and given the wide-area nature of todays transmitting devices, I only see more power (frequency) increase until a "beam-forming" transmitter comes into play.

If we have to use nn-EMF, then directionality of the transmitted EMF wave is always the goal -- you get it to only the places that you want it to go. The more coherent (as opposed to diffuse) we can make a beam of information, the more "EMF quiet zones" we can enforce. The technology required to do this in an air medium is basically science fiction for now , and I do not have expertise in that field to say how far we're off.

\---

Shielding technology is where I'm mostly hopeful for. You can take an Amethyst crystal and easily shield yourself from EMF today.

I think one day it will be possible to produce materials that have the property of absorbing incoming radiation and dropping the power (to something like IR) or mechanical (vibrational) energy. I think it is likely that we can make those shields entirely out of carbon, which will be cheap enough to afford comprehensive shielding without disrupting sensing of the earth's natural magnetic signals.

\----

Finally, I want a k-laser now!  [http://www.k-laserusa.com/](http://www.k-laserusa.com/)

....

When we get to these kinds of equations just about ANYTHING can be 'proven' (or disproven). Heidegger a German philosopher writing around the same time as Heisenberg, Schrodinger etc said the only important philosophical question was "Why is there something rather than nothing" I found it hard to agree with that if only because the way the question is framed makes it impossible to answer. The way I think of it is it is impossible to really 'think' about these kinds of issues. Even the attempt to think about it shows and assumes there is something there. "Equations" make these kinds of speculations 'respectable' but it is still just speculations and the equations seem more like window dressing. For that reason I think a lot of physicists are really 'philosophers' with equations.................

### yewwei.tan — 2015-01-17T11:21:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tensegrity-14-comments-are-now-welcome.12628/post-153122>

> [Josh (Paleo Osteo) said:](/goto/post?id=153109)
>
> how large does the amethyst need to be?...this is my interest too...

I actually don't know, but I suspect that a thin and wide layer is what we want.

I'm not 100% certain of the mechanism, but I think that certain crystals can respond to irradiation of nn-EMF by generating their own "repellent magnetic field", making them effectively diamagnetic.

Sidenote: I personally think that diamagnetic charge-separated water can repel nn-EMF as well, literally forming a water-shell around mitochondria and other important structures.​

Of the crystals, the pattern that I found is that the crystal:

-   Needs to have a Trigonal Crystal System -- [http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Trigonal\_crystal\_system](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Trigonal_crystal_system)
-   Needs to be doped with Fe3+

The crystals that fulfil these criteria are:

-   Amethyst (which is basically iron-containing Quartz) -- [http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Amethyst](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Amethyst)

-   Iron-containing Tourmaline (usually black and brown) -- [http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tourmaline](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tourmaline)
-   I've seen references to Onyx (but not 100% sure) -- [http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Onyx](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Onyx)

If what I said above is true, then the crystal should emit a "protective magnetic bubble" in all directions that repels nn-EMF within that bubble.

I think the following will increase the radius of the extended magnetic bubble:

-   More Iron and/or Manganese in the crystal
-   Stronger native magnetic field => I think the crystal can absorb and amplify magnetoelastic waves from the earth (this is apparently part of the mechanism behind how the Greenfield encoder I have on my wrist works). An experiment will be to see if the buble is extended by having a Schumann generator nearby.

This can easily be tested, by having:

-   a transmitting WiFi router
-   a variety of Amethyst Crystals with varying levels of iron => in general, if you control for EMF irradiation, and get all the crystals from the same source, the Iron content is generally proportional to how dark the Purple Hue of the crystal is.
-   a radio frequency Trimeter
-   a Schumann generator

Put the WiFi router and Crystal some distance apart, then just modify the variables I talked about above, and measure the EMF field strength while standing next to the Amethyst, while progressively walking closer to the WiFi router and checking the field strength as you get closer and closer.

Then of course, you can just do the simple test of the difference in EMF field strength between putting an Amethyst Crystal between you and your WiFi router vs it not being there.

Unfortunately, I left my small Amethyst Crystal in Melbourne , but this sounds like a cool experiment to do once I manage to control for some of those variables. Now that you mention it, I'll probably go and pick up another amethyst crystal and check for any effects.

### Josh — 2015-01-17T18:24:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tensegrity-14-comments-are-now-welcome.12628/post-153155>

[https://microbewiki.kenyon.edu/index.php/Bioluminescent\_Bacteria\_in\_Human\_Settings](https://microbewiki.kenyon.edu/index.php/Bioluminescent_Bacteria_in_Human_Settings)

> Bioluminescence is the production of visible light through a biochemically-mediated reaction, most commonly occurring in the marine organisms. Organisms use this visible light as an effective way of communication with other organisms (4). Light production has co-evolved with a wide range of organisms, especially those in bottom dwelling habitats, and it’s very important for their functioning, including defense against predators, hunting, and attracting a mate (4) (2). Bioluminescent bacteria are all [Gram-negative](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gram_negative), non-sporeforming and motile (6). One example of the benefits of producing light is found in the [zooplankton](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zooplankton) by researchers of the Hebrew University of Jerusalem; Zooplankton is attracted toward the luminous marine bacteria and ingests the bacteria, but it can’t digest the bacteria, so the bacteria stay in the zooplankton’s gut and produce light. This visible light also helps the zooplankton to attract their own predators (fish) to ingest them. Zooplankton can access to the nutrients in the fish gut and it’s very important for their survival in the deep and dark ocean (2). Based on the Annual Review of Marine, this phenomenon has evolved at least 40 times among existing organisms. In bacteria it has been suggested that the light-emitting reaction came from as a detoxification mechanism to remove the oxygen and also other possibility is that luciferase function like a “secondary respiratory chain” (6). By now scientists classified eleven species of bioluminescent bacterial into four genuses: Vibrio, Photobacterium, Alteromonas, and Photorhabdus (5).

[http://journals.plos.org/plosbiology/article?id=10.1371/journal.pbio.1001783](http://journals.plos.org/plosbiology/article?id=10.1371/journal.pbio.1001783)

> symbiont colonizes the tissues, how is it maintained in balance throughout the life of the host? Two early studies demonstrated that the squid-vibrio system is on a daily rhythm. The per-cell luminescence of the bacteria is brightest in the evening, when the squid is out foraging[\[36\]](http://journals.plos.org/plosbiology/article?id=10.1371/journal.pbio.1001783#pbio.1001783-Boettcher1), and ~90% of the symbiont population is expelled from the light organ each day at dawn[\[37\]](http://journals.plos.org/plosbiology/article?id=10.1371/journal.pbio.1001783#pbio.1001783-Graf1). When the technology became available, we explored the host and symbiont transcriptome over the day-night cycle to discover the dialogue between the partners underlying these daily rhythms [\[38\]](http://journals.plos.org/plosbiology/article?id=10.1371/journal.pbio.1001783#pbio.1001783-Wier2). The resulting data led to the finding that the host crypt epithelium undergoes dramatic remodeling over the day-night cycle. These diel changes in host tissues appear to reflect variations in the food source provided to the bacterial symbionts. In response, bacterial gene expression vacillates from anaerobic respiration to anaerobic fermentation, a switch that would dramatically alter the pH environment of the crypts. These data demonstrate that maintenance of the squid-vibrio symbiosis is a dynamic process with a profound daily rhythm.
>
> [\[39\]](http://journals.plos.org/plosbiology/article?id=10.1371/journal.pbio.1001783#pbio.1001783-HeathHeckman1). We identified two different Cry isoforms in the host. The gene encoding one cycles in the head with a pattern similar to that described in other animals, where the response is to diel environmental light cues; in contrast, the other _cry_gene has higher expression in the light organ and responds to the cycling of symbiont luminescence. The cycling of the light organ _cry_ gene requires the presence of the symbiont, and the symbiont must be luminous, i.e., Δlux strains do not induce cycling of the light-organ _cry_gene. Further, similar to development, to obtain normal _cry_ cycling in the light organ requires not only the presence of the bacteria, but also their presentation of MAMPs, which appear to synergize with symbiont luminescence.

### Josh — 2015-01-17T18:25:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tensegrity-14-comments-are-now-welcome.12628/post-153156>

[http://www.photobiology.info/Lin.html](http://www.photobiology.info/Lin.html)

**"Figure 23.** The confined environment, in which the produced autoinducers can accumulate, is the key to the activation of the quorum sensing mechanism.
It is known that marine luminescent bacteria that live free in the ocean do not emit light, **_while bacteria living as symbionts in marine organisms and/or in a localized, confined environment emit a high level of light._** Therefore, in order for the light emission to occur, luminous bacteria have to grow in a confined, nutrition-rich environment, in which the autoinducer can be accumulated. When the concentration of the autoinducer increases to a certain level in the extracellular environment, the autoinducers will activate the luminous system of luminous bacteria. This process has been called "quorum sensing", as the bacteria literally sense when a sufficient concentration of bacteria has been reached based on the accumulation of autoinducer (Figure 23). "

### Josh — 2015-01-17T23:39:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tensegrity-14-comments-are-now-welcome.12628/post-153192>

I am thinking that the significance of HSP's and their binding to TLR4 have a place here?

> **Levee 27:**
>
> _**Heat shock proteins**_ (HSP). HSPs are proteins that are only expressed when the cell is faced with higher temperatures or other cellular stress. It appears that heat shock proteins’ origins come from direct transcriptional processes once a stressor is encountered. Most heat shock proteins are named by their weight, but the more important ones have names. One such example is ubiquitin, which is a HSP that marks proteins for degradation. Most HSP can be thought of as cellular pathway chaperones that help the cell do the business of homeostasis more effectively. They are very active in infection, trauma, burns, inflammation, exercise, hypoxia, starvation, nitrogen depletion, alcohol use, UV radiation, and heavy metal poisoning.
> The interesting thing about these proteins is that they seem to affect tertiary and quaternary structural folding of proteins in our bodies. This effect seems to be lost as we age so we see a defect in protein folding in aging related diseases. They also are critical in transporting proteins across cell membranes in all tissues. HSP are also vital in maintenance of steroid receptor function. Folding issues are thought to play major roles in Mad Cow disease, Alzheimer’s disease and Parkinson’s disease.

[http://jackkruse.com/the-quilthow-to-beat-agin/#HeatShock](http://jackkruse.com/the-quilthow-to-beat-agin/#HeatShock)

Toll-like receptors in Multiple Sclerosis. Ligation of TLR2 and TLR4 induces the production of IL1, IL6 and IL12, which induce the differentiation of naïve T cells into Th1 and Th17 cells. Th17 and Th1 cells secrete IL17 and INFγ respectively.**...

**

> TLR4 is expressed on monocytes and macrophages, myeloid DC and T and B lymphocytes, as well as intestinal epithelium. It can bind LPS from Gram-negative bacteria, bacterial and endogenous HSP, as well as the endogenous ligands HMGB1, fibrinogen, heparan sulphate and hyaluronic acid.
>
> TLR4 expression has been identified in cerebral endothelial cells \[[28](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3714200/#b28)\] and microglia by RT-PCR \[[25](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3714200/#b25)\]. Both TLR4 and its endogenous ligand HMGB1 are increased in expression in the CSF mononuclear cells of MS patients compared to healthy controls \[[33](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3714200/#b33)\]. Association studies of functional (missense) mutations in _TLR4_(Asp299Gly and Thr399Ile) failed to identify any association with MS \[[42](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3714200/#b42),[43](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3714200/#b43)\]. A subsequent study of nine_TLR4_ single nucleotide polymorphisms (SNP) tested for association with MS in 362 MS patients and 467 healthy controls also failed to identify any significantly associated loci \[[44](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3714200/#b44)\].

**

[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3714200/](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3714200/)**

### yewwei.tan — 2015-01-18T00:47:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tensegrity-14-comments-are-now-welcome.12628/post-153198>

> [David Limacher said:](/goto/post?id=153178)
>
> Could it be possibly to take crushed amethyst crytal, add it to paint and paint windowless rooms with the amethyst infused paint?

My hunch is that this will work , so long as the paint medium doesn't disrupt the function of the amethyst.

I suspect that if you have a paint medium which can convert incoming energy (EMF) into mechanical energy (like vibration), then you could even take advantage of the natural piezoelectric nature of amethyst to generate a larger repellent magnetic field. I have no idea if the EMF->Vibration conversion is possible BTW.

Chalcedony ([http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chalcedony](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chalcedony)) and Jasper ([http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jasper](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jasper)) are basically rocks that are riddled with micro amethyst crystals. "micro" here is used literally, in that the embedded amethyst crystals are measured on a micro-meter scale.

The historical traditions value those rocks as having "unique energetic properties" as well, and probably they should be tested to see if the effect is the same as using a pure Amethyst.

### PaulG — 2015-02-07T03:20:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tensegrity-14-comments-are-now-welcome.12628/post-154948>

**Are dark photons behind biophotons? by Matti Pitkanen**
[http://journals.sfu.ca/jnonlocality/public/journals/1/PREPRINTS/PitkanenII-105122013.pdf](http://journals.sfu.ca/jnonlocality/public/journals/1/PREPRINTS/PitkanenII-105122013.pdf)

> One can raise a long list of questions about biophotons.
> 1\. **What is the actual nature of biophotons?** If biophotons are not primary entities (contrary to what is usually believed), what is behind biophotons? Could biophotons be decay products of something more fundamental and perhaps new from the perspective of recent day physics? If biophotons are fundamental entities responsible for control and communication, the extreme weakness of their intensity becomes a problem. The idea about biophotons as leakage of more fundamental entitities could allow even metabolic role for the these entities.
> 2\. **How biophotons are produced?** The proposed mechanism based on oxidative stress does not explain coherence nor the reported communication and control function.
> 3\. **How do biophotons or more fundamental entities relate to biology** in general and specifically to metabolism, to neuroscience, to certain findings of Gariaev’s group \[I10\], to Becker’s DC currents \[J5\] and the related TGD inspired model discussed in \[L1\]? **Could biophotons relate to vision and imagination**? Bischof \[J6\] and Bokkon et al \[J27, J13, J8, J14, J7\] have made several proposals in this respect. **What is behind the correlation between EEG and biophotons** (for which multiple sources of evidence exist - \[J4, J19, J16\] - or that between the changes in biophoton emission in meditative \[J9\] and qigong practices \[J12, J21, J23, J22\]? What about remote mental interactions, which are natural and ubiquitous if TGD-inspired biology is accepted: **could biophotons or more fundamental entities provide a control and communication tool**?

Of course, the sleeping underwater thing was said tongue-in-cheek, but I do think it is plausible in the future to alternate being "as asleep as possible" and "as awake as possible". Being submerged in temperature-controlled water, surrounded by weak IR lasers, and a strong magnetic field, is probably a good combination to get as good a sleep state as possible. 6 hours of that a night would be incredibly restorative.

Sidenote: I'm not looking at particularly cold water per se. Cooling of the body occurs on the order of 1-2 degrees Celsius during sleep, and major cooling will definitely disrupt functions. I do however think that it would be possible to say safely drop body temp by up to 4-5C across a night of sleep, while using strong magnetic fields to induce a deep sleep state, and come out of the process with much better energetic and signalling function.

We're talking about sleeping in 27-30C water here, not some cold tub. Cooling down of core temperature will have to happen mostly via internal mechanisms, and not some external cold stimulus. ie: Melatonin leading to massive uncoupling. Being surrounded with water is still valuable for doing all sorts of things though -- temperature regulation, solutes like epsom salts or H2O2, etc ....

I did some small subjective experiments and it feels like if I carry them together with switched on cell phone it makes me nauseous ;-(

I haven't had much time to test

But one thing I can say for sure is that Amethyst in particular is likely diamagnetic in some form, and excludes EMF away from itself.

This is a problem, because the way that the nn-EMF waves are going to be distorted by the crystal will depend on very many factors which I have no clue about how to control right now. With my relatively cylindrical crystal, it seems to increase the power per unit area of EMF waves in a direct straight line from the crystal to the emission source.

I can't make a good recommendation, except to actually take a meter and test the effects. This is hard to do, so personally I would recommend that if people want to try out the addition of Amethyst crystals, they should create a context whereby they can determine the effects of before-and-after use of the crystal.

You do this like kovita does, by having a qualitative measure of redox potential to guage the effects of amethyst crystal introduction. In kovita's case, it's pretty obvious that having amethyst near her did not do her any good at all. Discontinue use if this is the case

I have no clue if these effects generalise to black tourmaline. Need to get a big crystal and test ....

....

Yew, excellent scientifical work as always ;-) I can definitely confirm that all attempts I made to use amethyst did fail for me. I have no meter, but my personal built-in meter in my head told me it hurts, not protect. The effect of the water brought to my mind a memory of a documentary movie abput the best czech gymnasts of all times, Vera Caslavska. She was the female hero of the 1968 Mexico OLympic Games. She is still very fit and preserves remarkably sharp brain. She said in the documentary her secret recipe is to fill her bathtub woth warm water each evening at 8pm, get in and fall asleep. She than wakes up after midnight in a room temperature water and moves to her bed. I always wondered what is the "magic" of this recipe, but after the last blogs I started to suspect the hydrostatic pressure and now I see there is another point to consider.
It makes me thing as well about the fetus enclosed inside of the maternal body surrounded by wateralike liquid. Should be very reasonable emf protection, but than there is growing evidence that the fetus could be badly affected by the high nnEMF. So is it the maternal tissue somehow translating the high nnEMF into damage or is the fetus so sensitive that even such a powerfull filter as combination of water and flesh is not enough and the damaging effect happens directly? If the effect should be direct than it is reasonable to think the evolution did develope protection which is not reasonable for nowadays environment.

ugh

# Thread: Coffee pods and capsules .....danger lurking?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coffee-pods-and-capsules-danger-lurking.12629/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 22

### caroline — 2015-01-16T06:01:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coffee-pods-and-capsules-danger-lurking.12629/post-152974>

Does anyone have any thought on pods or capsules?

I was skyping wth Josh the other day and kinda had a lite bulb moment or two. Josh knows that I drink a lot of coffee ...and I use capsules. My lips were a little swollen and a bit numb ...and it isn't from too much kissing .....I don't think ...lol

You might wonder why I don't pay attention to this ....but since I crashed and burned in the street a year ago and damaged some nerves in the left side of my mouth - I am always a little numb on that side anyway

What are the ramifications .... if any??? The ones I use are plastic. I used to use Nespresso which are aluminum.

Josh was talking to me about metal toxicity ..... but plastic is probably much worse - even if it is BPA free. there is something about synthetic estrogen....
And even if it is a metal capsule - there are plastic parts in the machine. Apparently an espresso capsule is punctured 28 times ...so what would be leaching into the coffee?

I suppose to use a capsule everyone once in awhile wouldn't be a problem ....but here in Australia - sales of these things are thru the roof ....

As of yesterday I stopped using them - they are way too expensive anyway....

Any thoughts?

# Thread: Hello forum!!!!!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-forum.12632/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 25
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Erik — 2015-01-16T19:38:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-forum.12632/post-153031>

Ok so i will try to make this as short as possible.

My name is Erik, i am 19 years old, and a 4 years acne-rosacean. Since i was twelve, i was interested in bodybulding. I
read LOTS of books in the past, but when i was 14 i changed my focus to nutrition, and read as much as i could. Books, e-books
, forums, videos, testimonials, everything.

Sadly enough, i started the Atkins diet when i was 15 and felt super great: lost weight, had energy, an everything, even though
it was like conventional paleo ( i ate no seafood, i drank lots of caffeine, and no offal, just muscle beef and cabbage).
I did that diet for maybe a year, when i developed acne. My acne wasnt so bad beacuse i didnt eat any sugar at all. But for
those days, i sarted having kinda mild epileptic seizures. Those were just like mild brain fogs that would last 2 mins.
There where inocuous, but scary. So where did i go? to the doctor (fuck!!). He prescribed me Tegretol (carbamazepin),
which resulted in an allergy al over my body (a rash). Then he changed my pills to Trileptal. This gave me no allergy,
but aggravated my acne and made me feel abscent-minded all the time. I realised anyway that these "mini fogs", only appeared
when i didnt sleep much ( maybe after a party or a pijama party). So basically, controlling that and with the medicine, i started
only having them once every 3 months or so.

The medicine made me anxious, so i stopped Atkins and had binges of shitty food and carbs (fruits, sweets, deserts, whatever).
So it got worse. Later on i left deserts and cookies but i started 80% vegetarian (Ugh!! i regret so much). This kinda helped
me a bit ( but not for the fruits of course, but beacuse of leaving the desserts). And i stopped taking my medicine, but at the
time i controlled my sleep, and took mineral supplements. Almost magically, the "fogs" dissapeared almost completely ( and if
one happened, it was SUPER mild, like i didnt just realize i was having it).

So, it was the sleep?? the friuts and veggies? or the minerals?? Of course, later on i found it was the mineral suplement that helped.

By the time my Rosacea started to show up. With my acne pretty much gone, ( after using hundreds of creams and lotions with no relief)
my face was mildly pink and dry and irritated. I HAD rosacea. I started to read of course.

They say it has no cure, but i dont think so of course.

I tried EVERYTHING for rosacea: Herbs, teas, led theraphy, avoiding sun (LOL), drinking urine (yes, i know, ugh!!), applying
my own semen on my face, curcumin, licorice, ginger, lemon, alkaline water, juicing, raw honey etc.

All those things didnt help at all or made it worse.

The only things that worked were: Baths with table salt for 40 mins, fasting for 48-72 hours, leaving ALL CARBS again.

But after all. My rosacea just kept there. It was soul-crushing. With diet and lifestyle i could just control it a little.

But........

On one trip i made with my parents for a week to the mountains, after one day of arrival, my rosacea cleared up!! Wow,
what the hell was happening?? I did even ate pizza!!! lots of friuts and sandwiches!! SHOULDNT I FLUSH AND HAVE AN
ACNE ROSACEA FLARE UP RIGHT NOW??

But it didnt happen. Even more, the following days in the mountains it got even better.

Sadly, when we came home, just days after arriving, it came back. I quickly forgot of my improvement and started to try
home cures again (of course i failed).

Another 6 months passed away and we went for vaccation again, but this time it was summer and we went to Cordoba, full
o mountains and sun and rivers (while the other mountains where snowy).

SURPRISE!!! in this week my rosacea cleared up as well. And again, i happend fast and magically, eating things that in
my hometown would just flare me up.

So as soon as i came home, it started my REAL research. I became even more anti-social. (I started being anti social
with the epilepsy stuff years before, going to bed at 21:00 on fridays and Saturdays LOL)

I tried everything. First i thought it could be the water i drank during vaccations, (¿was it spring water from the mountains?):
nope ( tried drinking distilled water and water that a friend from Cordoba brouht me) but no different. Besides i drank
soda during vaccation lol.

I tried sun. Was the key taking a lot of sun??. No. I mean, it did help a bit, but not as much as when i traveled. After trying
lots of things like this i gave up. But started my search again when, in a Rosacea Forum, some people posted the same thing:
THEIR ROSACEA CLEARED UP WHEN THEY TRAVELED TO CERTAIN PLACES. But they said it probably was for the stress reduction of holidays.
Well that wasnt the case for me, because when i traveled i was extremely depressed and having suicidal thougts lol.

SO WHAT WAS IT THEN????

Like 6 months later. I found about EMF and artificial light.

WOW!!!.. were EMF affecting me??? And everything started to connect. The places where my rosacea cleared up, where highly isolated
:no antenas, almost ZERO artificial light, and no cellphone or wifi signal. And principally, there were full of mountains, in a
kind of valley (EMF blocker????? ). And they say the copper from the mountains acts like a giant faraday cage...

Well, i was amazed. But applying an emf reduction in my hometown was very hard, if not impossible. So with avoiding it a lot and
walking barefoot. My rosacea improved. But i still wasnt cured and flared up regularly.

i started with dietary changes again (ughh!!) and foun out that eating ONLY MEAT almost cleared up my rosacea. And combining it with
swimming in the river 4 times a week did wonders (Cold thermogenesis + Sun + grounded while swimming??) I dont know. but it helped me.

However, i cant always swim beacuse of the weather or my activities.

Later on, after more reading, i found out i have digestive issues, im sure i developed leaky gut from my 80% vegan shitty year.
My hypothesis is that Rosacea is caused by a Sensitivity i developed to EMF thanks to an autoimmune general disorder caused
by my digestive issues thanks to taking Trileptal and eating like shit. I read about probiotics and tried them. Again, they
helped me, but i increased the dosis and my body started to itch a LOT. So i left them. I will take them again in the future
i guess.

Today my rosacea is pretty conrolled. The pimples are ok, and the redness comes and goes. Only when i drink Yerba Mate ( a kind of
herbal infusion with cafeine), i get more red and my cheeks and pennis get an eczema-like patch, but it goes after 36 hours.
So here i am. This year i am going for vaccations again and check if my Rosacea goes away again. If it does, i told my mom that
im sorry but i want to live there. Because sadly, im not happy. My rosacea pretty much screws everything. i dont want to go tho
my class, i dont go out, i miss hanging out with girls.... well i miss a lot of things, all screwed up by rosacea.

Today i started Cold thermogenesis. I submerged in super cold water with 3 medium pieces of ice, and stayed 25 mins.
when i got out i shivered terrbly for a COMPLETE hour!! Is that normal?? lol.

¿Could EMF affect me because of a heavy metal toxicity caused by the neurological drug i used???

PD: I forgot to mention. Now i eat a lot of offal and makes me fell wonderfull. But only cow (is grass fed). I cant afford to
eat seafood. Only hake is cheap here, but i dont think it has nearly as omega 3 as red salmon for example. But here the
red Salmon is like 80 dollars 1 kg LOL!!! so forget about it.

Tried omega 3 capsules but brought me migrania and constipation. Tried 3 different branches. All the same (there were all cheap
anyway, here in town you cannot get supplements from good branches like MOXXOR)

### fitness@home — 2015-01-16T20:37:27+0200

Welcome Erik!

Wow, what a lot of information in your first post. I think you are in the right place and have made some correct assumptions about the EMF in your environment.

I have the flushing type of Rosascea on my cheeks (and sometimes my ears). Have had "rosy" cheeks since I was a child. Fortuantely, none of the acne that can also come with it. Certain foods and red wine can set off flushing for me.

# Thread: serum IgE flagged as High, antigen as of yet unknown
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/serum-ige-flagged-as-high-antigen-as-of-yet-unknown.12633/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Hope2Learn — 2015-01-16T23:08:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/serum-ige-flagged-as-high-antigen-as-of-yet-unknown.12633/post-153063>

Earlier I was at a pre-BHRT gyn appointment and my doctor had my health records up on her screen to show me something. I noticed my allergy RAST blood test results were in (but not yet available on my own online health portal). She was kind enough to go over them with me. Unfortunately, my GP did not specify they do the FOOD allergy RAST, so they only tested danders, mites/roaches, penicillin, and pollens.

\*sigh\*

I was not allergic to any of those BUT my serum IgE was flagged high nonetheless at 142 (reference range: < 115 IU/mL).

I have known for awhile that I am allergic to shellfish because I had a terrible reaction to clams once and had to go to the hospital when my throat closed and my body rejected the food in every way it knew how (\*eek\*).. but I do not know specifically what kind, or what other seafood is included in this allergy. But because it causes anaphylaxis, I need to know precisely before I begin trying to find sources of DHA to add into my diet.

Anaphylaxis is bad enough but I am also allergic to benadryl, so cannot use that in case of a problem, and we live half an hour from the nearest hospital. When calling 911 years ago for my husband, we learned that too has about a 10-15 minute wait before response by volunteer EMTs who I would rather not have do an emergency tracheotomy on me...

Anyway, you can see why it is imperative that I find out exactly what I'm allergic to WITHOUT doing a food challenge or even a skin test, because I have such bad reactions to whatever shellfish it is that I react to. (I am seeing my doctor about an epi pen on the 22nd so that I will at least have SOME sort of emergency response option.)

**SO, for anyone who stuck with me this long to see what my question actually is, here you go:

Does the presence of serum IgE mean that I was recently exposed to whichever antigen my body is reacting to?

OR

will it ALWAYS be present now regardless of whether recently exposed because my body is now primed and ready with little waiting soldiers regardless of the presence of the antigen?**

I know I did not eat any shellfish or seafood prior to my test (which was done after 12 hours of fasting because I also had a lipid test that morning).

So now I am wondering if this means I must also have some OTHER food allergy..?

Many thanks to any who can help me to figure this one out, seeing as how I do not currently have an open allergy tests going on and I don't know any more now than I did before this particular bout of testing, yeesh.. LOL!


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Vitamin D3??
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vitamin-d3.12637/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions

# Thread: Calorie Curiosity
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/calorie-curiosity.12640/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Haley — 2015-01-17T23:43:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/calorie-curiosity.12640/post-153193>

I know we all know calories are not the end all- be all in losing weight or becoming optimal. But I can't help my curiosity with them. Im wondering if any of you intellectuals could break it down for me.

Why is it that when I eat carbs my body wants less calories?
And when I shoot for ketosis i become ravenous? And usually am putting out less energy (because of lethargy).

I understand there is a deep shift happening in my body and there are several mechanisms taking place. But I would like to hear the scientific reasons as to why carbs are fueling me more "efficiently" (even if it is a superficial efficiency).

### yewwei.tan — 2015-01-18T05:23:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/calorie-curiosity.12640/post-153220>

IMO, calories give you rough information for "amount of food" consumed, and tell you nothing about how the contained energy (light) is dealt with in the body

Increased hunger on a high-fat diet likely means that complex II activity is broken to some degree -- ie: you cannot use fat for fuel in the mitochondria. The reason for this was covered in EMF4 ([http://jackkruse.com/emf-4-why-might-you-need-carbs-for-performance/](http://jackkruse.com/emf-4-why-might-you-need-carbs-for-performance/)), but basically, nn-EMF can cause a redox shift that forces you to rely on Complex I for energy. Carbs go straight to complex I, and can be thought of as "fast energy" that burns bright and fizzles out pretty quick .

Your location is the most likely reason for this redox shift happening. Being at elevation (and yours is above 2000m) forces the SCN to speed up, and if it can't, major circadian mismatches will occur. This was covered in the January Webinar (my notes here: [http://tanyewwei.com/notes/jk-webinar-jan-2015](http://tanyewwei.com/notes/jk-webinar-jan-2015) and I give some speculative thoughts here -- [http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/january-2015-webinar.12595/page-3#post-152928](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/january-2015-webinar.12595/page-3#post-152928))

Here's a question, do you see this issue more so in the winter time or summer time?

\----

EDIT: fixed "can be fat" to "cannot burn fat"

### Haley — 2015-01-18T05:47:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/calorie-curiosity.12640/post-153224>

Yewwei.tan-thanks for your input!

Yes I am at about 7000ft in elevation since June (coming from Houston) and noticed an immediate decrease in hunger when I moved up here. That is when I decreased my intake back to roughly 1300 cals /day..and that just kind of happened.

Do you mean that if my complex II isn't working my mitochondria canNOT burn fat for fuel?

I will read those blogs shortly when I get to my laptop. Do you know how I can access the webinar a by the way?

And unfortunately I don't think I can answer your question accurately because the only other time I truly tried ketosis was in winter as well. But I will note that after doing so is when I stared binging again after not doing so for 3 years. And in no way am I discrediting the fact that when I have been in a binge phase carbs absolutely do make me hungrier, too.

I guess part of me is trying to also understand the general cycle I have been in for the last 7 years with restriction/binging.

### yewwei.tan — 2015-01-18T06:17:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/calorie-curiosity.12640/post-153226>

> [Haley said:](/goto/post?id=153224)
>
> Yewwei.tan-thanks for your input!
>
> Yes I am at about 7000ft in elevation since June (coming from Houston) and noticed an immediate decrease in hunger when I moved up here. That is when I decreased my intake back to roughly 1300 cals /day..and that just kind of happened.
>
> Do you mean that if my complex II isn't working my mitochondria canNOT burn fat for fuel?
>
> I will read those blogs shortly when I get to my laptop. Do you know how I can access the webinar a by the way?
>
> And unfortunately I don't think I can answer your question accurately because the only other time I truly tried ketosis was in winter as well. But I will note that after doing so is when I stared binging again after not doing so for 3 years. And in no way am I discrediting the fact that when I have been in a binge phase carbs absolutely do make me hungrier, too.
>
> I guess part of me is trying to also understand the general cycle I have been in for the last 7 years with restriction/binging.

Yup, mistake on my part , meant to say that the mitochondria cannot use fat.

You need to be a member to access webinars unfortunately

The binge eating on carbs is a sign of issues with reward pathways in the brain (they're not getting enough electrons). Ideally, you'll want to be ketotic in winter time where you're at (and possibly even through spring and autumn), but I think a transition plan is needed. I'll have to follow your log to comment further

### Haley — 2015-01-18T06:41:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/calorie-curiosity.12640/post-153227>

Thank you yewwei.tan. My hope is I can pull through and not get intense enough cravings that it drives me over the edge. I do need a transition plan

Would appreciate any of your input as I have followed some of your posts & feel I could learn a lot from you!

# Thread: Blood Pressure Spiking at Dark
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blood-pressure-spiking-at-dark.12642/>
**Subforum:** Biohacking 101
**Posts:** 47
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Huck — 2015-01-19T01:59:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blood-pressure-spiking-at-dark.12642/post-153294>

In Oct, I went to ER because blood pressure was over 230/120. By the time a doctor finally saw me, bp had dropped to about 180/100. They monitored me, ran ekg, and gave me another pill. After 2 hours, bp was down a little more and they discharged me with instructions to see my pcp.

PCP put me on some drugs and bp was ok until 3 days ago.

1/15, 1/16, 1/17 and it looks like it is happening again tonight (1/18), my bp is normal from about 10 am until about 5 pm. Then it starts going up quite rapidly and by 7 pm is over 150/85. By 9 pm it is well over 170/90, and I have seen numbers as high as 230/130. By morning it has usually come down quite a bit and returns to normal after I take the morning medication.

All of the episodes were at night, starting sometime between 6 pm and 9 pm and maxing out sometime between 11pm and 2 am.

I believe this is called malignant hypertension. All I am finding on the web is a bunch of frustrated people who have been to the ER several times, tried just about every possible medication, and had every test the doctors can think of, with little to no success.

Hopefully I am seeing my pcp tomorrow for some tests. I think she is going for the basics: kidneys, adrenals, thyroid.

I have increased my water (spring) consumption, increased my magnesium, been really careful with diet, grounded for about 1/2 hour a day, and started walking again. The grounding and walking lower my bp during the day, but the effect only seems to last for a couple of hours.

My doc and I changed when I take some of the medication to take it about 5 pm, just before the rapid increase starts. Tonight is the 1st time to try it and I don't think it is helping. Between 4:30 pm and 6:30 pm, bp increased from 124/77 to 143/76. The systolic seems to increase by about 10 points an hour.

So I need some ideas.

Could the increase in bp be related to change from daylight to night? If so, what can I try?
What tests should I have run?

Thanks for any help you can give. It is scary having this kind of problem happening late at night when you know if you go to the ER, you will just sit and wait for hours - unless you have a heart attack or stroke!

### Inger — 2015-01-19T10:10:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blood-pressure-spiking-at-dark.12642/post-153309>

Huck, are you avoid all fake lights after sunset?

### Huck — 2015-01-19T15:13:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blood-pressure-spiking-at-dark.12642/post-153336>

I tested every hour yesterday to identify when this happens.

At 5 pm, when it is still light, BP starts to increase. My doc and I switched some of my BP meds around so I took some right when the increase started. This seemed to work last night.

I need to better about artificial lights. I think the red light on my heater is the only light that stays on in the bedroom.

### kovita — 2015-01-19T15:49:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blood-pressure-spiking-at-dark.12642/post-153340>

how is your cortisol? have you done ASI? For sure there are many different things that could be happening....

### kovita — 2015-01-19T15:59:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blood-pressure-spiking-at-dark.12642/post-153345>

just thoughts...your rising BP does not responde well the BP medication. If i recall well most of the BP meds work on renin system, so i would definitely think in adrenalin. Adrenalin as well raises the BP very quickly. Now what could cause massive adrenaline release connected witht the change from the light to the darkness? Have you tested whether the same happens if you fake night during the day or it is directly connected to the biological clock in you? have you tried some meditation/relaxation techniques to see what will happen?

### Huck — 2015-01-19T23:03:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blood-pressure-spiking-at-dark.12642/post-153366>

> [kovita said:](/goto/post?id=153345)
>
> just thoughts...your rising BP does not responde well the BP medication. If i recall well most of the BP meds work on renin system, so i would definitely think in adrenalin. Adrenalin as well raises the BP very quickly. Now what could cause massive adrenaline release connected witht the change from the light to the darkness? Have you tested whether the same happens if you fake night during the day or it is directly connected to the biological clock in you? have you tried some meditation/relaxation techniques to see what will happen?

Cortisol was low when I did ASI. Sometimes I use a progesterone cream and I think that boosts my cortisol. I also changed to methylated versions of B12, lipoic acid, and folate. Energy level has improved since then.

Haven't tried to fake night. Actually, I'm scared to do anything that might bring it on.

I have tried a relaxation method that usually relaxes me. When these episodes come on, I feel like I can relax my body, but my brain goes into overdrive. I have also tried EFT (tapping), but I don't see any obvious difference. Also tried deep breathing. It seems to help when BP is a little high, but has no noticeable effect when systolic is over 200.

I had a doc appt today and gave a copy of **TENSEGRITY #7: BLACK HOLE SUN, HERE WE COME** to my doc and her RN. I don't understand very much of it, but I know that I need protons to feel good. In summer, my bp drops considerably, in fact 2 summers ago, I was completely off bp medication. I have a followup in 10 days, so I hope they read and can make suggestions based on it.

### caroline — 2015-01-20T02:58:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blood-pressure-spiking-at-dark.12642/post-153391>

Hi Huck! you have a heater in your bedroom???

### Jack Kruse — 2015-01-20T03:43:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blood-pressure-spiking-at-dark.12642/post-153395>

How is BP affected? You need the accessory proton pump in arteries.......ala Pollack's work. When plasma is in hydrophilic tube a large central EZ is made that increase uni-directional flow free of energy charge.......when you lose that ability your heart must make up the difference. This increases the outflow from the PVN. It can be cause be a loss of electrons in the vessels to make the arteries hydrophobic and less able to make the proton flows. What powers the plasma and proton flows? The sun........skin power's the zeta potential and makes the EZ and you need the sulfated D3 and cholesterol to make the hydrophilic walls of the arteries..............quantum jazz at work for high BP.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-01-20T03:44:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blood-pressure-spiking-at-dark.12642/post-153396>

Vitamin D3 is a calcium channel blocker..........a natural BP medicine. And this is happening to you at night......when no sun is present. Tells me your redox is bad bad bad........

### Jude — 2015-01-20T04:12:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blood-pressure-spiking-at-dark.12642/post-153400>

Huck, great input from JK.

### BTA — 2015-01-20T04:23:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blood-pressure-spiking-at-dark.12642/post-153403>

The heart is an endocrine organ too. The heart muscle cells produce natriuretic hormones

[http://ajpheart.physiology.org/…/ajpheart/290/1/H17.full.pdf](http://ajpheart.physiology.org/…/ajpheart/290/1/H17.full.pdf)

[http://ajpheart.physiology.org/content/290/1/H17](http://ajpheart.physiology.org/content/290/1/H17) )

.
.
.
.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-01-20T06:31:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blood-pressure-spiking-at-dark.12642/post-153407>

Skin and blood are big players in BP.......why? The effects of pulsed non native irradiation may also be transmitted chemically in the blood. It affects the ion balance on colloidal particles such as those in blood plasma, giving them the ability to remove calcium ions from surfaces go make cell go from hydrophilic to hydrophobic. EMF also dehydrates so it makes sense a cell would become more hydrophobic as you get dehydrated. The effect on intracellular water is to lower the size of the EZ and the result is a higher BP. This effect is routinely used in electronic water conditioners to remove lime scale in water systems. However, the treated water has biological effects similar to direct irradiation, presumably due to its removing calcium from cell membranes. When this process happens in cell membranes you short circuit the Dirac mass of your topologic insulators.........and you lose you magnetic sense.

Topologic insulators are inherently chaotic on their surface and naturally show extreme disorder of the surface electrons on cell membranes. In plasma research individual electrons act as massless electrons, but groups of these electrons form a quasi-like crystalline structure on the surface that is chaotic but has a definite mass. This is known as the "Dirac mass." This Dirac mass is interesting for biophysics because of how it can interact with magnetic fields...........(large breadcrumb alerts)

### Huck — 2015-01-20T15:07:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blood-pressure-spiking-at-dark.12642/post-153427>

I started using a Bionase Intranasal Light for night-time sinus congestion. It is 630 nm red light. I know that infrared is also used for hypertension therapy.

Within a day (possibly the same day) that I started using the Bionase, my blood pressure starting spiking. I used the Bionase for 3 days and bp spiked every night that I used it.

I decided to stop using it until BP is under control. I can't tell if stopping made any difference because now I take most of my meds in late afternoon before the spike kicks in.

The meds totally wipe me out. I fall in bed and go into a sleep where I don't move for about 4 hours. Last night, both hands were numb when I woke up - I think from being clenched. Once I wake up, I can't go back to sleep.

Prior to these events with blood pressure, I was sleeping great and getting up 1 time at most for bathroom. I would sleep at least 5 hours straight, then bathroom, then right back to sleep.

### kovita — 2015-01-20T15:12:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blood-pressure-spiking-at-dark.12642/post-153428>

I have vielight myself. It is pretty powerfull little thing. It can make people very sick. I myself had to slowly work up to the full 25 min session, starting by something like 1 min during sunrise and sundown. From my experience, all adults around whom I gave full dose at the first time became quite sick, mainly headaches, malaise etc. Kids do just great, remember kids have much better working mitochondria and much better redox. It can be a powerfull 'kick' which you may not be able to held well. Someone on forum suggested, dehydration plays a role in how good you handle INred lights.

### Huck — 2015-01-20T15:39:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blood-pressure-spiking-at-dark.12642/post-153432>

A couple other things I just thought of. At the same time as spike in BP, I had severe gas (4 days). It would start in chest, move to stomach, and then to intestines. Worse at night. This is not a problem I normally have. I tried drinking more water, but that didn't help. I added a little baking soda, but that only helped for a short time. Doc's nurse said to add lemon juice to water. Had 3 glasses of water with lemon over 4 hours and that got rid of it. She said the lemon would cause the valve to close.

The other strange thing is I am starting my 3rd day without food. No appetite whatsoever. I have 2 cups of decaf with cream and stevia in am, and then just water the rest of the day. This is not normal for me to have no appetite. I plan on adding bone broth tomorrow if my appetite hasn't returned.

### Huck — 2015-01-20T17:52:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blood-pressure-spiking-at-dark.12642/post-153439>

A little more background.

I started taking methylated folate, b12, and lipoic acid a few months ago, based on one of Jack's blog posts. Around the 1st of the year, I suddenly had an huge increase in energy and motivation which I attribute to the methylated supplements. I finished a piece of furniture I had started 2 years ago and started getting my house ready to put on the market. I was knocking things out I had been putting off for months.

ETA: Just remembered this. My norm is to keep bedroom thermostat at 60 degrees. When the temperature is below 60, I am cold no matter how many covers I use. Recently that changed. I have had several night where I had to turn heat off because 60 was too warm. When I woke up next morning, I have seem temps as low as 46 and I was comfortable. Not every night, but at least 3 or 4 times in the last 2 weeks.

I also rededicated myself to getting healthy. I restarted epi-paleo diet and looked for ways to lower BP. I started doing deep breathing, EFT (tapping), increasing supplements known to lower bp, restarted grounding and exercise, and ordered the Bionase device. My plan was to reach my goal weight by summer and have the summer to stabilize and continue to get healthy before the sun started to disappear. The last time I lost the weight and was feeling great, Fall threw me for a loop and I haven't recovered. If I can get the weight off, I think I will also be able to get off the bp medication.

I also planned to move much farther south where I would get 2 months more good sun every year. The only time I haven't had a weight problem was when I lived near I-10 (which wasn't built at the time I lived there). My plan was to move to Louisiana and spend my retirement eating goodies from the swamp and gulf.

This spike in bp came out of the blue. In Oct when I had the 1st spike, I'm pretty sure the decrease in sun that caused it. Doc added another med and bp was fine until this past week and then every night the spike repeated. My intuition says there was a triggering event that caused it. Maybe the bionase or maybe something else. I just know I have to get off these drugs as quickly as possible!

### Huck — 2015-01-20T21:03:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blood-pressure-spiking-at-dark.12642/post-153451>

To show how weird the readings are:
7 am 160/76 (took med)
11 am 109/68
2 pm 123/77

I am supposed to take a med at 12 noon, but haven't yet because bp is still low. I get very light headed if bp goes much below 110/70. This is how my BP was acting before this started.

It's too much meds, but I guess the doc rather have me over drugged than have a stroke.

My hunger is starting to come back after 2 1/2 days. I hope that is a sign that whatever triggered the spikes has just about run its course.

### Huck — 2015-01-25T15:32:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blood-pressure-spiking-at-dark.12642/post-153813>

I think the worst is over. The last 2 nights, bp starting going up about an hour before sunset, but then started coming back down 2 hours later. I tried so many things, I have no idea which, if any, helped.

-   I found several articles and posts that indicated reducing fbg would help, so I fasted for several days and have been low calorie since then.
-   I added a few more supplements and increased some I was already taking, like magnesium, l-carnitine, l-arginine, etc.
-   Exercise - walking and riding stationary bike. I found 5-10 minutes on bike brought bp down substantially. Repeat throughout day.

-   Drinking lots of spring water
-   Retimed meds so 1 would start to peak around 4 pm and another at 9 pm

And the thing that most likely caused the problem was the bionase. I quit using it about a week ago and my sinuses stayed clear until 2 night ago. That coincides with when my bp stopped spiking at night.

### Huck — 2015-03-08T17:18:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blood-pressure-spiking-at-dark.12642/post-157836>

BP problem is still not resolved. I will have about a week where BP either doesn't spike or spikes only moderately at dusk. Then I will have several days where it spikes, jumping as much as 30 points in an hour, and then continues to go up for the next few hours. Almost every time, it has returned to normal in the morning.

On Friday, BP at 1 pm was 137/78 and at 3 pm was 185/91 with a heart rate over 100. BP kept going up and reached 202/102. I schedule my meds so that they peak about 4 pm to coincide with when the spike usually occurs. One of the meds I take is clonidine which is fast acting, so I take it when the spike starts.

I have done a bunch of tests and doc can't figure out what is causing the spikes. About 2 weeks ago, went to a walk-in urgent care because BP had spiked over 200 again and I was burping and had chest discomfort. All their tests came back negative for heart problem and they gave me script for prilosec. I have never had gerd or burping like this before the bp spikes started. I know prilosec is the wrong thing, but I was desperate and a little scared, so I tried it. I had almost immediate relief. Unfortunately, what I have found is that when the spike is real high, I still start burping and have chest discomfort.

Doc said Aldosterone and renin were both low, but lab report says aldosterone is normal.

Metaphrines - normal, catecholamines - normal, troponin I - normal, magnesium RBC - slightly below optimal, amylase - normal, CMP - normal, Cardiac Profile - Normal, H Pylori IGG Antibody - normal, urinalysis - normal, CBC - normal, 3 EKG's = OK, echocardiogram - normal.

Scheduled for stress test tomorrow.

It seems to me this is circadian rhythm related, but docs just give me a blank look when I mention it. A chart that someone had posted here shows blood pressure being highest about 6 pm. I'm guessing that's an average and on lower light days it may happen earlier. If that's the case, it seems to me that whatever that process is that causes bp to rise, in my case it is over-reacting.

What is that process or processes called that cause bp to rise about 6 pm? Maybe if I can use their language, the docs will take it more seriously.

### Huck — 2015-03-24T14:06:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blood-pressure-spiking-at-dark.12642/post-159363>

Don't have any symptoms of POTS.

My BP has been under control for 4 days with no spikes. I see a small increase in early evening, but meds bring it back down.

I am taking a calcium channel blocker and a beta blocker for high bp. These are both fairly new prescriptions for me. My research indicates both can cause GERD. I have asked every doctor I have seen if the drugs are causing my GERD symptoms and they all said it was unlikely. One prescribed prilosec which provided almost immediate relief, but I think it is simply covering the damage being caused by the other drugs. So I want off them as soon as possible.

I cut in half the dose of the ccb almost a week ago and halved the beta blocker yesterday. The ccb has a long half life, so I need to monitor closely for a few more days before I know for sure the half dose is enough. The beta blocker has a half life of about 7 hours, so I think I will know immediately if cutting the dose is OK.

I also have to figure out how to get off prilosec. I'm finding conflicting info so will talk to the pharmacist and see what they recommend.

In addition to the info here on Jack's site, I have been following a plan that is supposed to reduce need for bp medications in 8 weeks. I am about 6 weeks in and I think that is primarily what is lowering my bp. A few other changes I have made are to take an epsom salts bath late in the afternoon, take my BP supplements late in the afternoon, and I added magnesium oil rubbed on arms and legs. I think the longer days with "better" sunshine is also helping.

### Huck — 2015-03-31T01:16:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blood-pressure-spiking-at-dark.12642/post-159869>

It appears I was right that either or both the beta blocker and calcium channel blocker were causing the chest discomfort and GERD. I have been on half doses of both for over a week and have been off Prilosec completely for 2 days. No GERD, no burping, no chest discomfort.

Makes me wonder how many people have drug induced GERD? Take 1 drug that gives you GERD and then another to control it.

# Thread: Targets
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/targets.12648/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 68
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ElEva — 2015-01-19T11:45:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/targets.12648/post-153320>

Hello everyone!

I have been reading this site for a few months now and I decided to start a journal to keep track, to get advice and make adjustments where needed. I will add details later on and now just

About me: female, 35 yo, 61 kg (134 lb), 164 cm (5'4)
Work: full time office job, sedentary, high stress at times; overseas flights every 1-2 months.
General condition: overall healthy (although I did not really dig into this so far); my latest lab results are from late 2013, at the time all was ok. I sleep well, and, if given the time, 8-9 hrs. Everywhere (home/not home).
Eating patterns: until 4 weeks ago, no breakfast; lunch, dinner. No snacking.

Breakfast: black coffee
Lunch is mostly meat+ vegetables (peas, broccoli, greens)
Dinner is again along these lines + wine+ cheese

My FBG is in the 80s.

I am fairly fit: some time ago I used to do 2-3 indoor cycling sessions per week; now I only do resistance training 2-3x40 min per week.

I have gradually put on weight and reduced my physical activity in the past 10 years. Roughly 6-7 kg.
I have been hoovering around 60 kg the past 3 years, only lately breaking the 60-mark.
I have an activity tracker that shows 3-4 km and 10-15 flights of stairs per day in the last 2 years (vs. 40-50 min cycling to work/university daily 10 yrs ago).

Now, my targets are:

-   lose the extra weight/ fat
-   fix what is wrong in my system that gives me heavy, painful periods; swollen breasts and ovarian crams/pain roughly since ovulation
-   get stronger (powerlifting)

I have started having breakfast: avocado +some meat/sausage/ham/bacon +CO + kelp
With this I get hungry at 4-5 pm, which is not optimal as still in the office. We have dinner at 7 -7:30pm usually.

CT: I do with ice-packs + the occasional bath.

It is very frustrating that now things do not happen faster.
My weight is steady; my mind is clear, my sleep is good. I have been supplementing iodine and kelp for 2 months and I still feel another painful period coming my way.
During the holidays this fall, despite loads of sunshine, see food, non-stop grounding (a barefoot island on the Maldives), daily dives and snorkelling, I gained 3 kg in 14 days. Hm, ... must have been all the wine/beer? This would not bother me, but most of it is still there, and this bothers me.

OK, that's it for starters. I have much info roaming in my head right now, but the time is scarce.
BTW, I live in Germany.

### Inger — 2015-01-19T12:07:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/targets.12648/post-153321>

Hi Eva, and welcome to the forum
Would love to see you in your avatar

I live in northern Germany, cool to have more Germans here  (ok I am Norwegian but i do live in Germany so feel a bit like a German..lol)
I am 38 yo and 166 cm... we are almost the same

Now to your questions.....

How is your nnEMF situation? Are you living in a city...... how is it with wifi etc at your home..... how is your artificial light situation........ do you avoid blue light after sunset?
These are really important things.......
(you are getting lots of EMF already from those oversea flights.....)

If you are looking for fat loss, i would avoid wine/alcohol.... beer is really not good  Any alcohol disturbs fat burning I guess. at least for a moment. Especially not good is to drink alcohol after 5 PM

Peas... maybe are not so good to eat, they have quite some carbs too.... now in winter it could be wise to avoid them. Are you eating mostly meat or is it seafood? I would really increase the seafood... lots of good stuff here in Germany
Are you eating enough good fats..... and drinking good water?
I drink around 4 bottles of Fachinger/day... I love that water.....

I just got my grassfed raw butter from Schätze aus Österreich. You could order there too... they have really great stuff  Just google it...

I have clearly lost fat lately... (and i does not have much fat to lose to be serious...... i am maybe 53 kg now) with increasing fat and eating almost only seafood...... and i do it raw! Graved Lachs... Matjes..... oysters.... cold smoked salmon...... or you can do salmon sashimi! I eat a lot of raw herring........ it is my staple food..lol I get it for 6 € / kg here. I eat at least 13 whole herrings / week, mostly more...... I eat the heads too. Mackerel when i get it. But herring they always have....+ raw salmon.... and oysters when i am able to get them.

I would do sprints... not so much of the other stuff. Sprints you can do outside, you get lots of fresh air and real light, that is really important too...
and it does not take long either... 15 minutes and you are done
With sprints you really can increase muscle mass and it works the whole body... they are amazing! Great for fat loss
I just do them on my walks in the woods.... i walk a bit first, then i sprint a little (maybe 50 to 100 m), walk again.. sprint a little... etc.. maybe 5-6 time or so.. it is fun  I do that a few times / week
It is not hard at all  I have read you can get great results with doing it only once (!) / week!

### ElEva — 2015-01-19T13:23:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/targets.12648/post-153328>

Hello Inger,
Thank you!
I'll put a pic one of these days.
EMF: I live in a house in a residential area; the only WiFi I get is ours (which is on stand-by and getting activated the moment a device is looking for it). At work we also have WiFi, constantly on, but again the only signal is ours. The computers are generally pluged in; the wifi is for meetings.
Artificial light: oh my, this I get first thing in the morning as we get up at 6 am to get ready for work/school. We have some daylight spectrum lamps to wake up (the kind where start dimmed and gradually increse within a few min) but actually the cats start scratching on our bedroom door just a few minutes beforehand, so they are the best alarm clocs.
There is a lot to do every morning to get everyone in time out of the house, so I can not imagine to do this without light especially now as ist is still dark until 8 am and already dark at 5 pm. I wear blueblockers after 8 pm.

I drink tap water and Black Forest still. 3-4 L a day.
I aim for 75%-80% fat foodwise; I buy fresh and prepare the meals for dinner/breakfast. Carbs are below 30-50g.

Actually I try to avoid drinking alcohol before 5 pm ; but I do understand that it has negative effect on fat loss and will ditch it to see the effect. Beer I drink only when at some warm humid places. In Germany I drink mostly wine.

I have 2-3 times a week fish/mussels/shrimp. I do not like smoked products. I eat meat and butter daily.

I will try sprinting more often and will keep up with my powerlifting.

### Inger — 2015-01-19T14:33:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/targets.12648/post-153333>

Eva..... I was just out sprinting in the woods... and i was thinking more about you......

Your EMF situation does not sound too bad (but if you are covered in 3G and 4G it is not good....), still take a look here if you have not yet, if any towers etc. close;

[http://www.handymasten.com/#](http://www.handymasten.com/#)

could check the one posted from Lahelada too.......

[http://opensignal.com/](http://opensignal.com/)

where i live is no color at all, how is it at your place?

Have you tested your home with a EMF meter? You really should if you have not yet.
If you would like to, I would be so happy to send you mine so you could try it out... i just have a trimeter but it works great for the rough stuff! Just PM me your address and it will be on its way

Could you increase your seafood consumption? Sounds like you are eating pretty well and lots of fat tho  You are right about not preferring smoked products, fresh are sure way better!
I think the smoke makes it somehow carcinogenic.... still I believe to eat smoked mackerel is better than no seafood at all........ that said.. i do almost only fresh raw wildcaught fish like herring......

I would not drink tap water at all.......... or are your source an own well? That would be different for sure........

I use my blue blockers after 6 PM..... sometimes even earlier........ maybe you can put them on earlier too?
Or just dim the lights and use candles, red lamps / salt lamps... I use salt lamps i love them

I have 2 more suggestions that might help you Eva

1\. I have started taking MSM... I order this from amazon.de... not expensive at all

[http://www.amazon.de/Vita-Natura-re...\_4?ie=UTF8&qid=1421670057&sr=8-4&keywords=MSM](http://www.amazon.de/Vita-Natura-reines-Pulver-Methylsulfonylmethan/dp/B004GA88WM/ref=sr_1_4?ie=UTF8&qid=1421670057&sr=8-4&keywords=MSM)

and i am pretty amazed at the stuff. It seems to really be doing some good stuff for me... especially for my brain. I have ADHD (undiagnosed by doctors but family agrees i must suffer from that my whole life) and also suffered from ED.... and i always knew something was not right with my brain. As I started raw food only many years ago i felt a huge difference.... and i now start to think one big reason could have been increased sulfur - as it is partly destroyed by heating. Maybe it has helped my fat loss too.... i have no clue i do so many things! But if i were you, I would try it out. Very few people can not tolerate sulfur but you will know as you try it. You just need to give it a few weeks to really feel a difference. You can put it on the skin too as a toner, it is great

2\. Are you eating wild greens? They are amazing too. Also rich in sulfur... and many other minerals..... and here is lot at this time of a year... i just ate quite a bit on my walk in the woods.
Vogelmiere, Giersch, Gundermann.... Löwenzahn..... you know
Seaweeds are wild greens too, they are amazing..... but maybe you eat them already? If not i will tell you where I buy mine at orkos.com. They just have the best. Dried, French seaweeds, different kind of. Great quality, 100% raw. I have ordered from them for many years already. I order my nuts there too... and if i like veggies or fruit but i do not eat much of that stuff... anymore..lol

Very interesting that you lose weight when you are away from home........ makes me think.... hopefully there is not something in the environment you do not sense but that hurts you....
if you are covered in 3G or 4G... / bad wiring.. + you have mold in your house... mold + EMF is no good
or is there any factories around........... or anything else you can think of?

Stress is for sure a big factor... EMF causes stress... but also stress in family / work can really hurt us... especially women....

### ElEva — 2015-01-19T19:18:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/targets.12648/post-153353>

So, my periods tend to be very painful the fist 1-2 days. My mother had the same problem. The pain has increased with time and was a shock after removing my hormone spiral (5 years ago). Honestly, I have even considered getting a new one just to avoid all these effects: breast pain, fluids retention, heavy periods and pain/cramps. I have started taking medication at night the first day as I can not sleep. I suspect that I might have estrogen dominance (I put fat on my thights first) and will go for more testing the next month. In the meantime I was hoping that supplementing iodine would help, but I see not much difference. Stress deffinitely plays a role. Right now I do spot CT on the abdomen/belly and hope to see relief.
I wonder if anyone has (had) similar issues and what had really helped?
BTW, my body has had now 5 years time to adjust its hormones; I have not used any hormonal contraception since.

### ElEva — 2015-02-19T22:00:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/targets.12648/post-156083>

Short update from me: after 2 weeks travel in UK, lots of stress, long working hours and fish&chips I had one of the worst cycles/ TOM ever.
In February: seafood, seaweed water (Thank you, Inger!), Iodine on boobs now and then...again lots of stress and long working hours but much, much better...(amazing)
Weight is stable but also is strength. I sleep well, CT now and then in the bathtub and walk barefeet for a few minutes on the frozen/not frozen grass when I can.

Will travel to India next for two weeks. I have no idea what to eat (everyone says I should stick to vegetarian while there, as safer and tastier). I'm Really afraid that I might get some parasites...
I had some shots for Rabies and Typhus (travel in the country), could not find any adverse effects, so decided to go for it, as european vaccines seem to be better than local indian, in case something happens.
I do hope all will be fine.
