Oh, I forgot: I take Mg 500 mg Life Extension, Mg as oxide, citrate, succinate, lysyl glycinate chelate. I have to admit that if I take one cap in the evening, in the morning I just have to run to the toilet, ..it all just runs out of me. I am worried that this might do more harm than good, although I feel good..
It is just weird...Anyone experienced similar things?
Thanks

### ElEva — 2015-03-14T14:19:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/targets.12648/post-158507>

India was very interesting, loud, crowded, hot, dusty, colorfull...The food by default mostly vegetarian, but all very good. We travelled quite a bit in some rural areas, the nature was amazing. This was my first time in India, would not be the last. The good news is that I didn't get sick. So, two weeks there, lots of travel and long working days (business trip): no grounding, no seafood, the food was good, tasty, but I doubt healthy; good sun but very few chances to use enjoy it as all places were AC. Still I felt good
From there went to Hong Kong for another week. This was really bad sleep week. My hotel room was on the 15th floor, there was some kind of background noise/vibration from the outside, perhaps ventilation system of some huge underground parking lot for the biggest mall there. The offices were on the 40+ floor, all AC. I have noticed most asian people eat quite frequently and always warm, cooked food. I mean , of course there is sushi/sashimi and drunken shrimp and some small fish with spices only, but, say 99% is all cooked food. Lots of rice and dumplings, and noodles, starchy things. But still a big proportion of them are lean. They might not look healthy, but are not obese. Might be all the green tea
Well, back to Germany happy to walk barefoot in the garden and enjoy some early spring sun. That is, after mostly sleeping for two full days and losing a layer of dry skin on my face (all this A/C I guess).
I lost a couple of ponds on this trip. I notice I mobilize a lot of energy during, I mean how else can I explain that 4-5 hrs sleep per night, very long working days where I have to give 120%; all this long distance flying and I am still ok. It works well for weeks, perhaps months. And then a moment comes where I am just fed up with all of it. I mean all! Food, language, job, news on the radio even  Have you ever had the feeling when you start reading a book: oh, how boring, how simple the language, how worn out the topic..and this with 50 books you grab? I crave in such moments a very monotonous and tiring thing: like going hiking until the mind is completely blank and all you can feel is being physically tired to the bones. So far I compromise, I do sports we go on holiday (family holiday) and it works. But I still dream of a week on the bike and hiking in the Alps and going up on Kilimanjaro.
Oh well.

Now, another 10 days to go and Florida, here we come  Grounding, sunshine, seafood, family time. I am so happy I can not wait.
Anyone, any recommendations for eating out in Naples and Fort Lauderdale areas? Even Orlando?

### ElEva — 2015-05-14T20:36:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/targets.12648/post-164278>

Hello!
It has been a while since I wrote here, but I have been reading quite often.
Florida was a dream. Every time we go there I think of taking an year off and just staying there...People say it gets very hot and humid in summer though... We only visit in spring and fall and I just love how relaxed and easy going life is; the beaches, the ocean, good food...It is so different from my life in Germany, where life is a plan, numerous appointments and always trying to get more done in one day, only to do even more the next day, etc.., and everyone is in a hurry, and driving is a war, constant competition, a race...
Days are getting longer and it is getting warmer
I have been implementing this and that: blueblockers, grounding (even the cats -we have two house cats- are obsessed with daily grounding!), lots of seafood, less/no TV, CT-ing now and then; early morning sun.
I feel well most of the time but feel trashed when I fly for work. Just came back from a couple of days in Manchester/UK. It is a short flight, but it seems that this coupled to long working days in a very bad environment and mediocre food just take its toll. How was I managing this earlier, I ask myself.. Is it that now I am more aware? Or is this a sign that I am getting older /sicker and can not tolerate so much?
Next thing: detailed labs.
The EMF meter I ordered was not good so I sent it back. A trifield meter cost here 260 EUR ( in US 100 USD), wow...

### ElEva — 2015-05-15T21:31:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/targets.12648/post-164381>

> [Lahelada said:](/goto/post?id=164306)
>
> Hello und willkommen .
> Very late but somehow I missed your initial post . I am also German by the way. You seem to travel a lot. Have you seen the jetlag RX Dr K posted awhile back.
>
> [http://www.jackkruse.com/jet-lag-rx-human-flight-biohacking/](http://www.jackkruse.com/jet-lag-rx-human-flight-biohacking/)

Thank You, Lah!
Yes, I have read the jet-lag-rx, memorized the bullet points. I drink a lot of water, connect my foot, wear ear plugs/noise canceling earphones, blueblockers, take the aisle seat. I never have jet-lag in terms of sleep problems or being tired . I am always tired after the trip, no mater how long it takes: 2 days or two weeks. (here I mean business trips, working all the time; no holiday trips). This is because I can not control my environment , I believe. The days are too full to somehow compensate all of this. It is my dilemma- prolong the time at the foreign place to diminish effects, or keep it as short as possible and go home to restore and be with the family.
Do not get me wrong, I like traveling, I adore traveling, I wouldn't like to live my life without traveling; I like trying new things and seeing different faces, talking to different people, breathing different air, eating local food (now I think of the heavenly scones two weeks ago in Cork, Ireland, and the steaks and the oysters, and how the fields look from above, with all the fluffy white sheep, and the waves at the coast, and the cold wind, and the dewy grass in the mornings, and the friendly and polite people, and the vintage pubs, and the color of the sky at sunset...)
I only can not control the amount of work and the stress coming with it; or perhaps I can, but I try to be most efficient and do as much as possible in the time I have.
I see that I will have to make some compromise here.
Lets see how I do during my next trip (China).

Lah, I have read your journal; I admire your determination and creativity! And I absolutely love the way you write

I never have jet-lag in terms of sleep problems or being tired . I am always tired after the trip, no mater how long it takes: 2 days or two weeks. (here I mean business trips, working all the time; no holiday trips). This is because I can not control my environment , I believe. The days are too full to somehow compensate all of this. It is my dilemma- prolong the time at the foreign place to diminish effects, or keep it as short as possible and go home to restore and be with the family.
> Do not get me wrong, I like traveling, I adore traveling, I wouldn't like to live my life without traveling; I like trying new things and seeing different faces, talking to different people, breathing different air, eating local food (now I think of the heavenly scones two weeks ago in Cork, Ireland, and the steaks and the oysters, and how the fields look from above, with all the fluffy white sheep, and the waves at the coast, and the cold wind, and the dewy grass in the mornings, and the friendly and polite people, and the vintage pubs, and the color of the sky at sunset...)
> I only can not control the amount of work and the stress coming with it; or perhaps I can, but I try to be most efficient and do as much as possible in the time I have.
> I see that I will have to make some compromise here.
> Lets see how I do during my next trip (China).
>
> Lah, I have read your journal; I admire your determination and creativity! And I absolutely love the way you write

Soon we will have a the jet lag light bracelet. I will be unveiling it at my live talk in Austin at the Voice and exit event. The blog is a mastermind meeting place where thoughts come to be entangled. The forum is where the bond is solidified into reality. Live events like Voice & Exit are where I blow your mind. Come meet me in Austin and hear about bio hack construction. I will be speaking, and you should consider listening. ### ElEva — 2015-06-13T16:17:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/targets.12648/post-167963>

OK, lets see what I got so far:
Creatinine (serum) 0.6-1.1 --> 0.8 mg/dl
Urea 10-43-->25 mg/dl =11.7 mg/dl BUN
ratio=14.6
Optimal would be 10 and below. So, there might be some dehydration.
The values for Na, K, Cl,Ca as markers are all ok.
Kidneys seem ok.

Liver function seems ok too.

Pancreas and heart seem ok.

Fe, Transferrin: very, very low. Might be due to excessive bleeding/menstruation. Which is the case. I eat seafood/ meat daily. To me the only way to improve this Fe deficiency seem to work against this strong bleeding. I remember my gyn saying once that shortly before onset of my TOM I had 7 mm uterine lining. --> could this be due to estrogen dominance?
On the other hand the sex hormones seem also ok, at least I only got the E2 value 19.5-357 -->162,8 pg/ml. Others they could not evaluate in this lab.
I will try to find another one that can measure all E1,E2,E3.

Lipid profile seem ok:
Trig 49 mg/dl
HDL 122mg/dl
LDL 97 mg/dl
ApoB/A 0.29
Lp(A) 23.4 mg/dl

Blood cells/Hb/platelets etc. all ok

Thyroid: they do not measure rev. T3 --> my question is it worth trying to find lab that does this when the other values seem ok?
TSH 0.51-4.94 -->1.34 uU/ml
free T3 1.8-4.1-->27 pg/ml
free T4 0.83-1,77 -->1.2 microg/ml
Anti TPO, Anti-TG <10/20 IU/ml
Thyreoglobulin 1.6-59.9 -->3.7 ng/ml
TSH-R-Antib-->0

Then, the shock:
Vit D 19.9 ng/ml
Parathormon 14-72-->91,3 pg/ml
now, I am not fair skinned, but tanned, do not get sunburn, was not even remotely sick the past few years, I spend mornings 1 hrs in the sun, drive a convertible, eat seafood daily (oysters, fish, sardines, shrimp, roe).

How come my VIt D is so low? After 2 weeks holiday in Florida, and tanning almost daily?
I read on the forum and in the blogs that this could be the case only if a) I can not build Vit. D or b) all that I make goes away in fighting some infection.

my hs CRP is 0.4 mg/l
himocystein 3.36-20.44 -->12.9 micromol/l
\-->no infection

So, I can not build Vit D?

Acc. to the RX the doc gave me I should supplement 25000IU once a week.

interestingly, the markers for bone 'trouble' do not seem elevated
Osteocalcin 9.1-41.2-->27.9 ng/ml
Pyrilinks-D 3-7.4 --><7.0 nM

### ElEva — 2015-07-19T14:14:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/targets.12648/post-172345>

Why is it that once we embrace this way of life, small detours set us back to such great extent? How can months of work and strict following of all we learn on this site be wiped out by simple things: a trip, a night out, even eating a pizza as I read in one of the posts... It just doesn't make sense to me.
Next week I will be traveling with my family to NYC. I am so happy and eager and hungry for all it- the spirit, the people, the museums, the sightseeing, the food, ..and yet, there is this nagging concern at the back of my mind: big city, hotel rooms, nnEMF, whatnots.

I just do not like the idea that every time it will be a trade off: a little bit of culture, a new experience - for a bigger chunk of health.
Frustrating.

### ElEva — 2015-08-13T23:30:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/targets.12648/post-174632>

Hey everyone!
Pretty mind-blowing posts in the last days in the forum! Great examples of entanglement.

I will have to dig in in the next weeks. I will take the time to create some habits.

So, we had a lovely sightseeing trip / vacation in NYC followed by a few days on the beaches of Long Island. People that live close to the ocean:, you have no idea how lucky you are!
Ok so first things first through the eyes of a person from middle Europe.
I never visited USA until my early 30s. I was plain not interested. It all seemed so familiar to me (arrogant, heh?). Instead I travelled through Asia, Europe, Australia.
The first time I went to USA was on a business trip, to New Jersey.
Guess what happened when I took the PATH and claimed the stairs to the upper world that Friday afternoon.
I was high for days, I didn't need food, sleep, didn't get tired of hours and hours of walking, sitting around, watching people, being there.
I came back home and wanted to move to an assignment. My husband was surprised but somehow glad to find a new holiday destination (he had visited the west and Florida on previous occasions, years before we met). The following years I kept traveling to USA for business and holidays, roughly 3-4 times a year.
Random observations:
I believe people do live somehow healthier here compared to major asian cities (Singapore, Hong Kong, Shanghai, Tokio, etc): at least there is no cell signal in the subway, people actually read BOOKs or look at each other. Also tend to walk a bit more. Are more relaxed than europeans and asians.
There is no such thing as cold shower; all showers that I have had in all different hotels and states have cool water in the best case.
I was under the impression that NYC and Long Island will be full of small organic restaurants, offering all types of yummy goodies. They must be on some very secret list.
Long island is crowded.
The ocean is lovely, water temperature even higher than expected.
The ocean is dangerous: it was full of life guards and they had lots to do. People died.
Everyone coming to the beach, any beach I have been so far in USA brings stuff to eat and drink. Lots of stuff. It seems like a long beach picnic. Sometimes people even come, eat and leave (and pay $$ for beach passes). Totally weird to me.

Well, I felt well in this two weeks. Slept well, tried all different food, had a lot of sun (it was hot but not unbearable, I believe due to all the shade of all the tall buildings and the sea proximity). Didn't get sunburn, no sunscreen.
Lovely time.
The flight back was only 6,5 hrs, no complaints.

Then I went on a business trip to east-south Europe: Croatia and Turkey.
Boy was this bad. Istanbul is roughly at the same latitude as New York, see level or pretty close to (ca 30 m n.n.). Actually all southern Europe: Spain, Italy, Greece, are in these whereabouts.
I bloated to a new size, all clothes went to tight overnight, got even some zits on my face. Crazy.

Now trying to get back to normal, oysters, grounding, morning sun, blublockers, CT.
CT needs optimization as the tap water is 19 °C; not all shops offer ice and of they do it is pricey to fill up a tub.
I'm looking for a chiller. All the companies I know of offer only industrial size ,
the other option might be to dig a well in the garden, ground water is said to have 12 °C.

### ElEva — 2015-08-13T23:39:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/targets.12648/post-174633>

In the heat people tend to get sluggish , we have had a lovely hot summer in Germany. I notice that my brain melts above 40°C (>100F) as there is rarely air conditioning here; I wonder how is this beneficial to live close to the equator at see level.
If this is so good for one's health and cognition why is all getting slower the closer one is to the 0° and the less efficient and progressive all processes are...? I can think of very few scientists, composers etc. that were born and raised close to the equator.

### ElEva — 2015-09-05T22:04:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/targets.12648/post-176564>

random:
just came back from a few days trip in the Netherlands. It is weird how one pays attention to things that were completely usual before: it rains a lot; I mean a LOT.I do not rememberer so much rain in my part of Germany; seriously.
Locals do not pay attention, everyone still on their bikes, children in the sport fields, playing in pouring rain and some 12-13 °C air temperature. People do seem heathy though, might be all this outdoor time, sans sun bit still outdoors...
I would not say that the diet is very healthy, ..... I remember my first visit to a university in NL, I was shocked that the only things for lunch were some sandwiches and milk, also tomato soup. Seriously, milk? as a grown up? for lunch? I was missing my german canteen schnitzel and fries
Oh, and my highlight today: CT in the Noordzee, Scheveningen. People, you have no idea how lucky you are....

### ElEva — 2015-09-06T20:38:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/targets.12648/post-176612>

update:
I do feel better, clearer, more focused, calmer.
Which is a big deal: I am very emotional person, quick-tempered, impulsive. Nowadays I am way calmer, things that used to drive me crazy do not affect me much.
My hormones seem to be also a bit better, I do not have the painful periods that I had a few months ago; I am also not losing that much blood. This is huge for me as at some point I was really scared.
I am physically stronger, I train only 2 times a week, ca. 30 min and the weights have increased by 15-20%, easy.

The only thing that still doesn't change is my weight. Actually it went up by 3-4 pounds. Pretty much after my NYC trip and (no surprises here) moving to a new office building. Huge one, full of people, wifi, cell signal amplifiers, fluorescent lights.
The first week I had headaches every day: either from the new carpets, furniture etc. or from the rest. The trifled meter does not show much, which is a mystery to me. I will have to purchase a new meter for better cell and wifi tracking.
As we have learned, environment is key: I decided to have at least one home-office day per week, target would be two.
Home is good: I only get our wifi signal if its on; no dirty electricity; I get all my stuff wired.

I cancelled my two business trips to Singapore and China for the rest of the year. I will still fly to US (all business).

This year we are not going to any nice, hot place for sun and diving (it breaks my heart); the consolation is that we did have a very hot, sunny and long summer in our part of Germany.

Lets see that the fall brings to us.

I know that Jack is not done yet, but I am grateful for finding this place a year ago and not, say, 4 years ago. (because I am not a patient person)

### ElEva — 2015-10-24T22:56:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/targets.12648/post-180370>

I love my CT early in the morning, outside, the smell of earth from the fields around, birds singing, all quiet, the sun slowly climbing in the sky....
I stay not so long, 15-20min (full body, up to my neck), I am aware that most of the people here spend almost an hour CTing;
I am not cold, the water warms up by ca. 2 degrees in the time I spend in; I warm up well and fast. The water temperature depends on the weather, lately only in 10-12°C (low 50s) as the days are still warm.
Should I spend more time in?

Wow
Still try to spend 1-2 hrs outside every day; not much of a sun lately though.
We booked two weeks holiday on the equator in January.
Lets see how it turns out.

Finally getting cold, snow is expected tomorrow.

I can not stop wondering how focused and sharp my mind has been lately, how fast I have been working, how productive I am. When in meetings I catch up myself thinking: speak faster, man, I know what you are about to say...

Amazing!

### ElEva — 2015-12-06T14:34:09+0200

CT tub outside finally getting to 0-3°C range. I can stay 10-15 min, but then the toes start to be painful. I do not shiver; warm up pretty fast afterwards, and then I am warm the whole day. I turn red (and I am still tanned, recently someone asked if I've been in the tanning beds- have not); skin temperature goes below 55°F, but then when I'm out, it warms up within seconds.
I suppose it is good this way.
I like this. A lot

It is still very warm for December in Germany; all the x-mas decoration and Christmas markets are just ridiculous when the temperature is 15°C. How can one drink Gluehwine when it's 15°C...

We have booked a 2 week holiday at the Equator in a couple of weeks.
We all look forward to the sun, the see, diving.

### ElEva — 2015-12-06T20:20:33+0200

### ElEva — 2015-12-12T13:48:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/targets.12648/post-183956>

CT outside yesterday, water was -1,8°C. Managed 12 min, this time put socks on. Easy!
Today the water was 9°C. Oh, well, still better than tap water.
My hair and nails grow like crazy.
This week I spent 4 days in UK, normally, I'd come back 2-3 pound heavier due to travel, late nights, alcohol and all the sandwiches and fish and chips. This time, although it was a horrible travel due to fog, storms, rain and late trains, I am back to Germany at the weight. Slept for 10 hours and as good as new!

Next year I will have to travel roughly every other month overseas, I have read that the quantlet mitigates a lot of the effects of travel. It seems that import into EU is not clear yet, and I wonder if it comes with an adapter to recharge here...

### ElEva — 2015-12-14T21:00:11+0200

Hmmmm

### ElEva — 2016-01-16T11:20:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/targets.12648/post-186465>

Short update: had a three weeks holiday in the Maldives: lots of sun, actually all waking hours spent outside, barefoot; totally relaxed, fully energized; this time no weight gain, even a couple of pounds lost despite the heaps of food and wine. I did not pack any sunglasses; no sunscreen either.
I will have to check my Vit D level next week; before we departed in december it was barely 20. I wonder what to do if the values do not go up, i.e. no ability to build...? I also have very low Fe, although I eat lots of meat and seafood. I guess I will have to supplement Fe.

Something I noted: all diving staff, although they seem to live in optimal light conditions, lots of seafood, grounded, sort of CT (due to 1-2 hrs in water daily), no alcohol etc, they all seemed older than their actual age... They all use NITROX for diving, would that be the reason? Or might it be the excess of UV over the years?

### Inger — 2016-01-16T20:07:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/targets.12648/post-186504>

Eva, sounds like a amazing vacation.... Did the divers felt "old" or where they fit, just wrinkly? I guess a lot of sun will give you some wrinkles for sure

If your Fe is low, have you tried to supplement with a few drops of Vetyperoxid in water? I have a book where the author helped his wife that way... nothing else brought her Fe up... but that worked!
I think it would not hurt to try... Maybe you could try to figure out why it is so low?
Must be a reason behind it......

### ElEva — 2016-01-17T17:49:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/targets.12648/post-186575>

HI Inger, thanks!
H2O2 in water? How come this increases Fe? I will have to check on mechanisms... I think I know why my Fe is low, it has been low even as I was very young, it could be something hereditary.... anyways I believe it is low because I had very heavy periods, ...in the meantime the has improved a lot; the last few months...I think all the oyster shucking for breakfast, and CT and trying to spend more time outside is working. I was actually surprised that my Fe is still low, one should feel tired if Fe is low, I am never tired lately ( I might be fed up with something, but tired...nope); will see if supplementing for 2 weeks makes a change at all.

Well, dive guides seemed fit, but their skin seemed very old, loose; and many have bad teeth...

I tried the approach of Melanie, icing my eyes, laying flat on the beach for the first 1-2 hrs in the morning; I will go for an eye check just out of curiosity (myopic 2,0/land 0,5/r ).

I have been thinking a lot about what JK says, that most of us should move closer to the equator, ... There are not many options that might fit to my background, and I actually do have an issue with working attitude/ efficiency in hot climates.... I notice it on myself, all movements slow down, nothing is so urgent/important/immediate to be done asap (which is again a good thing, as it reduces the stress levels immensely...); I believe one can adapt to anything, if the motivation is high enough.
I still believe that being aware of all what JK writes and speaks of, a person who is not seriously ill could mitigate a lot and still enjoy good health
( I keep thinking that JK does his day job and does not live far way in a secluded place, and does not go to bed every day at 9 pm, so he is onto something, and there is a way; What is more important is,that he is driven and generous enough to share his knowledge with us; Thank you, JK)
Time to revisit my targets.

### ElEva — 2016-04-02T16:55:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/targets.12648/post-191897>

Laying in my garden in the sunlight, listening to the April webinar, drinking wine  at 3 pm.

### ElEva — 2016-04-02T17:12:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/targets.12648/post-191899>

I have been doing CT at really low temperatures (like -1°C-4°C) for the last few months. Easy. Now my tub is getting warmer, this morning it was nearly 10°C, less fun; I will have to purchase a heat exchanger if I want to keep the temps low.

It's been now 1,5 years since I found this site. Many issues have tremendously improved. NO heavy periods anymore, great endurance, energy levels, mental sharpness, physical strength. I never had issues with sleep, but this had yet improved. Just crazy.

I gained 2-3 KG when we moved in a new building in August (work). Steel structure, big windows, bad lights, wifi and cell repeaters/enhancers every 4 meters. 1500 people. Just crazy. No CT, BABs, grounding in snow did anything.
I am about to leave this building in a few weeks, new job.

I look much better than earlier, healthier, stronger. But there is still fat. There is a layer of subcutaneous fat all over me. Hm, considering a water fast for a reset.

### ElEva — 2017-05-26T21:10:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/targets.12648/post-214771>

I gained in Mexico. We stayed at a place called playa catalonia, which is ca. 10 min drive north from Playa. Less people, less EMF, same beacheas, same ocean, lots of seafood. Cenotes swiming, hours in the ocean, no sunscreen, 8 - 10 hrs a day at the beach, laying directly on the sand, all the time bare feet. We spent 2 weeks in the end of Dec and beginning of Jan. We actually were at the same time there as some of the members were . All they reported very good results.I gained. Not much, 2 KG, but stubborn ones. I gain each holiday. 1, 2 Kg tk stay. I have gained 6 kg in the last 4 years.
I have improved hormonal situation, I am never tired, very sharp, can go days without food, have almost no cravings. I can run and CT, never get jetlag, and work concentrated full days.
Yet Mexico was just ok. As every place with sun and beavh. I found no difference from Italy, Florida, Ctoatia, Greece, the Maldives, Indonesia ..you name it.

I don't normally measure the meals by weight but did on this occasion so I could work out how many mussels or whitebait were needed (cheap frozen here in the UK, would love to eat Oysters but I can only afford about 12 a week.). ### Jude — 2015-01-21T00:33:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bab-with-50g-protein-80g-fat-still-very-hungry-midday.12651/post-153466>

**I get hungry quickly and get really edgy when hungry, and considering snacking is frowned upon, people are having to avoid me when i'm hungry.**

That statement there reveals that you are still mainly a sugar burner...that is the typical symptom....hungry and edgy and need food NOW!
As Lah and Inger say...it takes one's body time to transition into being a fat burner as everyone is different and sometimes..... lots of food(at the correct time) is required during that transition!
Had a friend who was amazed at the difference of not being hungry made to his life....previously he was like a ravenous lion and one needed to be careful not to get between him and his food! Needed 3 meals a day and on time! Now he can go all day on breakfast...such freedom eh!

### Penny — 2015-01-21T21:20:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bab-with-50g-protein-80g-fat-still-very-hungry-midday.12651/post-153549>

Well, this is what works for me - my breakfast consists of 3 eggs with almost a quart of greens with olive oil and rice vinegar, one avocado and one can of herring - 190 kg - I also drink coffee with coconut oil and coconut cream in it - and d-ribose - along with 6 different herbs like turmeric, cardamom, cinnamon, black pepper, ginger, vanilla, star anise, fennel, cacao powder - then I take a digestive enzyme with ox bile because that is a lot of fat... if I cave and eat a carb - typically honey in my coffee, my downfall - I pop a bitter melon - this acts like insulin and brings the sugar into the cells - but I find if I eat anything sweet with breakfast, it acts like a diaretic and I get even more dehydrated than I already am by drinking the coffee...

I have come to the conclusion I have insulin resistance or something worse... the d-ribose/niacinamide combo sends you to cytochrome 2 with energy already and increases NAD - I get energy off of that - I also go into the morning sun and I find if I exercise for 5 minutes after I eat, that also brings the sugar into the cells - so in the end, I would skip the carbs, add coconut oil ( 2 tbls) and avocado - you might want to read cpc #9 - it's excellent - it seems to me if the hypothalamus is counting electrons, and electrons are found in seafood, this is why when I eat seafood, I am less hungry than if I eat meat - in fact, at least for me, at night, if I wrap bacon around the seafood, I could care less about food for the rest of the night -

_When I'm in the sun, I also could care less about food..._

My particular goal at the moment is to get the water under control and the only way it seems that works is upping fat (seafood, coconut oil, avocados, bone broth) and lowering any sort of sugar - a dead giveaway is how energetic you feel after a meal - not so much => energy is not getting into the cells - so fix the membrane with DHA and a whole lot of things get better...

If bitter melon gives you more energy when you eat a carb, that means you are insulin resistant or even a diabetic -

Carbs I'm presuming should be eaten in the morning because you are more likely to burn them off than if you go to sleep right after thus causing more stress on your pancreas - I think you need 50 grams of carbs to make serotonin but you can get it from veggies without a high glycemic index...

One more thing you can try is to add fiber before a meal with carbs -this makes the carbs not be absorbed as quickly so it isn't a direct hit to your pancreas - there is a product called Well Betx which contains some fiber if you want to go the supplement route...

As far as dosing for niacinamide, I think it's 500 mg 3 times a day for ADD... but as far as I know JK has never given a dose because that would be medical advice and that's a no no...

That's what works for me - your mileage may vary

### dantothep — 2015-01-21T22:25:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bab-with-50g-protein-80g-fat-still-very-hungry-midday.12651/post-153555>

I take it you mean 190g - 190kg is a BIG dose of herring!!

Let me give you an example to put it into context, this morning I ate 450g of mussels (frozen) cooked in turmeric ginger and garlic, with side of greens. All cooked in about 175g of butter plus homemade chicken liver pate. Midday = Hungry. Dehydration is a big problem for me too just cannot see the pattern yet.

Thanks for your exp with Bitter Melon, surely your IR should be getting better with the leptin RX and CT? How long have you been doing it?

Do you know what the rationale is with the timing of BAB and BAD (dinner)?

Will read CPC#9 but got a bit of info overload at present so looking for the hive mind here to help resolve issues in my following of the protocols; then once my symptoms have stabilized (which is happening) then I can push ahead reading ALL the blogs and listening to ALL the podcasts/webinars!!! Time is limited esp when attempting to limit EMF/screen lighting.

Dan

### Penny — 2015-01-22T00:00:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bab-with-50g-protein-80g-fat-still-very-hungry-midday.12651/post-153566>

Yeah, that would be like a whole school of herring...

I think the theory goes something like: intermittent fasting=teaching your body how to burn fat... after 3 hours, you run out of glucose and you have to burn fat - you want to eat enough protein/fat at breakfast to go 5 hours between breakfast and lunch then have enough protein/fat at lunch to go 5 hours between lunch and dinner and at dinner you want to eat enough protein/fat to go at least 12 hours between dinner and breakfast - you also want to time daylight to exercise and eating and nighttime to cold and sleep - so get up in the morning, look at the sun for a couple of minutes to deplete melatonin and let your body know it's daytime (cortisol will rise) - then you raise oxytocin (not sure but it probably counteracts cortisol which when too high maybe it does something to the blood sugar - I don't remember - then eat your BAB - theoretically, you should within 2 months become a fat burner...

When you're leptin sensitive - and if you know you're full and you're not overweight, you move the protein to BAD after exercising - it helps build muscle or helps protein absorb - again don't remember - there is so much to remember...

Something else you might not have thought about... protein turns into glucose... so maybe that is wrecking you - or cortisol might be too high - I think that's why we're not supposed to exercise in the morning during the leptin reset because it will raise cortisol even more... thus raising blood sugar - tai chi anyone?

As far as dehydration goes, a diabetic can't hold their water because they are always peeing it out - I don't see a lot of carbs in the breakfast you mention above... but if you are dehydrated, you will have less energy and maybe that is where the carb cravings come from... the carb pathway makes more mitochondria poo (ROS) than the PPP, but you do get energy faster...

But if I were you, I would do the bitter melon and niacinimide and maybe throw in an adaptagen or two that helps with hypoxia (swelling) - that might help your energy levels...

The other thing I would look at is digestion - are you making enough stomach acid to actually digest the food? Zinc, B1 and iodine are huge players in stomach acid - maybe pop some zinc lozengers -

His book explains it pretty well - how to transition from BAB to BAD...

### dantothep — 2015-02-10T22:01:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bab-with-50g-protein-80g-fat-still-very-hungry-midday.12651/post-155273>

Update on this: I can now pretty easily go from Breakfast to dinner with a light snack in between. I have found the more carbs in the BAB = the hungrier I get faster.

# Thread: New to forums - been lurking in background too long!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-to-forums-been-lurking-in-background-too-long.12652/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 108
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jenny S — 2015-01-20T04:08:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-to-forums-been-lurking-in-background-too-long.12652/post-153397>

Hi all,
I've been going to do this for ages, but long time coming.
I bought Epi-Paleo RX about 6 mths ago - can't remember how I got to it, probably googling health issues.
Had spiralling health issues for years, - like most people I guess. Lots of little issues didn't think much of at time, such as tiredness, tinnitus, bloating, constipation, dizziness, brain fog - put it all down to stress - even thought I didn't feel stressed. About 10 yrs ago started slowly losing weight & getting tireder & dizzy all time. Still nothing wrong after having gastroscopy & colonoscopy. Told no such thing as gluten intolerance, so kept eating gluten. I'm quite tall & about 2 yrs ago was only 44kgs. Luckily found GAPS diet, as GP just wanted to give me lots of drugs & put me in hospital which I refused - knew there must be more natural way.
Have been on GAPS for 2 years & a lot better but still not as good as would have liked. Since following Jack's info have improved quite a bit. Find it all a bit much for the brain but after re-reading blogs over & over sometimes if lucky I have a light bulb moment!
Think biggest improvements have probably come from eating fish by the ton, cutting out blue light at night & less EMF - one of the hardest things I've found to do.
Finding it difficult being in Australia to sort some information such as water, fish & sun.
Anyone out there from or near Bendigo? Would love to meet up with fellow Kruse followers as my husband, family & friends all think I'm nuts - don't really care about that, but nice to have someone else to meet & discuss things with!

I'm originally from Melbourne, and now I'm in Cairns

Will be following along , and feel free to ask any questions either here or directly through private messages or email (my email is on my site)

### Jenny S — 2015-02-27T01:50:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-to-forums-been-lurking-in-background-too-long.12652/post-156761>

Thought I should update this for later reference - amazing how you forget & nice to see progress.
Saw Josh on Tuesday - very helpful & implementing some of his advice from then. Borrowed his meter to measure wi-fi & smart meter EMF's,- very scary! Outside house great, only EMF is about 12m radius from smart meter but other than that none at all on property. Inside house another story. All kitchen & living area really bad even with computer & TV turned off. Can't work out where all coming from - no wonder I'm struggling to improve health. Luckily spare room where I mostly sleep on my sgl. magnetic (husband refuses to have one - waste of money so therefore I don't sleep with him that often, which he isn't happy about plus our shared room has smart meter on wall)
My current weight is about 53kgs which is improvement on 48kgs last October. Sleeping better but still wake up about 4 times. Wearing blue blockers @ night but difficult when big TV loud & most lights on. Nice if husband would come on board but gather lots of other members have same partner issues.
I was enjoying my raw organic milk kefir every day but new laws in Oz have fixed that - now adding poison to raw milk! So all dairy except ghee off my diet. Implementing Inger's suggestion having fish & eggs for brekky, red meat & eggs for lunch & fish for tea. Had 1 doz. raw oysters last night for 1st time. Struggled getting them down but I will keep eating them until I enjoy them. Have learnt all these dislikes are mostly mind over matter & oysters do matter! Had whole jar of anchovies with eggs this morning. Few months ago I would only eat one as salty & horrible - now I love them.
Started face dunking few days ago, got brain freeze within 20 secs using water about 6c. Josh suggested try 10c then come down which is now easy.
Using iodine @ Josh's suggestion & ordered Siberian Red, D-Ribose & Niagen. Also bought some full spectrum light globes for 1st morning light as sun doesn't appear here until 7.45 as on side of hill & find that to late to get up.
A lot less tired than I was a a few months ago. assuming that's from less computing & lots more fish.
That's enough for now.

Don't know whether this should have any effect or not. Home now back to face dunking, but shall have to get something for more CT - haven't got a bath.

### Jenny S — 2015-03-21T02:33:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-to-forums-been-lurking-in-background-too-long.12652/post-159086>

Found out smart meter only sends messages back to power company every 4 hours & of course I was assured there was no danger at all with smart meters.

### cinnamon — 2015-03-21T03:28:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-to-forums-been-lurking-in-background-too-long.12652/post-159087>

Hi Jenny!

Have you seen this video:

This is the video with the guy from the Canadian military explaining about the dangers of smart meters. The part where he talks about the "meshed grid networks" is especially alarming.

Also, it may well be true that your smart meter only **_sends data_** to the power company for a couple of seconds every 4 hours. Unfortunately, it is likely to be pulsing signals a lot more often than that. This is similar to the issue with wi-fi routers in general - they are a constant beacon, emitting a pulsed signal, even when no device is actually connected to them. It's like they are continually saying "Yoohoo! I'm ready over here!"

### nonchalant — 2015-03-21T17:32:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-to-forums-been-lurking-in-background-too-long.12652/post-159110>

When we lived in the city, my 8 gHz RF meter would register a short spike about once/minute. Then every 15 minutes or so, there would be a larger, more prolonged burst with several spikes. I figured the short spike was my smart meter scanning my house, and the longer burst was when it was conversing with my neighbors' meters, and I was picking up readings from several smart meters. I'm guessing sometime in the middle of the night, it (or another of my neighbors' meters) would send a big upload to the power company. I never kept my RF meter on that long. Whether it was my smart meter or someone else's, I think we still got zapped.

All I know is that my husband's occasional twitching while he is asleep has gone away. I used to notice him twitching about 4-6 times in series, each spaced about 60 seconds apart...

### Jenny S — 2015-05-15T09:29:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-to-forums-been-lurking-in-background-too-long.12652/post-164339>

THANKS JACK, had a big win today with my teeth. 6 mths ago I had a check up & x rays & was told a tooth on bottom front was dead & I could see it was grey & I would have to have it out & then root canal. Came home read Jack's blog & added K2 to my supplements..
Also the dentist at time was horrible to me - didn't believe I was cleaning my teeth or flossing which I was doing. Had build up of tartar on teeth - which I now know was mostly from leaky gut.
Different dentist today & I didn't mention dead tooth - X-ray showed teeth all good & very little tartar build up! So thanks Jack the dentist, you saved me from a root canal & saved my tooth.

### Jenny S — 2015-05-17T05:38:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-to-forums-been-lurking-in-background-too-long.12652/post-164498>

CT update. After 42 cold baths, I can finally see a few small changes. I don't shiver as violently. My legs work a bit better when I get out, at first I could barely stand up & I recover slightly quicker.
Thank goodness - was beginning to think I couldn't keep it up much longer when saw no improvements, altho I knew I had too.

### Jenny S — 2015-07-07T10:55:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-to-forums-been-lurking-in-background-too-long.12652/post-171240>

Update - Just had 3 weeks at Clifton Beach, just north of Cairns to get some sun & beach. It was lovely & warm, bit wet but better than home by long way. At Josh (paleo osteo) suggestion I had some blood tests just before I left & 2 weeks into holiday to see what changed. My cholesterol & hormones both improved a lot in this short time.
Just had discussion with husband Alan about moving to beach, he isn't going anywhere. Can't convince him I would be a lot healthier - says he thinks tests would have been same if repeated here rather than Cairns. He won't read any of Jack's stuff - says most of it's nonsense & I could be just as healthy here - I'm just doing this as payback as I moved here with him from beach 6 years ago but didn't really want to, but thought I should save marriage. He's quite convinced I'm just looking for an excuse to leave him.
I'm writing this as I cry my eyes out. Looks like I'm going to have to get tougher like a lot of other "Kruise's" & probably leave.

### caroline — 2015-07-07T12:29:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-to-forums-been-lurking-in-background-too-long.12652/post-171245>

sending you a big Hug Jennie. It is your health, your life, your journey. You can't do it for someone else and no one else can do it for you. JK always tells us ....we can only help ourself and then help others when we are well.

How is Your DH's health? Maybe give him some time? why is he taking such a strong position against living at the ocean? Is it his job?

As far as I can see .... being near the beach the last four years saved my life for so many reasons. I will be moving a little ways away from here in a month or so. We will be a 10 -15 minute drive away. I have said I have to be at the ocean every day ...and luckily - Tony has come to love the ocean as much as I do. He has always loved living here ...but now understands all the healing benefits. Now, when life gets in the way - we drop everything and go to the ocean .... we both have to be there no matter what. It brings such peace and contentment and at the same time - so much positive energy. Tony notices such a huge difference in me and will often say - lets go - you need to be at the ocean.

### Inger — 2015-07-07T16:19:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-to-forums-been-lurking-in-background-too-long.12652/post-171259>

Jennie........    I am so sorry........ I know how you feel
It is so painful.. stuff like that. My heart still is sore from what happened to me..... you know.
But we will heal... time heals.. and love. There is so much love around! We just need to stay open... if people are closed.... there is nothing we can do  except of loving
I cant compromise my health much.... a little and it is enough. I need to stay healthy and get healthier..... if I get broken (and I am pretty weak and sensible believe me.. so I need to take good care) I will only be a burden for this world and I have nothing to give.. how sad is that....
Relationships are not easy....... you need to know what you do and follow your heart always.... if you lose more energy than you gain by going apart... it is not good... only oneself can know what we lose what we gain.... but we need to not be too scared of change either... because my own N=1 is... when I have had to give up something very dear to my heart... I always have gotten something better - when I followed my heart
I just could not see it back then.. but I just jumped... and then I was scared but I did it anyways and then I got a new heaven.. a better one
I hope you get some silent lonely time in nature to figure it out..... walks in the woods, in the wild.... they always clear my mind and I know what to do

Maybe your husband do is scared of you leaving him... I am sure you show him so much love..... always do no mater what.. all of a sudden things can change like magic
to be tough... and to love BIG and be soft at the same time is not easy but I am sure it is the way to go

### fitness@home — 2015-07-07T16:25:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-to-forums-been-lurking-in-background-too-long.12652/post-171262>

> Can't convince him I would be a lot healthier - says he thinks tests would have been same if repeated here rather than Cairns. He won't read any of Jack's stuff - says most of it's nonsense & I could be just as healthy here

Sorry Jenny and I'm also sending hugs.

Similar kind of situation that my DH won't read any of the blogs, doesn't believe that wifi/electronic devices etc are bad for you, and thinks CT will make you fatter (that my body will put on another layer of fat to compensate for the cold), etc...

BUT on the flip side of the coin, I am be getting through to him on some level. He has built me a CT tank, adjusted to having just red lighting on in the evenings and the most recent breakthrough was his change of opinion toward Magnetico mattresses. I've wanted one for a few years and we had the opportunity to picked one up for a good price in May.

In early June he spent four days in the hospital. The mattress arrived while he was there. Once he was home, within 24 hours he had the Magnetico pads on our bed. Couldn't talk him into waiting a few days until he had recovered more. On some level he knew his body needed it.

### SeaHorse — 2015-07-07T22:11:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-to-forums-been-lurking-in-background-too-long.12652/post-171286>

It sounds like you know what you need....what you can't know yet is how everyone around will shift and change as you move towards that knowledge. Keep moving forward towards what is calling you....perhaps others in your life will fall into your slip stream. all the best.

### Jenny S — 2015-07-08T05:33:53+0300

Now I'm messing his life up as well by leaving. He doesn't like the beach - hates sand & when we've been together mostly walks on beach with shoes on. He loves it where we live & his friends & only son live here & wouldn't be happy any where else ..........

### Jenny S — 2015-07-12T05:08:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-to-forums-been-lurking-in-background-too-long.12652/post-171727>

Well I've decided I'm moving to Hervey Bay (Northern Oz beach) for 6 mths trial mid september to see how much better I feel. Hubby not at all happy, still refusing to come & getting more depressed every day. I'm trying not to feel guilty leaving as I know I have to do this for me. He will come & visit & of course & I 'll be back to visit him & family & friends. He wants me to promise I'll move back after 6 mths or less but I can't do that as I don't think it will happen.
I'm trying to keep positive & as all forum friends say you just don't know what is in future so trying not to worry to much. All this reinforces to me how little possessions mean at the end of the day. Obviously having enough food, shelter & money to pay bills is important but health & family mean so much more.

### Jenny S — 2015-09-30T06:04:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-to-forums-been-lurking-in-background-too-long.12652/post-178220>

> [SeaHorse said:](/goto/post?id=171791)
>
> ^^Yes, sounds like the "JennyS" is getting ready to launch!

Well I've launched! DH flown home & left me in Hervey Bay, Qld. He spent 2 weeks up here with me, we went to beach most mornings, me walking in water & sunning myself, him covered from head to toe with shoes on - oh well. Trying to do the long distance marriage thing at moment - he wants me to move back to Bendigo but think he has come to realise I won't be & he says he's not moving so........?? He's still not interested in knowing why even tho he has cancer & blah blah blah.
Must say it's helluva lot easier without him. No TV blaring & glaring at me all night, no bright lights, less mobile phone, more sand & sun.

Spose I better do something about making some friends - no loneliness hit yet but guess it will. Might have to start a beach nudist club - don't think it would go down too well here - mostly retirees or young families.
Think I might need a crochet bikini to let more sun thru - shame I can't crochet!

### caroline — 2015-11-02T04:55:11+0200

### Jenny S — 2015-11-19T04:30:22+0200

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=180954)

> Hi Jenny - How are you doing???

Hi Caroline, battling a bit - still husband. He's not coping at all with me being in Hervey Bay still, he's quite depressed & still getting all the usual didn't think this would happen to him - me moving without him blah blah blah. I've read too much rubbish on the internet & my family & friends are starting to think I've gone mad (so he says)
I'm going home in couple of weeks & be there for a month then coming back up. think it might be a long month. He still won't consider moving - says he would never be happy anywhere else & he'd only make my life & his miserable which is probably right!
I'm thinking of asking Josh (paleo osteo) for a certificate of saneness & stating that I am doing the only thing possible to get healthy & maintain it.
Hope you are still enjoying your new life
Cheers Jenny x

### caroline — 2015-11-19T10:47:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-to-forums-been-lurking-in-background-too-long.12652/post-182316>

A lot of manipulation going on .....so sorry Jenny

When we moved to Australia from Canada - a lot of folks asked us how we could do that etc. etc. - such a big move and weren't we scared and on and on. A close friend who is a therapist wanted to have a long talk to me and did I understand what I was doing and how huge a move it was.

My answer always was ..... It is just geography - wherever my DH is ..... is home. That is all that really mattered to me.

All the new shared experiences are magic.

Please remember to care care of you first and foremost and he has to do the same. To say he wouldn't be happy anywhere else is pretty extreme ....and amazingly boring!
xo
I will pm my phone number - call me when you get back this way. I would imagine it will be so stressful for you.

### yarralea — 2016-03-04T01:21:00+0200

>
> Hi all,
> I've been going to do this for ages, but long time coming.
> I bought Epi-Paleo RX about 6 mths ago - can't remember how I got to it, probably googling health issues.
> Had spiralling health issues for years, - like most people I guess. Lots of little issues didn't think much of at time, such as tiredness, tinnitus, bloating, constipation, dizziness, brain fog - put it all down to stress - even thought I didn't feel stressed. About 10 yrs ago started slowly losing weight & getting tireder & dizzy all time. Still nothing wrong after having gastroscopy & colonoscopy. Told no such thing as gluten intolerance, so kept eating gluten. I'm quite tall & about 2 yrs ago was only 44kgs. Luckily found GAPS diet, as GP just wanted to give me lots of drugs & put me in hospital which I refused - knew there must be more natural way.
> Have been on GAPS for 2 years & a lot better but still not as good as would have liked. Since following Jack's info have improved quite a bit. Find it all a bit much for the brain but after re-reading blogs over & over sometimes if lucky I have a light bulb moment!
> Think biggest improvements have probably come from eating fish by the ton, cutting out blue light at night & less EMF - one of the hardest things I've found to do.
> Finding it difficult being in Australia to sort some information such as water, fish & sun.
> Anyone out there from or near Bendigo? Would love to meet up with fellow Kruse followers as my husband, family & friends all think I'm nuts - don't really care about that, but nice to have someone else to meet & discuss things with!

Hiya Jenny!

I'm in Adelaide, well Burra really, so if you are ever this way, feel free to pop in and say hello.

### Jenny S — 2016-03-16T04:03:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-to-forums-been-lurking-in-background-too-long.12652/post-190948>

Time for an update. Well I've really flown the coop. Have bought a place on Bribie Island, Queensland & moved about 10 days ago.
Finding it a bit tough, don't know anyone, family & friends & husband all back in Victoria. Husband thinks I've really let him down, not coping but won't move. I've decided it's the opposite. He's let me down, not supporting me in quest for health - doesn't even believe I'll be any better up here. I've given up trying to convince him & think it probably for best. Much easier to maintain healthy lifestyle without him undermining it with bright lights, tv, smart meters at our head etc, etc. ( & he's the one with cancer)
It will be hard for a while but know I will be better off in long run. Can't wait to be really healthy. Already noticed less tired (chronic fatigue) even tho not sleeping very well. Obviously stress with moving & separating don't help.
Unfortunately finances aren't same anymore & am going to have to look for a job, but at least it will be nice to be well enough to do so soon I hope.
Not sure what I shall look for - have banking background but last thing I want to do is go back to an office sitting in front of computer & under fake lights all day.
Thanks for your good wishes Audrey & Yarralea. I must ring Haley soon as she is probably only 1 hour drive away, tho won't go that far yet - too tired.
Interesting have noticed I can eat as much chocolate as I like even at night & doesn't affect me at all & nightshades don't worry me anymore. Obviously both good signs.

### Jenny S — 2016-07-15T10:03:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-to-forums-been-lurking-in-background-too-long.12652/post-198386>

Update - my B/C is now 19.8, down from 30.1 from 2 yrs ago, thought it might be be better than that, but suppose I should be grateful that it's going the right way. I can now turn of circuit breakers at night, but unfortunately have wi -fi 24/7 from neighbours which I can't do anything about.
Other than that it is all pretty easy now. Still battling with sleep issues but could be worse. So once again my heartfelt thanks to Jack & everyone else on forum as know I would be very unwell without all this information.
My other great news is I have met a guy who has never heard of Jack but knows about everything except blue light but more than willing to learn - he has just ordered blue blocking glasses, mobile phone covers etc. Same age as me & he has fallen under my spell - whoo hoo!!

### caroline — 2016-07-15T11:35:51+0300

# Thread: Sore Toes & Numbness in Hands
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sore-toes-numbness-in-hands.12661/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### dantothep — 2015-01-21T19:55:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sore-toes-numbness-in-hands.12661/post-153543>

I know this has been covered here and a few other places - [http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/raynaud-thread.4235/](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/raynaud-thread.4235/)

I am three weeks into CT:

week 1 mostly face dunks for 10 mins or so.
week 2 was CT over tight stretchy top (not compression vest though)
week 3 is ice cubs in bags on torso (25 mins) + face dunks AM.

Throughout the weeks I have experienced sore toes, red swollen, throbbing sometimes itchy. Seems like mild frostbite not Reynaulds as I haven't noticed any white patches. I am now getting intermittent numbness in the hands.

Thing is my feet haven't been near the cold. And I am definitely still averse to the cold.

My SHBG was very high last week, could this be related to high levels of Estrogen which is being naturally released as a Ca channel blocker due to my high EMF environment?

# Thread: Seafood and Mercury risk:
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seafood-and-mercury-risk.12663/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2015-01-22T03:33:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seafood-and-mercury-risk.12663/post-153572>

As i have told all my members it is completely a fallacy of modern functional med docs and ancestral folks: Now we have some data points to support DHA.

Research abstract:[http://ajcn.nutrition.org/.../ajcn.114.100503.full.pdf...](http://ajcn.nutrition.org/content/early/2015/01/21/ajcn.114.100503.full.pdf+html?sid=e3cd9ff2-9092-40bb-a56e-4ad825d63fb7)

### Jack Kruse — 2015-01-22T03:34:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seafood-and-mercury-risk.12663/post-153573>

Research from the same cohort:[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3208775/](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3208775/). "For humans, fish is the primary source for some LCPUFA that are essential for neural development. The human body has a very limited ability to synthesize the n-3 LCPUFA docosahexaenoic acid (DHA) and eicosapentanoic acid (EPA), and fish is the primary human dietary source. ... The current findings are reassuring regarding the rewards and risks of fish consumption and indicate that exposure to MeHg during pregnancy from consumption of ocean fish is not adversely associated with neurocognitive and behavioral outcomes measured in late adolescence."

### Jack Kruse — 2015-01-22T03:35:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seafood-and-mercury-risk.12663/post-153574>

[http://medicalxpress.com/news/2015-01-fatty-acids-fish-shield-brain.html](http://medicalxpress.com/news/2015-01-fatty-acids-fish-shield-brain.html)

### PaulG — 2015-01-22T13:26:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seafood-and-mercury-risk.12663/post-153587>

This article dishes the n-3 brain evolution theory and makes some reasonable logical observations
[Has an aquatic diet been necessary for hominin brain evolution and functional development?](http://journals.cambridge.org/download.php?file=%2FBJN%2FBJN96_01%2FS0007114506001711a.pdf&code=9ae8aaa4ddce47a50e528452fc6608c7)

"A number of authors have argued that only an aquatic-based diet can provide the necessary quantity of DHA to support the human brain, and that a
switch to such a diet early in hominin evolution was critical to human brain evolution. This paper identifies the premises behind this hypothesis and
critiques them on the basis of clinical literature. Both tissue levels and certain functions of the developing infant brain are sensitive to extreme
variations in the supply of DHA in artificial feeding, and it can be shown that levels in human milk reflect maternal diet. However, both the
maternal and infant bodies have mechanisms to store and buffer the supply of DHA, so that functional deficits are generally resolved without
compensatory diets. There is no evidence that human diets based on terrestrial food chains with traditional nursing practices fail to provide adequate
levels of DHA or other n-3 fatty acids. Consequently, the hypothesis that DHA has been a limiting resource in human brain evolution must
be considered to be unsupported."

### Martin — 2015-01-22T15:05:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seafood-and-mercury-risk.12663/post-153593>

DHA, baby.

Sharing this with everyone I know.

NSH

>

### Josh — 2015-01-22T22:42:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rbm3-cold-and-neuroplasticity-cold-shock-proteins.12668/post-153626>

> In the healthy adult brain synapses are continuously remodelled through a process of elimination and formation known as structural plasticity[1](http://www.nature.com/nature/journal/vaop/ncurrent/full/nature14142.html#ref1). Reduction in synapse number is a consistent early feature of neurodegenerative diseases[2](http://www.nature.com/nature/journal/vaop/ncurrent/full/nature14142.html#ref2), [3](http://www.nature.com/nature/journal/vaop/ncurrent/full/nature14142.html#ref3), suggesting deficient compensatory mechanisms. Although much is known about toxic processes leading to synaptic dysfunction and loss in these disorders[2](http://www.nature.com/nature/journal/vaop/ncurrent/full/nature14142.html#ref2), [3](http://www.nature.com/nature/journal/vaop/ncurrent/full/nature14142.html#ref3), how synaptic regeneration is affected is unknown. In hibernating mammals, cooling induces loss of synaptic contacts, which are reformed on rewarming, a form of structural plasticity[4](http://www.nature.com/nature/journal/vaop/ncurrent/full/nature14142.html#ref4), [5](http://www.nature.com/nature/journal/vaop/ncurrent/full/nature14142.html#ref5). We have found that similar changes occur in artificially cooled laboratory rodents. Cooling and hibernation also induce a number of cold-shock proteins in the brain, including the RNA binding protein, RBM3 (ref. [6](http://www.nature.com/nature/journal/vaop/ncurrent/full/nature14142.html#ref6)). The relationship of such proteins to structural plasticity is unknown. Here we show that synapse regeneration is impaired in mouse models of neurodegenerative disease, in association with the failure to induce RBM3. In both prion-infected and 5XFAD (Alzheimer-type) mice[7](http://www.nature.com/nature/journal/vaop/ncurrent/full/nature14142.html#ref7), the capacity to regenerate synapses after cooling declined in parallel with the loss of induction of RBM3. Enhanced expression of RBM3 in the hippocampus prevented this deficit and restored the capacity for synapse reassembly after cooling. RBM3 overexpression, achieved either by boosting endogenous levels through hypothermia before the loss of the RBM3 response or by lentiviral delivery, resulted in sustained synaptic protection in 5XFAD mice and throughout the course of prion disease, preventing behavioural deficits and neuronal loss and significantly prolonging survival. In contrast, knockdown of RBM3 exacerbated synapse loss in both models and accelerated disease and prevented the neuroprotective effects of cooling. Thus, deficient synapse regeneration, mediated at least in part by failure of the RBM3 stress response, contributes to synapse loss throughout the course of neurodegenerative disease. The data support enhancing cold-shock pathways as potential protective therapies in neurodegenerative disorders.

[http://www.sciencealert.com/scientists-discover-cold-shock-proteins-for-dementia-treatment](http://www.sciencealert.com/scientists-discover-cold-shock-proteins-for-dementia-treatment)

# Thread: Effectiveness of Laser treatment at acupuncture sites compared to traditional acupuncture in the tre
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/effectiveness-of-laser-treatment-at-acupuncture-sites-compared-to-traditional-acupuncture-in-the-tre.12669/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Penny — 2015-01-23T01:02:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/effectiveness-of-laser-treatment-at-acupuncture-sites-compared-to-traditional-acupuncture-in-the-tre.12669/post-153639>

[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/21095914](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/21095914)

Cardiovascular diseases are the main causes of mortality, not only in Brazil, but around the world. The use of acupuncture as a complementary and alternative treatment for cardiovascular diseases has been suggested for animals as well as human beings. Possible advantages in using acupuncture are the low cost of treatment and low risks of collateral damage from a combination of acupuncture with other medical treatments. The purpose of this study is to assess the effect of traditional acupuncture with needles and laser acupuncture on arterial pressure and peripheral circulation of inferior limbs in patients with circulatory deficiency. Ten acupuncture points were stimulated in 40 individuals, being that 20 were stimulated by Aluminum gallium arsenide (AlGaAs) laser with emission in the red spectral region (650 nm) using an energy density of 2,4 J/cm2, and 20 were stimulated by systemic acupuncture needles. The analysis of peripheral circulation was performed based on the measurement of arterial pressure of the ankle and arms, using sphygmomanometer and Doppler equipment. The variance of the revascularization index of the laser acupuncture group was 0,057, and 0.030 for the needles group with p= 0,006. These results show that only those treated with laser acupuncture exhibit a significant increase in systolic pressure of their lower limbs, with a consequent improvement of the Revascularization Index, suggesting that different stimuli on acupuncture points generate different variations of peripheral resistance of lower limbs. is column.

# Thread: Molecular Hydrogen/H2 - Ultimate Mitochondrial/Redox Compound?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/molecular-hydrogen-h2-ultimate-mitochondrial-redox-compound.12674/>
**Subforum:** Mitochondrial Rx
**Posts:** 45
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### bio-fractal-soul-self — 2015-01-23T08:40:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/molecular-hydrogen-h2-ultimate-mitochondrial-redox-compound.12674/post-153668>

Hey there everyone - hope all is well! Have yet to make an intro post, but will in due time. Anyhow, before I get to doing a bit of work, I wanted to post about something that has been subtly mentioned before, though in different molecular forms and in a different context - hydrogen.

Hydrogen is very interesting. As I continue my journey studying science - and recovering my health - I plan on incorporating a lot more physics and chemistry into my curriculum so I can more readily grasp these things, make _novel_ connections, etc. to help more people. Anyhow, since I've got some work to do, I'm going to just post some studies and what I find interesting, perhaps I can elaborate more later, and begin to ask everyone here for any available input.

**[Molecular hydrogen as a preventive and therapeutic medical gas: initiation, development and potential of hydrogen medicine](http://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0163725814000941)**

"Molecular hydrogen (H2) has been accepted to be an inert and nonfunctional molecule in our body. We have turned this concept by demonstrating that H2 reacts with strong oxidants such as hydroxyl radical in cells, and proposed its potential for preventive and therapeutic applications. H2 has a number of advantages exhibiting extensive effects: H2 rapidly diffuses into tissues and cells, _and it is mild enough neither to disturb metabolic redox reactions nor to affect signaling reactive oxygen species;_ therefore, there should be no or little adverse effects of H2. There are several methods to ingest or consume H2; inhaling H2gas, drinking H2-dissolved water (H2-water), injecting H2-dissolved saline (H2-saline), taking an H2 bath, or dropping H2-saline into the eyes. **The numerous publications on its biological and medical benefits revealed that H2 reduces oxidative stress not only by direct reactions with strong oxidants, but also indirectly by regulating various gene expressions. Moreover, by regulating the gene expressions, H2functions as an anti-inflammatory and anti-apoptotic, and stimulates energy metabolism.** In addition to growing evidence obtained by model animal experiments, extensive clinical examinations were performed or are under investigation. Since most drugs specifically act to their targets, H2 seems to differ from conventional pharmaceutical drugs. Owing to its great efficacy and lack of adverse effects, H2 has promising potential for clinical use against many diseases."
