### bio-fractal-soul-self — 2015-02-07T02:53:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brain-cns-vestibular-issues-working-towards-a-recovery.12766/post-154947>

The Pharmacology of Actoprotectors: Practical Application for Improvement of Mental and Physical Performance

"Our own opinion is that actoprotectors, most logically, should be regarded as synthetic adaptogens having a strong positive influence on physical work capacity. This means that for pharmacological classification convenience, some synthetic adaptogens that significantly enhance physical performance
can be termed “actoprotectors” but that other synthetic adaptogens cannot.

"6. **Actoprotectors’ principal efficacy is independent of extreme conditions** (physical load, stress, hypoxia, ischemia, hyperemia, gravitation overload etc.), which fact suggests their influence on the basic mechanisms of resistance;

7\. Administration of actoprotectors (for example bemitil) can modify specific effects of many pathogenic chemotherapeutic and somatic direction’s drugs. This fact is a strong theoretical basis for co-administration of actoprotectors and pathogenic
therapy agents.

Actoprotectors can be classified into three groups based on their chemical compositions ([Fig. 3](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3762282/figure/F003/)):

1\. Benzimidazole derivatives (bemitil, ethomersol, etc.);

2\. Adamantane derivatives (bromantane, chlodantan, ademol);

3\. Compounds that belong to other chemical classes (thiazoloindole derivatives, 3-hydroxypyridine derivatives, **nicotinic acid derivatives**, 1-oxa-4-aza-2-silacyclanes, **ginseng**, **chitosans**, etc.).

**"The high efficacy of bemitil under the indicated conditions differs substantially from psychostimulants** (phenamine, sydnocarb): in the latter case, the positive influence on work capacity (under normal conditions) diminishes or even becomes a negative effect under hypoxia or over-heating, due to increases in heat production, heat exhaustion, and oxygen consumption.

Numerous clinical studies have confirmed bemitil's positive normal-conditions influence on the physical work capacity of healthy people and patients with asthenic disorders [(Boĭko _et al_., 1986](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3762282/#B012); [Aleksandrovskiĭ _et al_., 1988](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3762282/#B002); [Makarov _et al_., 1997)](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3762282/#B049); **its influence on healthy people under extreme conditions also has been documented**: for example, **high altitudes** [(Shahnazarov and Makhnovskii, 1991](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3762282/#B084); [Oliynyk and Shevchenko, 2009)](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3762282/#B060), heating microclimate [(Pastushenkov and Badyshtov, 1995)](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3762282/#B061) and its combination with **hypoxia** [(Sedov _et al_., 1991](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3762282/#B076), [1993)](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3762282/#B077), and various factors related to long-duration voyages [(Novikov, 1991)](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3762282/#B055) and **space flight** [(Bobkov and Epishkin, 1988)](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3762282/#B010). Our study on high-performance athletes confirmed the positive effects of bemitil on the process and results of training [(Oliynyk, 2009)](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3762282/#B058).

"It has been established that the therapeutic effect of bemitil is a function of its complex mechanism entailing cell genome activation, optimization of mitochondrial oxidation, oxidative stress reduction, and stimulation of cellular immune response [(Shabanov, 2009b)](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3762282/#B083).

"Basically, bemitil (and other benzimidazole actoprotectors) is similar to purine bases in its chemical structure ([Fig. 4](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3762282/figure/F004/)). It was supposed that this structural similarity explains the influence of bemitil on the cell genome, the amplifying expression of RNA and proteins, particularly enzymes of gluconeogenesis and oxidative phosphorylation, as a central link in bemitil’s mechanism of action.

**Bemitil primarily encourages anaerobic energy production, ATP formation, and resynthesis of glucoses from the products of carbohydrates decay (lactate and pyruvate) and from glycerol and amino acids, which mostly occurs in the liver and kidneys. Bemitil promotes the utilization of lactates (one of the main factors in work capacity reduction) under excessive physical loads which process is conjugated with the Cori and glucose-alanine cycles ([Fig. 5](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3762282/figure/F005/)). In these cycles, bemitil neutralizes and eliminates not only lactate but also the nitrogeneous products of decay (ammonia, etc.).** Enhancement of the synthesis of mitochondrial enzymes and the structural proteins of mitochondria effects an increase in energy production and maintenance of a high degree of contingency between oxidation and phosphorylation. Maintenance of a high level of ATP synthesis under oxygen deficiency conditions promotes apparent antihypoxic and antiischemic activity.

"Thus, bemitil is a pharmacological agent of metabolic, non-exhaustive action, which comprises cell genome activation and expression of RNA and proteins, including enzymes and other proteins associated with the immune system. Also occurring is expression of gluconeogenesis enzymes synthesis, which facilitates lactate utilization and carbohydrate resynthesis, which leads in turn to increased physical working capacity. The enhancement of the synthesis of the mitochondrial enzymes and structural proteins of the mitochondria makes possible an increase in energy production and the maintenance of a high degree contingency between oxidation and phosphorylation. Maintenance of high-level ATP synthesis under oxygen deficiency promotes apparent **antihypoxic and antiischemic activity.** As an indirect-action antioxidant, **bemitil enhances biosynthesis of antioxidant enzymes.** The **antimutagenic properties** of bemitil might be important to **genomic protection against mutagenic factors of chemical, physical or biological origin.** Finally, **bemitil increases an organism's stability against the influence of extreme conditions** (heavy physical loads, stresses, hypoxia, hyper- and hypothermia, etc.)."

The extensive paper goes on to outline other Actoprotectors, too.

### bio-fractal-soul-self — 2015-02-15T09:33:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brain-cns-vestibular-issues-working-towards-a-recovery.12766/post-155670>

Seems as though most are unfortunately not paying much attention to the journal..... yet ;-)

Have been tinkering with some things and am hopeful that in the coming month I will make even more progress due to these changes. Have been taking cold showers and keeping my room very cold, as well as taking coolish-cold showers prior to a hot epsom salt and baking soda and vinegar bath followed by cooling off in my cold room once again. Also, it's been extremely cold out as of late, like -40 degrees F. I have been doing short exposures to this with just pants, shoes, shirt, etc. but no coat, hat, gloves or anything, and can actually bare it. Coming back into a warmer home environment (which is still, again, cold) is kind of like a more extreme contrast thermogenesis.

I've also been eating at least one tin of smoked oysters (DHA, taurine, zinc) or sardines (DHA, nucleotides, b-vits, A+D, calcium) a day. Today, I ate two tins of sardines and one tin of oysters in addition to some beef gelatin; I've noticed that my skin, hair, and nails appear better. I used to be worried about excess protein inhibiting ketogenesis and ketone utilization, but I read the blog by Bill Lagakos cited in a recent thread which discussed protein and energy balance and ketosis, and how 'excess' protein won't decrease ketone production unless it is eaten in one or two large lump sums, or if one is fasting and then breaks the fast with a very large, high calorie, high protein meal. I'm going to start testing this with blood and breath ketones, but I suspect this will hold true as long as carbs are 10-30 grams/day, let alone coconut oil and caprylic acid. Was taking 10mg of PQQ + 100mg of Coq10 and 100mg of Shilajit a day for 30 days, and now have begun 20mg of PQQ. Energy seems enhanced, and I remembered what roads my significant other should take to get back home, which should be easy, but for me as of late has been completely confusing, even when it comes to roads frequently traveled. I took it as a good sign. I also received some caprylic acid and plan on staring to ingest 2-3 tablespoons per day at the minimum for enhanced ketone production.

I'm planning on starting transcranial infrared light treatments, too, starting at my prefrontal cortex once per week, then adding in the temporals, them occipital lobes, and progressing to shining light upon my entire brain at each 10-20 EEG spot once every 7-10 days. I'll post updates as I do this.

Also, question: Does anyone here have any info or input on consuming things like fish scraps, organs, heads, etc? I can essentially cut my food bill in half if I start buying whole cod and pollack, but I've spoken to individuals who have actually substituted fish heads or thyroid glands of fish and animals for their thyroid meds, and noticed a pronounced thermogenic effect (increased body temp and heart rate, redness, etc). I'm worried that if I start consuming cod heads frequently, along with adding sea veggies, I may induce hyperthyroidism or something....

### bio-fractal-soul-self — 2015-02-18T08:37:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brain-cns-vestibular-issues-working-towards-a-recovery.12766/post-155950>

I remember some of Dr. Kruse's older posts in which he mentioned the thyroid hormone T2, and how it is actually a potent metabolic stimulant with very interesting properties. Well, found some interesting information, and discussed its use with a biologist friend - I'm thinking of trying it:

**[3,5-diiodo-L-thyronine upregulates rat-liver mitochondrial FoF1-ATP synthase by GA-binding protein/nuclear respiratory factor-2](http://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0005272809002746)**

[3,5-Diiodo--thyronine (T2) has selective thyromimetic effects in vivo and in vitro](http://jme.endocrinology-journals.org/content/19/2/137.full.pdf+html)
[**Short-Term Stimulation of Lipogenesis by 3,5-l-Diiodothyronine in Cultured Rat Hepatocytes
How the thyroid controls metabolism in the rat: different roles for triiodothyronine and diiodothyronines**](http://press.endocrine.org/doi/full/10.1210/en.2005-0345http://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1111/j.1469-7793.1997.529bb.x/full)
From the above abstract: ["Chronic administration of either T2s or T3 to hypothyroid rats significantly enhanced the oxidative capacity of each of the tissues considered. In the case of T2s the stimulation was almost the same whether it was expressed as an increase in specific activity or total tissue activity](http://press.endocrine.org/doi/full/10.1210/en.2005-0345http://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1111/j.1469-7793.1997.529bb.x/full) (...) The effects of T2s are rapid and possibly mediated by their direct interaction with mitochondria."

**[Thyroid hormones, mitochondrial bioenergetics and lipid handling](http://journals.lww.com/co-endocrinology/pages/articleviewer.aspx?year=2010&issue=10000&article=00004&type=abstract)**

There are about 4-5 other citations I have, too. T2 appears to both increase the metabolic rate (which, contrary to popular opinion, increases longevity and intelligence) and fatty acid oxidation rate (sound similar to something else? CT? Ketosis?). It also modulates Nrf2 and ampk. A biologist I speak to claimed that he is familiar with someones work (unpublished data I believe) that shows that T3 is largely just a prehormone to T2, and T2 has tons of pro-metabolic effects. I currently know of no one who has used T2 over a short or long period of time, though... I found this source:

[http://mind-and-muscle.myshopify.com/products/t2](http://mind-and-muscle.myshopify.com/products/t2) T2 Fat Incinerator, developed by Todd Lee, M.D. There is one review, which was great, but that is it. It seems to be a 'high dose', as well. Does anyone know of any other sources? Has anyone used T2? Is the dose in the product I linked extremely high, or am I just too cautious? Also, are fish heads an adequate thyroid hormone surrogate or replacement if not extremely hypothyroid ('subclinical hypothyroidism')?

### bio-fractal-soul-self — 2015-03-23T06:27:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brain-cns-vestibular-issues-working-towards-a-recovery.12766/post-159255>

[http://lib.gen.in/next/MTAuMjMwNy8yODI5MzI1/10.2307\_2829325.pdf](http://lib.gen.in/next/MTAuMjMwNy8yODI5MzI1/10.2307_2829325.pdf) **Energy Metabolism, Brain Size and Longevity in Mammals**

"The mathematical relations between basal energy metabolism, brain size, and life span in mammals have been investigated. The evolutionary level of brain development, or encephalization (c), is a function both of brain weight (E) and of body weight (P) according to c = E/P0O732 **Brain weight was found to be a linear function of the product of encephalization and basal metabolic rate.** The oxygen consumption of the brain (Mbrain) is proportional to both encephalization and body weight according to Mbrain cx cP059. The ratio of metabolic rate in the cerebral cortex to that in the brain as a whole depends solely upon the degree of encephalization and is independent of the size of the animal. **The maximum potentialife span of a mammal was found to be proportional to the product of its degree of encephalization and the reciprocal of its metabolic rate per unit weight.** Life span may be regarded as the algebraic sum of two components: (1) a deduced somatic component (Lb) inversely related to the basal metabolic rate per unit weight, and (2) an encephalization component (Le) related directly to the evolutionary increase of relative brain size."

Reading through the rest of the paper, but it appears to reinforce some previous research I've read that indicates that as basal metabolism increases in an organism, independent of body weight, brain size and cognitive function also increases.

### bio-fractal-soul-self — 2015-03-27T06:20:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brain-cns-vestibular-issues-working-towards-a-recovery.12766/post-159508>

[http://elifesciences.org/content/elife/early/2015/03/11/eLife.06003.full.pdf](http://elifesciences.org/content/elife/early/2015/03/11/eLife.06003.full.pdf) **No turnover in lens lipids for the entire human lifespan**

Apparently, lipid turnover in the human lens is almost non-existent our entire life; sphingomyelin content, particularly sphingomyelin filled with saturated and monounsaturated fat, and cholesterol, remain stable throughout our life, while glycerophospholipids and other ceramide products, particularly those with unsaturated fats, degenerate through the human lifespan.

### ga7777 — 2015-02-01T16:37:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tamiflu-and-neurotransmitters.12768/post-154442>

I don't even know where to start. I have been battling with leaky gut and hormone/neurotransmitter imbalance for 6 years. One if the main symptoms was vertigo. I was low in progesterone at one time and started taking a supplement called vitex to boost my levels. Ironically within just a few days my vertigo was completely gone except just a few tiny episodes here and there. I was happy to keep just taking the vitex. It didn't help with any of my other issues but at least I was functioning at work. Last week I was given Tamiflu for influenza a positive test. I knew after the first dose that I felt "off" but I assumed it was just the fever. By the third day taking it I felt like I was having a mental breakdown. I felt anxiety, panicked, disconnected from my family, numb, vision blurry, disoriented and confused and depressed. After reading some tamiflu reviews...I see this is an issue with the medication. I wish I would have known. Not only am I having these symptoms but now my vertigo is back full force and the vitex isn't helping. Please tell me how to get back feeling normal. I feel very out of sorts and in a dark numb place. I'm scared. I think the tamiflu has messed up my neurotransmitters. I pray that this medication is not able to cross the blood brain barrier and that it is soon taken off the shelf!!! Big pharma is the devil.

I still don't feel like I am connected to my husband...its a very weird thing and difficult to explain. It was the tamiflu though...not lack of sunshine. Although I am missing the sunshine lol

### Sean L. — 2016-03-26T02:48:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tamiflu-and-neurotransmitters.12768/post-191534>

@[ga7777](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/12650/) I know this is an old post but wanted to see how you are doing a year later.. Tamiflu can actually cause serotonin syndrome when your serotonin levels are already elevated. Given your reference to 6 years of neurotransmitter imbalances, this may be the case. Were you on an SSRI around this time? I've experienced serotonin syndrome 3 times, once with Tamiflu, and you are right - it is difficult to describe the state of mind, but is one of the scariest things you can experience mentally. My point in commenting, outside of providing a possible explanation and hopefully some closure to a past experience, is to see how your symptoms are today. Based on my personal experiences, I suspect you may still struggle with symptoms here and there. Recovering from prolonged periods of high serotonin levels can be a roller coaster of an experience and a long, frustrating road. Here is some reading that may help accelerate the process in getting you back to baseline. I would start with research on symptoms of high serotonin and see if these sound familiar (low energy, low sex drive, vertigo, mood changes, gut inflammation, etc). If so, there are ways to reduce these levels and help accelerate your rebalancing of neurotransmitters. We've been conditioned to think that low serotonin is the cause of depression however it's not until I made steps to reduce my chronically high levels of serotonin that I made big strides in my path to healing. Message me if you'd like to discuss further as I've struggled with this issue for a long time and spent far too much time researching and experimenting with everything around this subject.

Reading:

[http://raypeat.com/articles/articles/serotonin-disease-aging-inflammation.shtml](http://raypeat.com/articles/articles/serotonin-disease-aging-inflammation.shtml)
[http://raypeat.com/articles/aging/tryptophan-serotonin-aging.shtml](http://raypeat.com/articles/aging/tryptophan-serotonin-aging.shtml)
[https://raypeatforum.com/community/threads/depleting-serotonin-with-bcaa.2449/](https://raypeatforum.com/community/threads/depleting-serotonin-with-bcaa.2449/)

### Erik — 2015-02-01T23:36:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hell-so-now-body-odor.12770/post-154485>

I never had body odor. Never. I could not take a bath for four days and my armpit could smell like nothing. The last 6 months i re adjusted my diet. Ive been eating raw milk and raw yoghurt, a lot of raw honey, and a lot of slighlty cooked offal (grass fed). I dont know why but eating raw honey almost takes completely away my rosacea. Dont know why....

Well I was doing super great with that. A month ago i went to my cousins in Bs. As. Being there i just ate for four days only raw honey and Yoghurt (but made from pasteurized mik). When i came home i realized my armpits started to stink like rally bad. I dropped Milk and yogurt. Still had some smell. I dropped Honey, and STILL HAVE SMELL. These 2 days i ate only lighlty cooked offal and beef, and some herbal teas with lemon, i am stinking bad. Shit, why does this happen? Once after 4 years that i am slighltly getting over Rosacea, this curse appears.....

Why could it be? the Yoghurt and Honey caused a bacterial infection??? or is something of a liver enzyme that im lacking to digest protein or fat?? please help me, im fucking killing myself if my college starts after February and im still with this smell.....

### Erik — 2015-02-03T21:01:56+0200

BUT these 3 days i took probiotics, applied baking soda after my baths under my armpits and disconnected the Wi-Fi Router and plugged the ethernet cable directly con my CPU. Hi Guys,

So I've started my journey with Cold Thermogenesis and have a few questions. My story: I've been doing cold water facial immersion nightly before bed, and can handle 10+minute sessions and is not uncomfortable at this point. On top of this, I've been taking cold showers daily, starting out warm and finishing up around 3-5 minutes in the coldest setting. It's gotten easier every time and I focus the water on my face, back of neck, and chest.

Having said that, how should I transition into baths? -- Just start doing it once-twice a week?
Secondly, how do you all go about getting the amount of ice necessary to take cold baths?

# Thread: Viral loads
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/viral-loads.12783/>
**Subforum:** Biohacking 101
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### cantweight — 2015-02-03T01:06:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/viral-loads.12783/post-154564>

I have a hard time understanding how to keep a viral load in check. Redox seems an obvious answer but there have to be specifics to this particular shit storm I am just not understanding.

About 5 years ago my kids/family were sick from September through March...non stop bouts of strep, ear infections, colds etc. Constant antibiotics and nebulizer treatments.

In recent years we are almost never ill, I cant remember the last time anyone has taken an antibiotic. However, we seem to constantly fight these feelings of general malaise. Low energy is a chronic issue. Often fevers will appear as well either as nagging low grade or fast and furious higher temps.

We have tested positive for HHV6, CMV, EBV....and other random crap.

What am I missing in the battle to defeat these gnarly things? Is it simply a redox balance game to see who is control? If so, any tips for keeping things in our favor or fighting back when its not? Im just done with this and would like to move the hell on...

### yewwei.tan — 2015-02-03T01:44:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/viral-loads.12783/post-154565>

Heheh, I was just reading a little bit about this topic yesterday , so I have thoughts brewing. But I think I'll have to leave it for later in my day to fully explain a bunch of mechanisms that lead me to think what I currently think.

Winning the "Redox Battle" is right, but that battle includes both the ability to modulate charge on the cell membranes to become either stronger or weaker in response to stimulus. Jack basically gave me a reply to a question on viral entry mechanics to say that high charge on the outer cell membrane can be a way to allow for viral entry --http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/is-it-the-vaccine-or-is-it-something-ancient.11972/#post-144945 . It's gonna get complicated in that regard

What I do know is that the key here is light emission. This study linked to by @[CindyB](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/5731/) contains basically all the details, and I intend to do a proper breakdown in the coming days -- [http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.ph...dy-for-you-comments.12507/page-10#post-153032](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/tensegrity-13-is-live-and-ready-for-you-comments.12507/page-10#post-153032)

The short answer though, is that monochromatic near infrared light in the 1020nm to 1190nm "de-activates" certain viruses. The viruses are then likely cleaned up through autophagic processes, which would more easily detect and target these viruses (due to different biophoton emissions when they are "de-activated").

I think that a well functioning set of mitochondria would be able to uncouple respiration as needed (requires higher delta-psi), and emit the necessary monochromatic IR light to cause this "viral deactivation".

Introduction of that IR light directly via something like IR lasers will have the same effect. Using un-coherent light will not work as well, but I suspect that it will still have a positive effect.

So what I currently think is the best treatment is to introduce as much of this near IR band of heat to the cells as possible, either as direct treatment using a laser, or by enhancing cellular uncoupling as much as possible.

If I were to come down with a virus:

-   CT with fournier effect. (Contrast Showers are my personal go-to). Both the hot and cold stimulate uncoupling.
-   2mg Nicotine + high dose Bitter Melon, both of which are known uncouplers

Any other herbal additions may also be helpful, depending on in which tissues the virus is located

Hopefully I'll get to more stuff later

....

### yewwei.tan — 2015-02-03T02:08:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/viral-loads.12783/post-154569>

> [Josh (Paleo Osteo) said:](/goto/post?id=154566)

> nicotine gum??

Yeah, that's my preferred method for getting a quick dose directly into the blood. I chew vigorously (as opposed to the package instructions ), and that seems to get the effects more quickly.

Only precaution is to not get a gum with phenylalanine. I just use the nicorette 2mg pieces.

> [Josh (Paleo Osteo) said:](/goto/post?id=154568)
>
> so stomach flu
>
> high fat electron dense epipaleo diet, cold RO water
> fournier effect CT
> bittermelon or nicotine...

> hmm nice ideas

And go get a red-laser helmet as well

Bitter Melon can also apparent give some people hypoglycemia-like symptoms. I'm personally experienced some headiness when trying to dose bitter melon via naturally brewed soup/tea (buy bitter melon, boil it down in concentrate stock), but when I checked my blood glucose levels, it was normal enough (slightly less than 5mmol/L or 90mg/dL).

I don't get that reaction with crushed up supplemental bitter melon extract pills. Example, right now I'd crush up 4 of these pills -- [http://www.iherb.com/Himalaya-Herbal-Healthcare-Bitter-Melon-60-Caplets/3690](http://www.iherb.com/Himalaya-Herbal-Healthcare-Bitter-Melon-60-Caplets/3690)

From an traditional Chinese perspective, I've always been told while growing up that Bitter Melon (we call it Bitter Gourd) was supposed to ward away the flu. I'll eat it any time though  ....

### cantweight — 2015-02-03T03:49:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/viral-loads.12783/post-154572>

@[yewwei.tan](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/6824/)
Thank you so much for the input it is appreciated.

Bitter melon and nicotine make sense. Safe to assume they are ok for children? They are older 9, 12, 14 and all near or over 100lbs.

My knowledge of light is very limited. Right now I have the ct tank set up with an infrared light over it. It is a giant 250 watt clear heat lamp that one would use for a chicken coop....having bought an older home with a farm it was one of the things we found in the basement. We also have the very 70's red heat lamps in our bathrooms. Aaaaand we have 2 large grow light units for plants "full spectrum" tube lights. Are any of those what you are talking about????

They are also on high fat low carb dha rich diet...

Very safe assumption we are dealing with mitochondrially defunct humans but they have come a long long way from where they were.....arthritis, migraines, motor and vocal tics.....GONE

### yewwei.tan — 2015-02-03T04:43:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/viral-loads.12783/post-154576>

> [cantweight said:](/goto/post?id=154572)
>
> @[yewwei.tan](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/6824/)
> Thank you so much for the input it is appreciated.
>
> Bitter melon and nicotine make sense. Safe to assume they are ok for children? They are older 9, 12, 14 and all near or over 100lbs.
>
>
> My knowledge of light is very limited. Right now I have the ct tank set up with an infrared light over it. It is a giant 250 watt clear heat lamp that one would use for a chicken coop....having bought an older home with a farm it was one of the things we found in the basement. We also have the very 70's red heat lamps in our bathrooms. Aaaaand we have 2 large grow light units for plants "full spectrum" tube lights. Are any of those what you are talking about????
>
> They are also on high fat low carb dha rich diet...
>
> Very safe assumption we are dealing with mitochondrially defunct humans but they have come a long long way from where they were.....arthritis, migraines, motor and vocal tics.....GONE

Bitter Melon I would say is probably OK for kids. Well, at least I know that us Chinese feed the whole fruit to kids . The extract I linked above is presumably just dried, powdered, and compressed bitter melon fruit and stems, so it should be decently close to the real thing. Dosing will probably have to be adjusted accordingly, but I think this is a pretty natural remedy with not many side effects.

Obviously, test on yourself before attempting with kids.

\----

The nicotine I will advise against dosing to children if possible.

You will see very significant effects with 1mg of nicotine in adults. I think the uncoupling effects of nicotine will continue to be prevalent in any human, and nicotine works through specific receptors on adipose tissue.

The issue that I have with making recommendations to children is the addictive potential of nicotine. With adults, I can safely recommend up to 4mg of nicotine a day for up to 14 days without any addictive properties. Taking a break for 7 days after that will negate any addictive potential. I have no clue where the addiction threshold stands with kids.

Some adults even report feelings of dizziness with high dose nicotine. Personally, 2mg doesn't really give me any issues, but a 4mg dose gets me pretty high .

Some random notes regarding nicotine on John Kiefer's nicotine book -- [http://tanyewwei.com/notes/kiefer-nicotine](http://tanyewwei.com/notes/kiefer-nicotine) (there is a bias towards athletic performance in this book)

One thing that I will note in there is that nicotine can cause a transient triglyceride dump. This is fine so long as your body is great at using free-fatty acids. I wouldn't eat carbs together with nicotine though.

\----

I think the heat lamps are great so long as you can ensure that the nn-EMF risk is mitigated.

....

# Thread: Sad News..
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sad-news.12784/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### PaulG — 2015-02-03T06:05:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sad-news.12784/post-154577>

Very sad news from Alison Heath organiser of [http://www.takebackyourpower.net/](http://www.takebackyourpower.net/). Her websites are [http://longevityrescuer.com/](http://longevityrescuer.com/) and [https://www.yourvibranthealthsecrets.com/index.php](https://www.yourvibranthealthsecrets.com/index.php) and interview on her EMF Sensitivity with is [here](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=jAMgABUssGQ)

Dear Paul,

It is with deepest sorrow that I relay the loss of my beloved life partner.

He had become highly sensitive to electromagnetic radiation while working in
various locations across the country and felt he was being zapped whenever he
was exposed to WiFi. Together these past few months we worked diligently on
shielding our living space and had just ordered a BEMER.

He was regaining his strength by grounding barefoot on the beach, eating
superfoods drinking green juices and organic smoothies, rebounding, etc.

A complete examination of this heart several months ago including a 72 hour heart
monitoring indicated his heart was fine. However he suffered a cardiac arrest in the
presence of two paramedic teams and was without a pulse for nearly 30 minutes
before they were able to resuscitate him.

Within 24 hours it became apparent he had suffered a massive brain infarct and he
was declared brain dead.

A form of consciousness remained as his heart did beat stronger when he was
surrounded with loved ones in the hospital. I was able to sleep in his arms in the
intensive care unit on the last day of his life.

In death as in life, he was able to be of service to others donating his liver,
kidneys and pancreas to give the gift of life.

I will be taking some time off to recover from this shock.

The grand finale for the complete season of the Best of the Best of Your Vibrant
Health Secrets will air at a later date.

Those of you subscribed to the EMF summit can expect more to come. My
dedication to get this information on protecting ourselves has been renewed by this
tragic loss.

I will also keep the special price of $77 for the complete bundle of these
18 life-saving interviews available for a while longer. Please get informed and
share the information with others.

As always, sending you loads of love and great energy.
This time I request some of your love and healing energy back.

Alison Heath

host of the EMF summit and Your Vibrant Health Secrets

Your Vibrant Health Secrets

P O Box 20082
Kelowna, BC
V1Y 9H2
CA

[info@yourvibranthealthsecrets.com](mailto:info@yourvibranthealthsecrets.com)
250-769-8804

### MIKE WILKINSON — 2015-02-03T08:39:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mike-from-melbourne.12789/post-154582>

Hi all,
1st time poster so hopefully I am following the correct protocol here
I only found out about this website when visiting Josh Lamaro down in Torquay
Josh is very knowledgeable and good bloke very caring
A bit of a background on Mike from Melbourne
Well here we go
First started being concerned about my health many years ago with with a really bad fatigue mainly affecting me after sports
symptoms like elevated heart rate sinuses that I never had before ever and had to stop doing exercise needed lots of sleep etc wasn't probably the classic "fatigue symptoms no aches but something was definitely wrong
Even today I have to be very careful with how much exercise I do
Recently diagnosed with Parkinsons and on a small amount of Sifrol
Main symptoms from the Parkinson's down my right side with the tremor in my arm but the thing that's really quite debilitating at times is my walking movement with my right leg
Really giving me the shits
I'm still very active and working full time
Love to surf when I can and renovating the house
Couple of months ago I found out about the Paleo diet and the effect on autoimmune diseases
Been Paleo for four weeks now and strictly off Gluten
Body seems to be detoxing with my sinuses going rampant off the Gluten
Now reading the auto immune solution by Amy Myers and interested in her program
Before I jump on any program I want to get professional guidance run some blood tests etc
That's about it there's other stuff but that's the main points
Other than that fit and healthy
I figure can't wait for conventional medicine to do its thing with doctors just treating the symptoms drugs profits drugs
Not expecting miracles
Pretty sure I'm in good hands now with Josh getting some baseline markers on my health looking forward to delving further into The mountain of information from Jack Kruse
Happy and Healthy
Mike

# Thread: Dan from Florida
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dan-from-florida.12796/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Danny — 2015-02-03T18:05:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dan-from-florida.12796/post-154626>

Hi Everyone. Firstly, I feel very fortunate to have found Dr. Kruse and his blog.

I am 39 and have had digestive issues my whole life. I was never breastfed, started on grain cereal around 3 months old, and grew up on heavily processed box food, fast food, and microwave dinners. Ever since I could remember, from around 6 years of age, I knew something was wrong w/ my digestive system. Was a vegetarian in college and lived on beer and pasta. On antibiotics for weeks and months for skin issues when in my early 20's. I could go on and on.

My list of symptoms have included the gamut of digestive ills, back/joint pain, brain fog, fatigue, depression, underweight.

I've tried many many diets and supplements w/ little or no success.

Was already paleo ketogenic before finding Kruse's site back in Nov. 2014. Started the leptin Rx and adding seafood to my diet right away, at the end of November. I was finally, after years of horrible sleep, getting restful sleep. I realized, through Kruse's writings, that dairy and nightshades were not my friends and dropped them like a bad habit in the beginning of 2015. I am in the process now of mitigating my nnEMF exposure as much as possible, while finding ways to ground myself. My digestive symptoms continue, but seem to be improving since cutting dairy and nightshades (strawberries and blueberries included). I am very metabolically damaged and hope I can turn this around.

I just finished Epi-Paleo Rx for the first time and will start re-reading in the next day or two. I feel so fortunate to have found Dr. Kruse and his site!

### fitness@home — 2015-02-03T18:29:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dan-from-florida.12796/post-154631>

Hi Danny, glad to see you here

### nonchalant — 2015-02-03T18:30:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dan-from-florida.12796/post-154632>

Welcome, Danny. You're getting more sunshine and good water, right?

### nicld — 2015-02-03T21:03:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dan-from-florida.12796/post-154637>

Welcome Danny. You have come to the right place.

### Danny — 2015-02-03T21:34:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dan-from-florida.12796/post-154641>

Wow! Thank you for the community welcome. It is much appreciated and much needed.

# Thread: Journal for my girlfriend
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/journal-for-my-girlfriend.12797/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 27
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### sebbie — 2015-02-04T00:33:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/journal-for-my-girlfriend.12797/post-154657>

I have my own health issues, but they seem to be under control for now (maybe I'll start a journal some other time).. I'm actually a gold member, not sure how to link the forum account.

My girlfriend on the other side is having some more time sensitive issues. I'm the one doing all the research on this site and trying to help her so I'll start a journal for her. Probably not a good idea to give her more work at the moment anyway.

We live in a rural area and there have always been issues, but right now she is dealing with some increased anxiety and sleep loss as she is doing an internship in the city.

**Background:**

Age is now 27
Emotional stress during childhood, seizures (only during childhood)

First menstruation around 17, irregular cycles
Lost her menstruation for a couple years, got it back 4 years ago (after quitting university)
Since then, cycles have been happening, but they have lasted 40+ days.
Cycles have been shortening progressively down to 32 days recently.

Vegan until about age 25

Generally easily stressed out, has a hard time working more than 3 days a week.

Has symptoms of estrogen dominance:
\-

-   Irregular or otherwise abnormal menstrual cycles
-   Bloating
-   Weight gain in the abdomen and hips
-   Cold hands/feet
-   Trouble sleeping/insomnia
-   PMS

This past year she also had times when she would wake up at night , not know where she is, and start convulsing (her awareness was still there)

**Current situation:
**
As said above, she temporarily moved to the city 3 weeks ago to do an internship for 2-3 months. She had another internship last fall and had major sleep issues for 8 weeks. She brought those sleep issues back home duing the 2 months in between the two internships, but it got a bit better.

I was with her for her first week and put her on the leptin reset, early dinner, avoid lights after dark, getting started with CT, etc. She has been taking magnesium malate before bed too. Her sleep was good during that week.

Then her sleep issues came back the day I left. She sometimes has good nights, but then something will stress her out and she loses her sleep. Last night she took 750mg of Mg, 500mg of theanine and still only slept for 2 hours.

Her heart is pounding and her mind cant stop. It can happen before bed, or when she wakes up in the middle of the night.

So theanine hasn't done it. Melatonin didn't help too much either. Things like acupuncture, chinese herbal teas, have helped but are also hit or miss.

I think I will get her to do labs through ZRT, but this is unlikely to happen before the second half of March. So I am looking for some help to get her sleep back in the meantime.

I just ordered some new supplements:

\- Ubiquinol
\- Vit B complex
\- D-ribose
\- Phosphatidylserine
\- Acetyl-L-Carnitine

I was thinking of starting with Ubiquinol and Vit B because they seem like they would help and not be a risk of making her more anxious (she is generally sensitive - didn't really like how theanine made her feel the first time)

Then maybe try D-ribose in the AM on a day off and see how it makes her feel.

Then L-carnitine on another day off.

Phosphatidylserine seems like the one that could potentially calm her off on an anxious evening. At the same time, it seems pretty potent and I don't know about having her try it on her own. I might have her try it when I go back there next week. She also has a tendancy to pop 2 pills when I tell her to take one only. Did that with theanine and melatonin.

Would love some guidance! Thanks

### Jack Kruse — 2015-02-04T01:38:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/journal-for-my-girlfriend.12797/post-154660>

welcome and I love the fact that you care so much that your journaling for her............that is brilliant.

### sebbie — 2015-02-04T01:52:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/journal-for-my-girlfriend.12797/post-154666>

Thanks Jack.

I forgot to mention one thing: bad cramps on the first day of her menstruation. She usually has to stay home that day.

### nonchalant — 2015-02-04T02:01:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/journal-for-my-girlfriend.12797/post-154668>

Welcome sebbie. She follows the leptin reset protocol when you are with her, with good results. What does she do when she is in the city? Does she continue with it? She needs lots more protection when she is in the city.

When I visit the city now, it takes me a couple days to recover. And I'm pretty careful when I'm there -- not drinking city water, grounding myself, getting sunshine, etc.

I can tell I'm a bit low on energy by my shivering, or lack of it actually. When I'm really low on energy, I can't self-regulate my temperature very well at all.

### sebbie — 2015-02-04T02:20:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/journal-for-my-girlfriend.12797/post-154672>

To clarify, I visited her in the city for a week and that's when she started the leptin reset. Good sleep for a week, except for the night before I left... Also I stressed her out one evening when I came out of the cold tub shivering from CT. I have to be careful with that.

She is continuing the leptin reset in the city. I agree the environmnetal stress is higher there (as well as work stress), and she has fewer opportunities to ground etc. Going into nature and in the sun is something she will naturally seek, but she has less time and opportunities over there

She is also generally cold especially at night; my presence helps as we were too hot during my stay. I should remind her to get a hot water bottle.

### JanSz — 2015-02-04T03:40:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/journal-for-my-girlfriend.12797/post-154679>

### sebbie — 2015-02-04T04:06:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/journal-for-my-girlfriend.12797/post-154682>

Thanks - very relevant link that will be even more relevant when we do labs in a few weeks...

Definitely think there some pregnelonone steal going on

Interestingly, she has more energy in the first part of her cycle. Even though progesteone is supposed to be higher in the second part. Also she had some PMS about 7 days before menstruation.

We thought it was because of progesterone, but maybe it's because of estradiol? (or because of a low pg:e2 ratio) ?

If it is recommended to do the labs on day 21, should she adjust for her longer cycles? We've been tracking basal body temperature to detect her ovulation day for a while now

### JanSz — 2015-02-04T16:56:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/journal-for-my-girlfriend.12797/post-154721>

> [sebbie said:](/goto/post?id=154682)

You are sayng things like this:

Cycles have been shortening progressively down to 32 days recently.
\-----------------------
Then you are having problem figuring day for likely maximum progesterone level:
\-----------------------

\-----------------------
It is a long project.
Set up rules.
Consider doing doing major bloodworks on days 2-4.
Much easier to define time of blood draw.
As far as progesterone goes, you are covering about 50% time of menstrual cycle, instead just of couple days of max progesterone.

///

load her diet with marine derived foods (fish,seafood, algae...). I just happened to start my sister's cycle first time in her life (almost 21 now) with this approach plus some very mild CT (just short dips). ### sebbie — 2015-02-17T21:00:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/journal-for-my-girlfriend.12797/post-155927>

day 27 (approx) of cycle yesterday: more bloating, drinking lots but dehydrated, anger, high cortisol evening and insomnia... Seems like an estrogen fest.

Also dealing with some constipation from the epi-paleo diet. More magnesium? How do we fix dehydration?

We aren't sure if she actually ovulated yet. Morning temperature may or may not have shifted 2-3 days ago.

### sebbie — 2015-03-05T18:37:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/journal-for-my-girlfriend.12797/post-157537>

Update: she finally got her period on day 43. Not too unusual that it took a bit longer than normal since she is dealing with more stress than usual.

She also developed some sort of rash (urticaria) all over her body in the past few days... Wondering if it's some allergic reaction or some die off or a result of Jack's prescription...

From the CT protocol:

> If you develop **cold urticaria at this time,** this is a sign you have high levels of tissue and serum omega six content or your body has lost energy from EMF/artificial light due to dehydration. Stop the experiment and adjust your technology exposure and alter your diet to an [Epi-paleo Rx](http://www.jackkruse.com/brain-gut-6-epi-paleo-rx/), or until you have a blood omega six to three ratio that is below 10 to 1.

Is cold urticaria supposed to happen immediately, or can it peak 3 days after a cold bath?

### sebbie — 2015-03-07T18:33:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/journal-for-my-girlfriend.12797/post-157746>

From reading the die-off thread I would guess the rashes are die-off from losing fat...

On the plus side, her menstruation has gone a lot easier. First menstruation since starting the protocols, and first time she doesn't have major debilitating cramps!

Also her sleep is improving. Recently she has a couple nights where she didn't even have to get up in the middle of the night to go to the bathroom.

### sebbie — 2015-09-05T19:47:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/journal-for-my-girlfriend.12797/post-176552>

OK so we finally got some labs done....

Day 5 of her cycle, blood serum

INR 0.9 (ref 0.9-1.1)
FSH 6.3 IU/L (3.5-12.5)
LH 7.2 IU/L (2.4-12.6)
Estradiol 130 pmol/L (46-608)
Progesterone 2.5 nmol/L (0.6-4.7)
DHEA-S 8.4 umol/L (2.7-8.6)
Testosterone 1.1 nmol/L (0.2-2.9)
Bioavailable T 0.3 (<=1.0)
SHBG 67.2 nmol/L (26-110)
Urine Albumin 2 mg/L (<=20)
Urine ACR (Albumin/Creatin ratio) 1.1mg/mmol (<=2.8)

### sebbie — 2015-09-05T19:54:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/journal-for-my-girlfriend.12797/post-176553>

So everything is within range... What's a good PG:E ratio during the follicular phase? Hers is around 19.

I guess E, Pg, T could be a bit higher

DHEA looks very nice

SHBG could be lower

### sebbie — 2015-09-05T19:56:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/journal-for-my-girlfriend.12797/post-176554>

I'm thinking we need a few more tests but that's all we got through the local lab. Also she has a hard time getting blood out and only got 1.5 tube out of 8 (!)

Might be nice to get luteal phase values as well, but I think she doesn't want to get her blood drawn. Might go with ZRT...

### sebbie — 2015-09-05T20:00:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/journal-for-my-girlfriend.12797/post-176555>

Overall things are improving since the Spring. Cycles are 32 days long. Menstruation cramps are no t as bad. Sleep is decent, insomnia still happens but less often.

I think she lost an inch+ in the waist. No more bloating.

Mood is better overall. Things get hard a couple times every cycle.

She spends 10-15 minutes in the 15 degree C ocean head to toe, no problem.

# Thread: The first of many blogs that tackle bio hacking your Redox.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-first-of-many-blogs-that-tackle-bio-hacking-your-redox.12798/>
**Subforum:** Redox Rx
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2015-02-04T01:37:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-first-of-many-blogs-that-tackle-bio-hacking-your-redox.12798/post-154659>

There was a young medical student in my Q & A after my talk. Here is my debt repaid as promised. [http://jackkruse.com/redox-rx-2-biohacking-mri/](http://jackkruse.com/redox-rx-2-biohacking-mri/)

### Jack Kruse — 2015-02-04T23:39:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-first-of-many-blogs-that-tackle-bio-hacking-your-redox.12798/post-154746>

Hydrogen is a chemists conundrum, a biologists enigma, and physicists dream because it can lose or gain this single electron. I have always been of the belief that hydrogen did not really belong to any group in the periodic table based upon this ability. After many thoughts on this topic, I realized under some environments it can be placed into group 7 or group one in the periodic table. All known elements of group 7 are halogens. The group 1 elements compromise the alkali metals. Hydrogen is often placed in group one of the periodic table by convention due to its electron configuration, but it is not considered by many to be an alkali metal. Why?

Hydrogen rarely exhibits behavior comparable to that of the alkali metals. For example, all the alkali metals react with water, with the heavier alkali metals reacting more vigorously than the lighter ones. The word “alkali” received its name from the Arabic word “al qali,” meaning “from ashes”. These particular elements were given the name “alkali” because they react with water to form hydroxide ions, creating very basic solutions (with pH > 7), which are also called alkaline solutions. Hydrogen forms water with oxygen directly and does not form a basic solution. Adding more hydrogen to it does not cause a special reaction at all, as it does with the other metals in group 1. Why is hydrogen fundamentally different?

Water is most famous for forming hydrogen bonds with other water molecules and with other ions dissolved in it. A hydrogen bond consists of a hydrogen shared between two electronegative atoms like oxygen or sulfur. The compound that donates the hydrogen to the chemical reaction is the hydrogen donor, and the acceptor atoms is the hydrogen acceptor. **Water is unique because it can be both an acceptor and a donor of hydrogen.** It means water can be a switch hitter in many biochemical reactions. This is why water is the universal solvent on Earth.

Hydrogen is the only thing in nature that mitigate the hydroxyl free radical.

### BTA — 2015-02-04T05:54:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/life-after-death-quantum-theory.12801/post-154685>

[Scientists Claim That Quantum Theory Proves Consciousness Moves To Another Universe At Death](http://higherperspective.com/2015/01/consciousness-death.html)

How Life and Consciousness Are the Keys to Understanding the Nature of the Universe
"In one universe, the body can be dead. And in another it continues to exist, absorbing consciousness which migrated into this universe. This means that a dead person while traveling through the same tunnel ends up not in hell or in heaven, but in a similar world he or she once inhabited, but this time alive. And so on, infinitely. It’s almost like a cosmic Russian doll afterlife effect."

[http://higherperspective.com/2015/01/consciousness-death.html#sQpqtmeO9xKmWcZD.99](http://higherperspective.com/2015/01/consciousness-death.html#sQpqtmeO9xKmWcZD.99)

.
.
Just to think there could be an infinity number "_you_" out there in parallel univeries?!?!

# Thread: Leaky Gut -My positivity is coming back
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leaky-gut-my-positivity-is-coming-back.12814/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 175
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Danny — 2015-02-04T19:13:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leaky-gut-my-positivity-is-coming-back.12814/post-154731>

If you ask almost anyone they'd say it doesn't get much more positive than Danny. Well, if you asked anyone 6 to 7+ years ago that is. I thought I was going to solve the world's problems (world hunger and cure cancer) and that attitude remained w/ me until I hit 33/34. I've dealt w/ digestive problems my whole life and it had finally caught up to me. I slowly saw everything slip away and along with it my positivity.

My pertinent health history:

\-Never breastfed / started on cereal at 3 months of age.
\-Raised on heavily processed microwave tv dinners, junk food, and fast food.
\-Physically and mentally abused by step parent.
\-On and off antibiotics for skin issues during teens and early 20's.
\-Vegetarian from the ages of 20 to 25. Ate lots of pasta, beer, dairy.
\-27 years old - I couldn't ignore my digestive issues any further. It was clear that I had a problem so I cut most processed food out of my life, cut back on sugar and alcohol. Had mixed results, but seemed to improve a little.
\-33/34 - Horrible back and sciatic pains, joint pains, brain fog, fatigue. Went on the good old willy nilly supplement train for about two years never really determining what helped and what didn't.
\-35 - Went gluten free. Better, but pains and digestive symptoms persisted.
\-36/37 - Adopted Weston A Price / raw primal diet style of eating. Definitely noticed some improvements, but digestive system and joint pains were still there. (Was still eating dairy, nightshades, lots of carbs, and not eating fish)
38 - Started experimenting w/ paleo ketogenic diet (still doing some WAP/RAW). I had a 6 month learning curve. I just couldn't seem to get results. After the learning curve I felt like I was getting somewhere however things were still off. My digestive symptoms seemed to persist though improving a little, my joint pains improved by about 60 to 70%, but I was still fatigued and still had a leaky gut and still depressed and my positivity was no more. My wife told me it seemed as though I was giving up the fight. I definitely did not want to give up on my family, but I think she was right.
39 - I found Dr. Kruse's website and in some ways feel like it was in the nick of time. I'm 2 months into the leptin Rx and my sleep has improved greatly. For this alone I am so thankful. I cut nightshades out (including blueberries and strawberries), cut dairy and eggs out, cut nuts and seeds out. I'm trying to mitigate my nnEMF although there's still a lot for me to learn. I need to become a paying member so I can listen to the EMF webinar. I am experimenting w/ CT. I know Jack says it's vital for those w/ autoimmune conditions so I will give it a go. I am being a little cautious however, b/c I am thin and sometimes feel pretty weak.

I feel like I am getting there, slowly. I still feel like a prisoner to my leaky gut, but my positive attitude is starting to peak through again. I've read Epi-Paleo Rx once and am about to start reading it again. I'm reading Jack's blog posts at every opportunity. I have an appointment with a nutritional doctor to discuss blood panels.

Current health issues:
\-Leaky gut. Getting better, but my stomach is very sensitive in the morning. BAB sometimes gives me a stomachache. Also, my diet is very strict and if I stray a bit I get stomachaches
\-Underweight and feel malnourished (6 ft. 1 in. - 153lbs)
\-Still slightly depressed.
\-Dry skin especially when having stomachaches.
\-My joint pains and sleep have both improved tremendously thanks to Jack's info!

Current Situation:
\-I live in a rural area. I don't think I'm exposed to many sources of nnEMF outside of the home.
\-I shut off the breaker to the bedrooms and turn off the wi-fi at night.
\-I'm 2 months into the leptin reset - so thankful for finding this info.
\-Experimenting w/ CT, but a little reticent as I am so thin. Trying to eat more fat, but sometimes tough w/ leaky gut.
\-I am taking a DHA supplement w/ iodine as a temporary boost.
\-I am eating about 4 dozen raw oysters along w/ 4 to 6 seafood meals, and few offal meals a week. I thought I was getting stomachaches from shellfish, but I think this is just when I eat in the morning.
I will try and go lighter on my meals in the morning and hope it doesn't mess w/ my leptin sensitivity reset. A krill oil supplement I took gave me a stomachache and general feeling of being "off".
\-I do carpentry and farm work. I try and do my light work in the morning and save the heavy stuff for the afternoon. I don't do any heavy jobs anymore as I no longer have the energy anyway. I can only manage working part-time, but it's getting better.
\-I am meeting w/ a D.C./nutritionist to discuss blood panels and will hopefully have some results to show soon.

### yewwei.tan — 2015-02-05T00:04:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leaky-gut-my-positivity-is-coming-back.12814/post-154748>

Welcome , I had basically the same disease profile (until I moved), and I'll be following along.

Some random notes:

\----

Not being breastfed along with having lots of processed food definitely isn't a good setup. But you're doing things right, so let's see where you need to do

\----

Living in a Rural Area usually is associated with lower nn-EMF levels. Sometimes it can be opposite. Examples:

\- More powerful cell towers to cover a wider area
\- More high-voltage power lines
\- Transmitting devices like cell phones emitting more power due to decreased signal.

Though I would say, that if you scout out your area and find very few towers or powerlines, and you are minding of your cell phone use, then things should be better than in an urban area.

\----

Obviously I'd tell you turn off the WiFi completely all the time

\----

CT is going to be tough given low body fat and poor skin quality.

When dumped into the cold, the body either has to increase uncoupling by liberating body fat as free heat (you don't have lots of fat), or to shiver. Shivering can make CT really difficult, especially when you're not healthy

Where the skin is relevant is that skin cells with good redox potential (high charge over their membrane) likely "keeps internal body heat in" while still allowing for surface cooling (skin has many layers too).

You should see cold tolerance improve as skin quality improves, and skin quality is directly tied to the quality of the GALT tissues in your gut.

Personally, I think you're doing many things right. In your case, I would work the CT protocol slightly differently. I would not bother with full-body immersion or cold showers, because it's really going to be difficult, and possibly take away from your motivation to do other things that are more helpful for your specific disease.

(If you were say someone who had some major neurological issue, immersion CT would have a much bigger bang-for-its-buck)

I would:

-   Only do cold face dunks
-   Drink lots of ice water (direct CT to the gut )
-   Simply try to wear less clothing in the cold

You can start water-immersion CT when you start to see your skin quality improve.

\----

You should consider intermittent fasting for your gut. (this is relevant to the H2 threads that Dr Kruse mentioned above)

I'm going to assume that you're truly Leptin Resistant, and judging by being underweight, I think that's decent assumption -- [http://jackkruse.com/the-leptin-rx-faqs/](http://jackkruse.com/the-leptin-rx-faqs/) .

You're not that underweight though, and don't display neurological or psychological issues related to eating, so I'm expecting a quick recovery (your slight depression is a side effect of a bad gut not producing enough neutrotransmitters, and not a fundamental brain issue per se)

That makes implementing intermittent fasting a little more difficult, since it would mean skipping dinner. You can try that, basically eating only the Leptin Reset breakfast, and then doing lunch 5-6 hours after breakfast.

As you recover, you can and should start to skip breakfast, and start shifting to a lunch and dinner eating pattern. This is because protein synthesis is higher during the dinner time window, and you want to take advantage of putting some protein there.

\----

Need to know your exposure to artificial light. While such visible light is in the native EMF wavelength bands, the intensity that you can shine a light bulb into your nights at night make this a non-native intensity.

Any strong light exposure at night? And if so, what is the current strategy to mitigate that?

\-----

Last thing, how are your sleep and wake times?

\====

That's it for now. Good luck

....

First of all, thank you so much for taking the time to write out this info Yewwei! I will heed your advice about the CT. I am definitely noticing higher tolerance to cold temps. I am in Florida, however, so the cold is relatively mild compared to most on the forum. I believe you are right about intermittent fasting. It is an absolute must for me at this point. I've tried it a little before, but I believe I will be smarter in how I go about it now. I will try your suggestion of skipping dinner while doing the leptin Rx. I'm a little leery, b/c, as you mentioned, 4 to 6 PM is the best time for protein synthesis (probably closer to 4 in the winter and 6 in the summer).
My exposure to artificial light is very minimal at night. I really am trying to be regimented w/ the basics. I turn down lights immediately after sunset and only candle light an hour later. I've only needed to have lights on a few times later than 1 hr after sundown and I've worn blue blockers then.
My sleep is much improved after starting the leptin Rx. I fall asleep b/t 830 to 10 and most nights it's b/t 9 and 930. I wake up b/t 530 and 630. I have only been slightly tired when waking once or twice. Other than that I'm now ready to go in the morning and I don't fade in the afternoon. This is a huge change for me! Is it a problem that I am waking up before it is light out? Sometimes I am up at 530 and feel like I can't go back to sleep. I've been testing my blood glucose in the morning and it's been below 88 mg/dL. Can this be a sign that I can maybe consider dropping the BAB from my leptin sensitivity protocol? When doing paleo-ketogenic before the leptin reset and before eating seafood (I ate almost no seafood before reading Jack's work) I was getting readings from mid 90's to low 100's.
I will also become a member soon so I can watch the webinars, mainly the EMF one. And I have paid for blood panels to be done. I don't have time for an appointment until late next week however. I will post results in my journal when I get them. Yewwei, thanks again for taking the time!

### Danny — 2015-02-06T19:24:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leaky-gut-my-positivity-is-coming-back.12814/post-154912>

> [yewwei.tan said:](/goto/post?id=154748)
>
> Welcome , I had basically the same disease profile (until I moved), and I'll be following along.
>
> Some random notes:
>
> \----
>
> Not being breastfed along with having lots of processed food definitely isn't a good setup. But you're doing things right, so let's see where you need to do
>
> \----
>
> Living in a Rural Area usually is associated with lower nn-EMF levels. Sometimes it can be opposite. Examples:
>
> \- More powerful cell towers to cover a wider area
> \- More high-voltage power lines
> \- Transmitting devices like cell phones emitting more power due to decreased signal.
>
> Though I would say, that if you scout out your area and find very few towers or powerlines, and you are minding of your cell phone use, then things should be better than in an urban area.
>
> \----
>
> Obviously I'd tell you turn off the WiFi completely all the time
>
> \----
>
> CT is going to be tough given low body fat and poor skin quality.
>
> When dumped into the cold, the body either has to increase uncoupling by liberating body fat as free heat (you don't have lots of fat), or to shiver. Shivering can make CT really difficult, especially when you're not healthy
>
> Where the skin is relevant is that skin cells with good redox potential (high charge over their membrane) likely "keeps internal body heat in" while still allowing for surface cooling (skin has many layers too).
>
> You should see cold tolerance improve as skin quality improves, and skin quality is directly tied to the quality of the GALT tissues in your gut.
>
> Personally, I think you're doing many things right. In your case, I would work the CT protocol slightly differently. I would not bother with full-body immersion or cold showers, because it's really going to be difficult, and possibly take away from your motivation to do other things that are more helpful for your specific disease.
>
> (If you were say someone who had some major neurological issue, immersion CT would have a much bigger bang-for-its-buck)
>
> I would:
>
> -   Only do cold face dunks
> -   Drink lots of ice water (direct CT to the gut )
> -   Simply try to wear less clothing in the cold
>
> You can start water-immersion CT when you start to see your skin quality improve.
>
> \----
>
> You should consider intermittent fasting for your gut. (this is relevant to the H2 threads that Dr Kruse mentioned above)
>
> I'm going to assume that you're truly Leptin Resistant, and judging by being underweight, I think that's decent assumption -- [http://jackkruse.com/the-leptin-rx-faqs/](http://jackkruse.com/the-leptin-rx-faqs/) .
>
> You're not that underweight though, and don't display neurological or psychological issues related to eating, so I'm expecting a quick recovery (your slight depression is a side effect of a bad gut not producing enough neutrotransmitters, and not a fundamental brain issue per se)
>
> That makes implementing intermittent fasting a little more difficult, since it would mean skipping dinner. You can try that, basically eating only the Leptin Reset breakfast, and then doing lunch 5-6 hours after breakfast.
>
> As you recover, you can and should start to skip breakfast, and start shifting to a lunch and dinner eating pattern. This is because protein synthesis is higher during the dinner time window, and you want to take advantage of putting some protein there.
>
> \----
>
> Need to know your exposure to artificial light. While such visible light is in the native EMF wavelength bands, the intensity that you can shine a light bulb into your nights at night make this a non-native intensity.
>
> Any strong light exposure at night? And if so, what is the current strategy to mitigate that?
>
> \-----
>
> Last thing, how are your sleep and wake times?
>
> \====
>
> That's it for now. Good luck
>
> ....

By the way, in what ways did your move help? Did you move from a higher nnEMF exposure to a lower?

\----

> [Danny said:](/goto/post?id=154911)
>
> First of all, thank you so much for taking the time to write out this info Yewwei! I will heed your advice about the CT. I am definitely noticing higher tolerance to cold temps. I am in Florida, however, so the cold is relatively mild compared to most on the forum.

Good!

> I believe you are right about intermittent fasting. It is an absolute must for me at this point. I've tried it a little before, but I believe I will be smarter in how I go about it now. I will try your suggestion of skipping dinner while doing the leptin Rx. I'm a little leery, b/c, as you mentioned, 4 to 6 PM is the best time for protein synthesis (probably closer to 4 in the winter and 6 in the summer).

You're going to get the benefits of that elevated protein synthesis regardless of whether you eat dinner. Of course, having some food (specifically protein) at this time is even better

> My exposure to artificial light is very minimal at night. I really am trying to be regimented w/ the basics. I turn down lights immediately after sunset and only candle light an hour later. I've only needed to have lights on a few times later than 1 hr after sundown and I've worn blue blockers then.

Good!

> My sleep is much improved after starting the leptin Rx. I fall asleep b/t 830 to 10 and most nights it's b/t 9 and 930. I wake up b/t 530 and 630. I have only been slightly tired when waking once or twice. Other than that I'm now ready to go in the morning and I don't fade in the afternoon. This is a huge change for me! Is it a problem that I am waking up before it is light out? Sometimes I am up at 530 and feel like I can't go back to sleep.

Waking early shouldn't be too much a problem as Inger mentioned. So long as you're waking up was good energy levels, then I don't see it as an issue.

> I've been testing my blood glucose in the morning and it's been below 88 mg/dL. Can this be a sign that I can maybe consider dropping the BAB from my leptin sensitivity protocol? When doing paleo-ketogenic before the leptin reset and before eating seafood (I ate almost no seafood before reading Jack's work) I was getting readings from mid 90's to low 100's.

Yeah, it's normal for slightly elevated blood glucose and physiological insulin resistance when going ketogenic. This is a GOOD sign, and if your body can't do this, you will be energy depleted. This was discussed in deep detail in the 'Low carb / Insulin Resistance' thread -- [http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/low-carb-insulin-resistance.8934/](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/low-carb-insulin-resistance.8934/)

The fact that you're getting controlled glucose levels is a good sign that you are decently healthy. Whether or not you keep the BAB in your protocol is up to you at this point. It's definitely not a sign that you should drop the BAB, but you likely don't need the food stimulus in the morning.

> I will also become a member soon so I can watch the webinars, mainly the EMF one. And I have paid for blood panels to be done. I don't have time for an appointment until late next week however. I will post results in my journal when I get them. Yewwei, thanks again for taking the time!

Awaiting updates

For years I've struggled w/ digestive issues. I cut out gluten and improved. Then cut out grains and improved more. Then started eating PALEO raw and improved some more. Went ketogenic and improved more (it was extremely hard for me to achieve any level of blood ketones until I did the Leptin Rx). Found Jack and started the leptin Rx and improved some more. Cut out dairy and eggs per Jack's blog advice and improved some more. I was still getting some stomachaches and loose bm once or twice a week. Even though I felt as though I was improving, I felt as though my progress was too slow. Then on Feb 11th I turned off the wi-fi (it might be on for 15 to 20 mins on some days just for my wife and guests, but then immediately turned off) and I haven't had one hint of a stomachache since. I even fell out of ketosis a week ago and while feeling tired and having some nervous system issues I still had no stomach problems. Thanks to Jack's message and your reminder to shut off the damn wi-fi I finally feel like I have made a real change and turned a corner. I plugged a leak!

### Danny — 2015-08-31T02:42:23+0300

I have been extremely busy w/ a big move recently. A friend and I have been doing most of a 400 sq ft renovation (replacing exterior walls and windows and building interior walls) alone. Now it's time to call in an electrician and other professionals to help finish up the job. Things have finally slowed down to a manageable pace and I'm ready to get back in on the webinars and catch up a bit. I'm only up to Ubi 17, which I will finish tonight. I will read 18 tomorrow in the morning sun.
We moved from just north of Tampa to about 10 miles west of Flagler beach. We went from 180ft above sea level to 12ft, lowered nnEMF exposure from surrounding towers, 2 story home to 1 story. We're living on the border of a conservation area w/ a lot of pine trees and about 25 minutes from the beach. All I've been doing is working and going to the beach. On some days I've been taking my son to the beach before school or after. This is helping him tremendously. I've still been reading; CT, tensegrity, ubi, "life on the edge". It finally dawned on me that nnEMF in the form of tyndall scattering of sunlight through windows, glasses, etc. is a big deal.
I have adjusted my diet to include more carbs… been doing this since June. I've been adding in some apple cider vinegar. I will experiment w/ nicotine spray shortly. The vinegar definitely seems to work well as far as energy production is concerned. I seem to be able to handle 100 to 150 g of carbs a day. I've had a few times where I felt my energy take a hit. On those days my carb consumption might have been closer to 200 to 250 g of carbs.
W/ poor mitonuclear functioning you should avoid constant ketosis or doing LCHF. Should someone w/ bad mitochondria still eat a considerable amount of fat in the summertime along w/ seasonally appropriate carbs?
Soon I will be setting up a CT tank in the back yard. I am definitely excited for this.
I am still strictly following natural light cycles, nude sunbathing in the morning (this makes me feel so amazing), sleeping on the floor, eating more and more seafood everyday, going to the beach 3 times a week, a lot of grounding, my CT face dunks have not been very consistent, but I will get right back to it. I am learning all the time that nnEMF is everywhere. I never thought about tyndall scattering before. I am now trying to mitigate this as much as possible while inside or driving. Also covering up much more in artificially lit environments. Excited to keep learning and keep improving.

### Danny — 2015-09-25T22:06:14+0300

Magnetico update plus a few other notes:

Started sleeping on a 5 G on 9/1.

I stopped sleeping on the Magnetico from 9/10 - 9/17, b/c I felt dehydrated from a stomach bug (whole family + neighbors sick). I had no appetite for meat including seafood, so w/o DHA and being dehydrated I wanted to be cautious about sleeping on the Magnetico.

Since being back on my 5G : Dream recall still pretty good. If I do wake up w/ a sore throat now it is negligible. I still get drainage after waking that seems to be stuck. It takes a little time for it to come out.

I've been super tired (weak legs) lately, but I think this is more to do w/ me cutting way back on carbs in the past few days. I over did the carbs this summer. Obviously my mitochondria are still functioning poorly. W/ mitochondria proton phase shifted and slacking on DHA b/c of lack of appetite for meat while sick I am having a hard time w/ cutting back on my carbs. Should have done low carb plus nicotine for superoxide pulses and left the excess carbs alone this summer.

Corrective Actions: Seafood at every meal (probably should have been doing this from day 1) and more water. Continue w/ sun, beach, grounding, spot CT and CT face dunks, living by natural light cycles routine. As soon as my mitochondria phase shift back (don't know if I'm saying that right) will move forward w/ CT baths. Continue w/ ACV + water away from meals.
